note: 80 acres, 100+ sites (primitive to full hookup), big-rig friendly pull-throughs, 5-acre lake w/ boat rentals. Open May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp Toodik
note: 93 acres, ~223 sites, RVs to 45 ft. Main Hill full-hookup + River Edge water/electric RV sites fronting the Lake Fork (Mohican) branch. Canoe livery, pool, mini-golf. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Oak Crest Campground
note: ~136 sites (34 full hookup, 102 elec/water), 50 pull-throughs, RVs to ~35 ft, heated pool, 7-acre stocked lake. Open Apr 15-Oct 31. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Timberwoods Campground
note: ~158 sites, full hookup 30/50-amp, RVs to 60 ft, pull-throughs, pool + fishing pond. Seasonal Apr-Oct, takes short-term. New owners; mixed mgmt reviews. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Hickory Grove Lake
note: ~38 acres, full-hookup standard & premium RV sites, big-rig pull-throughs, 9-acre stocked lake, beach, pool, dog park. Borders Marion Tallgrass Trail. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: A1-Twin Valley Campground
note: 107 gravel sites, full/partial hookups, big-rig friendly to 40 ft (est. 1980); pool, stocked fishing pond. Public/transient. Facebook-only web presence. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Kokosing Valley Camp & Canoe
note: ~150 sites, water/electric/sewer, big-rig friendly to 50 ft, canoe livery on the State Scenic Kokosing River, pool. Riverfront sites reserved for full-season campers (not transient). Season May-Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Shady Trails Family Campground
note: ~130 sites, 30/50-amp full hookups, pull-throughs to ~85 ft, near Rocky Fork Lake. Mostly seasonal but accepts transient nightly. No standalone website (Good Sam/Campendium). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Atwood Lake Country Campground
note: Full-hookup RV sites (20/30/50-amp), large rigs, pool, catch-and-release fishing pond; inland near (not on) Atwood Lake. Firefly online reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Shadow Lake RV Park & Campground
note: 96 acres w/ lake + 3 ponds, ~100 full-hookup big-rig sites, pool, mini-golf, pickleball; some sites have lake views. Open year-round; transient + long-term. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Campers Grove RV Park
note: ~30 sites, pet-friendly, tenting, buddy sites, playground; off I-70 near Dillon Lake & Hopewell Culture NHP. Season Apr-Oct, $21/night, reservations by phone. No official website (directory listings). RV Life 4*. --Claude  Phone-only booking (740-453-1454); no official website. ~30 W/E RV sites + dump station, ~$19-21/night, seasonal Apr-Oct. --Claude

name: Hidden Hills Campground
note: ~62 sites, full hookups 20/30/50-amp, pull-throughs, RVs to 60 ft, dump station, playground. Seasonal mid-Apr to Oct; some full-time residents but transient-bookable. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Nelson Ledges Quarry Park
note: 250-acre quarry swim/scuba/festival park w/ a designated ~60-site RV camping area + dump station, limited FCFS electric, no water hookups. Non-festival camping is walk-up/first-come. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Indian Trail Campground
note: 169 sites (106 full hookup, 60 water/electric, 40 primitive), 30/50-amp, pool + 8-acre lake; max RV ~48 ft. Open late Apr-early Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Clinton Lake Camping
note: ~200 sites (160 water/electric 30-amp, 40 tent, 10 pull-throughs), pool/clubhouse, fishing + swim lakes. Nightly + seasonal. Open May-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: River Bend Family Campground
note: 240 full-hookup sites, 30/50-amp, pool + pond, on the Olentangy/Whetstone River (riverfront RV sites unconfirmed). Nightly + seasonal. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Sun Valley Family Campground
note: 242 water/electric sites, pool + fishing pond (since 1987), ~9 mi E of Lima. Open Apr 15-Oct 31. Primary web presence Facebook/ohiocamper. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: RiverView Kampground
note: 53 full-hookup sites + marina/boat ramp, pool, on the Muskingum River; riverfront/marina sites are seasonal-only (daily campers get inland sites). Facebook web presence. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Schaun Acres Campground
note: 130 gravel-pad sites, pool + 2 fishing ponds; mostly seasonal but offers nightly electric ($35) + primitive. Open May-Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Arrow Point Campground
note: 34 acres on the Mohican River, RV-only, big-rig friendly (smallest site 25x50 ft). On-river sites are seasonal-only; daily/transient sites are off-river inland. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: River Trail Campgrounds II
note: 56 acres on the St. Marys River; ~248-260 full-hookup sites, 30/50-amp, big-rig friendly, pool + 2-acre pond + swim beach. Nightly ~$37-40 + seasonal. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Miller's Campground & Gun Club
note: Small wooded campground + gun club & stocked fishing pond in Waynesville; 30/50-amp RV lots, tent sites, clubhouse. Open to public, reserve by phone. No standalone website. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Wolford's Landing Campground
note: 83 concrete-pad sites, full & water/electric hookups, vehicles to 60 ft, on-site cafe, on the Ohio River; some RV sites have river frontage (reviewer-confirmed). Facebook/directory web presence. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Philabaun's Hidden Cove Resort
note: On Berlin Lake/Reservoir; full hookups, primitive to water/electric sites, boat-docking cove. Daily RV rate ~$40, open to public. Sites close together per reviews. No standalone website. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Hillside Park of Clinton
note: 65-acre family park between Akron & Canton; 30/50-amp water/electric, vehicles to 80 ft, laundry, golf-cart rentals; overnight/weekend/seasonal. Near Cuyahoga Valley NP & Towpath Trail. No standalone website. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Franklin Furnace Inn & RV Park
note: 16 full/partial-hookup big-rig sites, putt-putt, fishing/swimming lake, dump station; budget-friendly from $15/night. No standalone website. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Blue Water Campground
note: 209 gravel-pad sites on 84 acres, 30/50-amp water/electric (no sewer; pump-out + dump station), pool, fishing pond/quarry. Online booking via CampLife; reserve seasonal or nightly. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Timashamie Family Campgrounds
note: Full hookups (20/30/50-amp), pull-throughs, pool, 5-acre spring-fed fishing lake, creek; overnight from ~$40. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Trackside Camping
note: 30 sites, 30-amp + municipal water, limestone pads, WiFi; reservations required, $35/night. Adjacent to Atomic Speedway; 15 min to Lake White & Pike Lake. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Sunset Springs Campground
note: 250 sites (50-amp full & 30-amp water/electric) since 1969, camp store, swimming pond, floating water park, zip line; overnight/weekend/seasonal. Near Mohican SP. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Buffalo Hills Resort
note: ~400 big-rig sewer/water/electric sites on 175 acres, large pool, two stocked ponds, store, clubhouse; reopened under new management 2024 (confirm open before a trip). ~1-2 mi from Seneca Lake. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Maplewood Lake Christian Campground
note: 25 full-hookup sites (50-amp), pull-throughs, RVs to 40 ft, two small fishing lakes; group/retreat facilities but open to all for nightly camping (~$30). Near the Ohio River. No standalone website. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Elkhart County Fairgrounds
note: Elkhart County 4-H Fair Association. Public RV camping Mar 1-Nov 30, nightly reservations by phone; back-in 30/50A W/E/S, no tents/pull-thrus. Blackout during fair/rallies. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lynnville Park Campground
note: Town of Lynnville park. 24 full-hookup + 16 W/E (50A) + ~22 primitive sites on strip-mine reclamation lakes; some sites have a lake view. Reserve online, honor-system late arrivals. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Johnson County Fairgrounds
note: Johnson County Agricultural Association. Transient RV camping outside fair week (~Apr-Oct), online + phone reservations, $30/night W/E/S; sites tight, pick a perimeter site for a 23-ft rig. Big rigs use Worthsville Rd exit. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Irwin Park
note: Town of Edinburgh park on the Big Blue River. 12 W/E RV sites, $20/night, reservations accepted, Apr 1-Oct 31. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Johnson County Park
note: Johnson County Parks & Recreation (near Nineveh). ~58 W/E (30/50A) + 11 full-hookup sites, level gravel pads, dump station; season Apr-Oct, reserve by phone/office. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Riverview Campground (Madison)
note: City of Madison campground on the Ohio River. 33 W/E sites (18 directly riverfront, rest river-view), no tents, online reservations via Firefly. RV Life 4*/$$$. --Claude

name: Pike Lake Campground
note: City of Warsaw Parks & Rec, on Pike Lake. 44 full-hookup sites, boat docks, no tents; most sites have a lake view. Season Apr 15-Oct 1, reserve by phone. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hawthorn Park
note: Vigo County Parks & Rec, on Burns Lake. ~70 electric sites (some water), pull-throughs, dump station; some sites have direct lake views. Season May 1-Oct 15, online reservations. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: France Park
note: Cass County park near Logansport. ~133 modern electric sites (water at most) + ~67 primitive; quarry/Wabash River on-water sites are primitive-only. Modern sites reserve by phone Mon-Thu, primitive first-come. RV Life 3.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Fowler Park
note: Vigo County Parks & Rec, on Fowler Lake south of Terre Haute. ~53 electric + ~15 primitive; some electric sites are directly on the lake (short - choose carefully for 23-ft). Reservable online (Campspot). Season May 1-Oct 15. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Muscatatuck Park
note: Jennings County Parks & Rec. 50 sites (8 full-hookup pull-thru, 26 W/E, 15 primitive); online reservations + walk-ins. Water off ~Nov-Apr. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Jefferson Township Community Park
note: Jefferson Township (Allen County), New Haven. 6 gravel sites, W/E + 50A, reservations, Apr-Oct, 30-day max. Sulfur well water; railroad close. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Wabash City RV Park
note: City of Wabash, by the Wabash River 2 blocks from downtown. ~18-24 W/E sites ($15-16/night); check in at city Water Treatment Plant by 4PM for hookup keys, first-come + reservations. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Tipton County Fairgrounds
note: Tipton County fairgrounds. ~100 electric sites (20/30/50A), dump station, new bathhouse; year-round nightly booking via Campspot. RV Life 3*/$$. --Claude

name: Delaware County Fairgrounds
note: Delaware County fairgrounds, Muncie. Mostly 50A sites, walkable to downtown; books most of year via Campspot (primitive in winter, water off Dec-Apr). Few truly level spots for big rigs. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: St. Joseph County Fairgrounds
note: St. Joseph County 4-H Fair Association, South Bend. 75+ sites with E/W/S, modern restrooms/showers; seasonal Apr-Nov when no events, book via RoverPass. RV Life 3*/$$$. --Claude

name: Sunset Park (Linton)
note: City of Linton park beside Sunset Lake. Gravel RV sites W/E, shower house, laundry, boat ramp/docks, gated to 11PM. First-come-first-served. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Westwood Park
note: Big Blue River Conservancy District (Henry County), on 180-acre Westwood Lake. Point Campground: 38 W/E sites (RVs to ~40 ft), dump station, some sites front the lake. First-come-first-served, ~late Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Rivertown Campground (Vevay)
note: Town of Vevay, Ogle Park on the Ohio River. 44 gravel sites incl. 12 full-hookup, boat launch, showers, dump, dog park; year-round, Campspot reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Delaney Creek Park
note: Washington County park near Scottsburg, on an 88-acre lake. Paved electric/water sites that overlook (and slope toward) the lake; reservable. Steep narrow ~8% entrance - passable, bring leveling blocks. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Dubois County Park
note: Dubois County park, 5 mi S of Jasper. 34 concrete-pad sites, 20A electric + water, dump station, showers; first-come-first-served, 14-day max. Open Apr 1-Nov 1. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Prairie Creek Park (Terre Haute)
note: Vigo County Parks & Rec, Terre Haute. ~42 modern electric sites (mostly 30A, some 50A), showers, dump station; open year-round, reserve online. Adjoins a 13-acre lake + pond (sites in a maple grove, not waterfront). RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Lake Waveland Park
note: Little Raccoon Creek Conservancy District (Montgomery County), on a 360-acre lake. Modern W/E sites reservable by phone, primitive first-come; big rigs reported (40-44 ft) but many pop-up-sized sites - request a larger site. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Sullivan County Park & Lake
note: Sullivan County Park & Lake Board, on 461-acre Sullivan Lake. RV slip sites (W/E, some sewer) on the water + pull-throughs; reserve via Campspot. ~Mar-Oct (some year-round). Large park, transient sites bookable. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: White River Campground (Cicero)
note: Hamilton County Parks & Rec, on the White River. 106 sites, paved roads, level pads to 50 ft; some sites back directly to the river with canoe/kayak launch. Nightly bookable. County-managed (no standalone website). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Johnny Appleseed Campground
note: City of Fort Wayne, Johnny Appleseed Park by the St. Joseph River. ~45-49 W/E sites (RVs to 50 ft), nightly bookable May-Oct. Closed ~Sept 13-24 for the Johnny Appleseed Festival. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Wabash & Erie Canal Park
note: Non-profit Wabash & Erie Canal Association, Delphi. Small: 7 RV sites (4 full-hookup) behind the Interpretive Center, sites fit large rigs; winter has 3 self-contained-only sites. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Scales Lake Park
note: Warrick County Parks, on 66-acre Scales Lake near Boonville. Class A/B/C drive-in sites, swim beach. (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is county-run.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: West Boggs Park
note: Daviess-Martin County interlocal park on 622-acre West Boggs Lake. 154 sites, full bathhouse, beach, marina. (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is county-run.) RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Buffalo Trace Park
note: Harrison County Parks & Rec, on a 30-acre lake near Palmyra. Modern ($22) + primitive ($13) drive-in RV sites, beach. (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is county-run.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ouabache Trails Park
note: Knox County Parks, 254-acre park on the Wabash River near Vincennes. 35 RV sites (20/30/50A W/E). (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is county-run; distinct from Ouabache State Park.) RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Bixler Lake Campground
note: City of Kendallville Parks & Rec, Bixler Lake Park (117-acre lake). 103 electric sites, RVs to 60 ft. (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is city-run.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Rockville Lake Park
note: Parke County park, 400 acres around a 102-acre lake. 160 sites with full / water-electric hookups, accommodates large RVs; seasonal ~late Mar-Oct. (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is county-run.) RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: New Lake Campground (Oakland City)
note: Oakland City municipal campground on a 69-acre lake. 32 RV sites (W/E). (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is city-run; Facebook 'New Lake Campground'.) RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Ross Camp
note: Tippecanoe County Parks & Rec, near West Lafayette. 15 electric sites (RVs to 50 ft), modern shower house, catch-and-release pond; seasonal, reserve by phone. (RV Life mis-tagged commercial; it is county-run.) RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Deer Ridge Camping Resort
note: Private. 60 full-hookup sites off I-70 Exit 153 (Richmond), big-rig 50A pull-thrus, AAA/Good Sam listed; some seasonal but takes nightly transient. Season May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Cornerstone Campground
note: Private Christian retreat/campground near I-70 (New Castle). 110 sites incl. big-rig pull-throughs to 50 ft, full/W-E hookups, on-site fishing lake (amenity); nightly bookable via RoverPass. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Miles Landing Campground
note: Private veteran-owned. 59 full-hookup sites incl. pull-throughs on reclaimed mine land in Bluegrass FWA; ponds nearby (waterfront unconfirmed). Nightly via Campspot. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (9.2/10). --Claude

name: Unhitched Rising Sun RV Park
note: Private (fka Little Farm on the River / Beyonder), on the Ohio River. All full-hookup, big-rig friendly, year-round; lower-section sites back directly to the river. Distinct from the nearby casino RV park. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Sugar Creek Campground & Canoe
note: Private family-owned, 48 acres near Turkey Run/Shades with half a mile of Sugar Creek frontage; creekside full-hookup RV sites to 65 ft. Nightly bookable. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (9.2/10). --Claude

name: Grandpa's Farm Campground
note: Private family-owned (Christian) near Richmond; the Middle Fork Whitewater River flows through, riverside full-hookup pull-thrus to 70 ft. Season Apr 15-Nov 1, limited winter. RV Life 4*/$$ (8.4/10). --Claude

name: Maple Ridge Campground
note: Private family-owned (fka Beaver Ridge), 38 wooded acres near Potato Creek SP. Pull-thru 50A big-rig sites; nightly via RoverPass. RV Life 4*/$$ (8.4/10). --Claude

name: All My Family & Friends Campground
note: Private, <1 mi off I-70 (Straughn). All pull-through full-hookup sites 90x17 ft, online nightly booking via Campspot; mostly seasonal (140 seasonal / 20 transient) but takes nightly. 10-acre lake (amenity). RV Life 4*/$$ (8.4/10). --Claude

name: Charlarose Lake & Campground
note: Private family RV campground wrapping its own Lake Charlarose; 117 W/E sites + dump, big-rig pull-throughs, some sites lakefront/lakeview. Nightly bookable. Seasonal Apr 1-Nov 1. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Old Mill Run Park
note: Private family park on Sugar Creek (Thorntown). ~41 transient full-hookup sites (plus 280 seasonal), 140 gravel pull-thrus; two stocked ponds. Nightly via RoverPass. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Camp Timber Lake
note: Private family campground (since 2003), 62 acres with a 15-acre stocked lake/beach/boat ramp (Huntington). ~60-65 sites incl. full hookups + pull-thrus (to 70 ft), 20/30/50A; year-round. Official site has a TLS cert mismatch but is live. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Sycamore Springs Park
note: 64 FHU and W/E sites; some on a small pond, some on the Little Blue River. Cheap ($25-35). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --AWH

name: Walnut Ridge RV Park
note: Private, year-round (New Castle). 160 concrete/stone sites incl. big-rig pull-throughs (to 38 ft), pool + mini-golf; takes overnight transient + seasonal. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Free Spirit Family Camping
note: Private, 40 gravel-pad sites with full/partial/primitive hookups (Bedford), ~15 min from Lake Monroe; open year-round, nightly bookable. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Amishville USA Campground
note: Private agritourism campground on the Wabash River (Geneva). Full/partial/primitive hookups, RVs to 50 ft; 2-night-min reservations, season Apr 1-Oct 31. Some sites may have a river view. No standalone website (Good Sam/RoverPass listings). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: White Oaks on the Lake
note: Private RV resort on Lake Freeman (Monticello), 1,500 ft of frontage; 100+ sites, Lower Village RV sites are lakeside with beach/piers. Daily/weekly/seasonal public bookings. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Covered Bridge Campground
note: Private, closest campground to downtown Rockville. Full hookups (W/E/S, 50A), grassy/flat sites, RVs to 40 ft; popular for the Covered Bridge Festival. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Wildwood Outdoor Escape
note: Private (fka Wildwood Acres), 120 primitive/full-hookup sites near Hartford City; small fishing ponds (amenity). Nightly/weekly via Campspot, seasonal mid-Apr to mid-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: AOK Campground
note: Private, year-round near Prophetstown SP (Lafayette). 30/50A, 65 pull-thrus, RVs to 60 ft, gated; wooded. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Happy Camper Campground
note: Private, tiny: 5 full-hookup sites + dry cabin and a 1.5-acre pond (Brazil). Class A over 40 ft may drag at the driveway entry; a 23-ft rig is fine. RV Life 5*/$$. The official campground domain is dead/parked (scam); the active Facebook page is used instead. --Claude

name: Peaceful Waters Campground
note: Private near Bloomingdale/Rockville. Electric (30/50A) + primitive sites + cabins, modern shower house, dump station, fishing pond; nightly via Campspot. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Oakridge Campground
note: Private family-owned, 36 gravel-pad sites with full hookups near Rockport (Ohio River area); stocked pond. Nightly transient, open Mar 1-Dec 1. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Riverbend Campground (Noblesville)
note: Private family campground on a half-mile of the White River (Noblesville); full hookups, 50A, big-rig pull-thrus, on-site boat launch, some RV sites on the river. Nightly + seasonal, open Apr-Dec. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Hillbilly Acres Campground
note: Private, on the edge of Greene-Sullivan State Forest (Linton). Full hookups with a dedicated overnight transient area + own bathhouse (24 overnight sites) plus seasonals; two catch-and-release lakes. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Rocky Falls RV Park & Campground
note: Private, year-round W of Evansville off Hwy 62. 70+ full-hookup sites (30/50A), big rigs to 40-50 ft; reservations not normally required (first-come friendly, call ahead). RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Robinson's Whitewater River Campground
note: Private family-owned, 40 acres along the Whitewater River (Connersville); daily/weekly + seasonal. Largest sites are tight (longest documented rig 24 ft) - a 23-ft rig fits but confirm site length at booking. Facebook-only web presence. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Gordon's Camping
note: Private family campground (Wolcottville), RVs to 45 ft, full hookups, pool; nightly + seasonal. Despite the name it does NOT front Big Long Lake (boat launch is offsite). Some access-road roughness noted. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Rising Sun Campground
note: Private family campground on the Tippecanoe River (Monterey). 90+ W/E RV sites (nightly $40-48, RVs to 50 ft) + primitive; canoe/kayak/tubing. Tent/primitive sites are on the riverbank; RV sites set back (river view at best). RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: JT's Lakeside Campground
note: Private, on Little Barbee Lake (Barbee Chain near Warsaw); all sites are on the channel or lakefront. 30A + water, RVs to 50 ft, pull-thrus, boat/pontoon rental. Nightly from $40, open Apr 15-Oct 15. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Eagles Nest Camping
note: Private, small: 5 full-hookup RV sites + 3 cabins, stocked pond, ~2 mi S of Linton. Reservable by phone; active Facebook. Official website is a dead placeholder - booking by phone. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Long Lake Resort
note: Private family RV campground (since 1926, 100th year) adjacent a recreational lake near North Manchester; full hookups, public nightly $38 + seasonal/monthly (not a membership park). Open ~May 15-Sept 1. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Turkey Run Canoe & Camping
note: Private, ~125 gravel-pad sites 2 mi W of Turkey Run SP; 30/50A full hookups, pull-throughs, dump, store; canoe livery on Sugar Creek (sites are wooded/interior, not creekside). Reservable via Campspot from ~$27. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Harper Lake Campground
note: Private, on Harper Lake near Kimmell (RV Life lists it as '10 Lakes Campground'). 120+ sites on ~30 acres with over 1/2 mile of lakefront; waterfront, wooded & open W/E sites. Daily/weekly/seasonal, open May-Sep. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Eel Weir State Park
note: OPRHP. ~38 rustic sites (no hookups), drive-in loop on the Oswegatchie River (several sites on/along the river). Reservable via ReserveAmerica, ~Memorial Day-Labor Day. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Sacandaga Campground
note: DEC (Adirondack Forest Preserve) on the Sacandaga River near Wells. 143 drive-in sites, no hookups, dump station, boat launch; some riverbank sites. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Watkins Glen State Park
note: OPRHP. ~305 sites in 6 loops on the plateau above the gorge; Mohawk & Cayuga loops have electric. Max RV varies by loop (some 20 ft, others 30-40 ft). ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Allegany State Park - Quaker Area (Cain Hollow)
note: OPRHP, Cain Hollow Campground. 158 sites (loops A-D), 96 RV sites 20-63 ft, many electric, showers, dump station. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Robert H. Treman State Park
note: OPRHP, lower-park campground near Enfield Creek (Ithaca). ~70 sites, ~11 electric. ReserveAmerica, ~May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Keuka Lake State Park
note: OPRHP above the W branch of Keuka Lake. 150 sites/3 loops, 53 electric, max RV ~40 ft, dump; no lakefront sites but some loop lake views. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Taughannock Falls State Park
note: OPRHP near Cayuga Lake (camp area inland across Rte 89; only cabins are lakeside). 76 sites (16 electric), max RV ~30 ft, dump, 2-night min. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Fillmore Glen State Park
note: OPRHP near Moravia. 60 sites, 14 electric (20/30/50A), dump, 2-night min. ReserveAmerica, ~May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Allegany State Park - Red House Area
note: OPRHP, Red House Tent & Trailer Campground (5 loops). ~121 sites, 104 electric (30/50A), 3 pull-through, max RV ~40 ft, dump. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Sugar Hill Recreation Area
note: DEC Sugar Hill State Forest camping area (free, first-come-first-served, no reservations, no hookups). ~22 self-issue-permit sites that fit 28-31 ft rigs; has horse facilities but is general camping. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Schodack Island State Park
note: OPRHP on Schodack Island (Hudson River). 66 sites all with electric, pull-throughs to 50 ft; no waterfront sites (river via trails). ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Thompson's Lake Campground (Thacher State Park)
note: OPRHP. 140 wooded sites, no electric, max RV ~40 ft; some lakeside sites with foot trails to Thompson's Lake. Beach, boat rentals. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Taconic State Park - Copake Falls Area
note: OPRHP. ~106 sites incl. ~36 trailer sites (max ~26-ft trailer, no hookups), tent platforms, cabins, dump. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Woodland Valley Campground
note: DEC (Catskill Forest Preserve), Phoenicia. 69 tent/trailer sites, RVs up to 30 ft, no hookups. Slide Mtn trailhead. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Bear Spring Mountain Campground
note: DEC (Catskill Forest Preserve), Launt Pond near Walton. 41 tent/trailer sites, no hookups; offers horse tie stalls but is a general campground. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Darien Lakes State Park
note: OPRHP. 155 sites, 141 electric (30A), 13 primitive, max RV ~40 ft (Loop B for larger rigs); no waterfront sites. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Long Point State Park - Thousand Islands
note: OPRHP, peninsula in Chaumont Bay (Lake Ontario). 80 sites, select 30A electric, RVs 20-40 ft; most sites on/near the water. Boat launch. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Moreau Lake State Park
note: OPRHP. 145 sites/5 loops along Moreau Lake, essentially no electric, max RV ~40 ft (tight loop roads). Beach. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Frontier Town Campground
note: DEC (Adirondacks, North Hudson; opened 2020). 91 sites incl. 13 RV/trailer w/electric (max 40 ft), tent, group, 19 equestrian (general campground). Dump. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Higley Flow State Park
note: OPRHP (Adirondack foothills) on Higley Flow (impounded Raquette River). 128 sites/6 loops; loops A/B & part of C water/electric; max RV ~40 ft (narrow roads). Beach, boat launch. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Limekiln Lake Campground
note: DEC (Adirondacks, Inlet). 271 sites, no hookups; confirmed lakeside trailer sites up to 40 ft. Guarded beach, boat launch, canoe rentals. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Caroga Lake Campground
note: DEC (Adirondacks), East Caroga Lake. 105-151 sites/loops A-F, no electric, max RV ~40 ft (tight spacing, best for smaller rigs). Beach, boat launch. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Stony Brook State Park
note: OPRHP. ~88 gravel back-in sites/3 loops at the south entrance, no hookups, dump, max RV ~40 ft. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Max V. Shaul State Park
note: OPRHP, Fultonham. 30 wooded sites, no hookups, water spigots, showers, max RV ~34 ft. ReserveAmerica (same-day allowed). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Hamlin Beach State Park
note: OPRHP on Lake Ontario. 264 electric (30A) sites/loops A-F, water/showers/laundry/dump, max RV ~40 ft; loops set back inland from the bluff/beach. ReserveAmerica, mid-May-early Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Cayuga Lake State Park
note: OPRHP at the N end of Cayuga Lake. ~283 sites (36 electric 30A East Camp + non-electric West Camp), max RV ~40 ft, dump; some East Camp sites have a lake view. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Whetstone Gulf State Park
note: OPRHP, Lowville. 56 wooded sites, some electric (15/30/50A), showers/laundry/dump, max RV ~30-40 ft. ReserveAmerica, mid-May-mid-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Paradox Lake Campground
note: DEC (Adirondack), Dark Bay of Paradox Lake. 58 sites/3 loops, no hookups, showers/dump/boat launch; ~5 waterfront sites, large rigs fit. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Mills Norrie State Park
note: OPRHP on the bluffs above the Hudson, Staatsburg. 44 tent/trailer sites (6 electric, 3 platform) + cabins; river glimpses from some sites, marina below. ReserveAmerica, May-Oct. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Beaver Pond Campground (Harriman State Park)
note: OPRHP (Palisades Interstate). 7 drive-in RV sites (no hookups, max ~30 ft) plus 73 tent + 55 platform sites near Lake Welch; mostly a tent campground. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Little Sand Point Campground
note: DEC (Adirondack), Piseco Lake (one of the Piseco Lake Campground loops). 78 wooded sites (~56 RV-capable), no hookups, showers/dump/boat launch, max RV ~30 ft; many lakefront sites. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Newtown Battlefield State Park
note: OPRHP hilltop park SE of Elmira. ~28 sites: Main Loop (2 electric to 40 ft, rest primitive ~20 ft) + North Loop (10 to ~35 ft); dump, showers. Many sites cap 20 ft - book a longer site. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Cherry Plain State Park
note: OPRHP. 10 trailer sites + lakeside-tent/hike-in sites, no hookups, tight sites (no large-RV spaces); late June-Labor Day. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Kenneth L. Wilson Campground
note: DEC (Catskill Forest Preserve), Mount Tremper. 76 tent/trailer sites, no electric, RVs up to ~40 ft (best <=30). ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Hearthstone Point Campground
note: DEC on Lake George (2 mi N of the village). 243 tent/trailer sites, no electric, RVs to 40 ft; campground largely across Rte 9N from the lake. Sand beach, dump. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Eagle Point Campground
note: DEC on the W shore of Schroon Lake. 72 sites, no hookups, RVs to 30 ft; ~17 lakeside sites; boat launch, beach. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Sharp Bridge Campground
note: DEC on the Schroon River, North Hudson. 40 sites, no hookups, RVs to 25 ft (rustic, best for tents/small rigs). ReserveAmerica. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Beaverkill Campground
note: DEC on the Beaver Kill trout stream (Catskill), Roscoe. 52 sites incl. ~45 pull-throughs, no hookups, RVs to 30 ft; historic covered bridge. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 1.5*. --Claude

name: Brown Tract Pond Campground
note: DEC (Adirondack), Brown's Tract Pond near Raquette Lake. ~90 sites (8 remote paddle/walk-in), no hookups, RVs to 30 ft on the main-road sites; non-motorized pond, boat rentals; gravel access. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 1*. --Claude

name: Heckscher State Park
note: OPRHP near Great South Bay (East Islip, Long Island). 69 tent/trailer sites, no hookups, RVs to 50 ft (site-dependent, 11-ft height limit); campground loop set back inland. ReserveAmerica. RV Life 2.5*. --Claude

name: Devil's Tombstone Campground
note: DEC (Catskill), Hunter. 24 tent/trailer drive-in sites, primitive/no hookups, max ~40 ft but tight mountain access (smaller rigs advised). ReserveAmerica. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Little Pond Campground
note: DEC (Catskill), Andes. 67 tent/trailer + 8 remote sites, max RV 40 ft, 11 drive-up sites directly on the pond. Showers/dump. ReserveAmerica. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Hunts Pond State Forest
note: DEC state forest, New Berlin. 13 drive-in sites on a gravel loop (sites 1-8 allow a trailer, 9-13 tent). FREE permit from DEC Sherburne office, May 1-Sep 30 (no ReserveAmerica); vault toilets, no potable water. RV Life unrated. Free self-issue permit, first-come, first-served (no online reservations). --Claude

name: Poke-O-Moonshine Campground
note: DEC (Adirondack). 25 tent/trailer drive-in sites next to Rte 9 at the mountain trailhead, primitive/no hookups, dump; confirm a 23-ft site at booking. ReserveAmerica. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Lincoln Pond Campground
note: DEC (Adirondack), New Russia. 35 sites (24 drive-in + 10 remote shore + 3 boat island), max RV 30 ft; loop sites 1-15 on the pond. Showers/dump. ReserveAmerica. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Taylor Pond Campground
note: DEC (Adirondack), Au Sable Forks. 30 sites (25 drive-in + 5 boat), max RV 30 ft, primitive (outhouses, water spigots, no showers); some sites on Taylor Pond. ReserveAmerica. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Camp Gateway - Floyd Bennett Field
note: NPS / Gateway National Recreation Area, Brooklyn. ~17 RV non-electric sites (Amelia Earhart loop) + tent sites; no hookups, dump station, generators 8a-10p, ~$30/night, 14-night max. Reserve online, ~Apr-Oct. RVs must avoid the Belt Parkway for access. --Claude  RV Life 2.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Blueberry Patch Campground
note: USFS / Finger Lakes National Forest (Hector Ranger District). 9 semi-primitive sites for tents or small campers under 24 ft. First-come-first-served, no drinking water, vault toilets, $10/night, ~mid-May to end of Nov. --Claude

name: Ontario County Park at Gannett Hill
note: Ontario County park (Bristol Hills). 24 RV sites (30A electric) + 20 tent + 2 cabins; reservable via Firefly. Hilltop with a valley overlook. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Chemung County Park Station
note: Chemung County. 40 sites, 30/50A electric; dedicated Lakeside loop with RV sites on a 100-acre lake. ReserveAmerica, Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Kanakadea Park
note: Steuben County park on Almond Lake (USACE reservoir). 40 electric (30/50A) + 30 non-electric + 2 cabins, dump, boat launch; some lake-view sites. Phone reservations, Apr 15-Dec 1. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Montour Falls Marina & Campground
note: Village of Montour Falls municipal marina/campground. 120 sites, 30/50A, 20 full hookups, 10 pull-thrus, ~35 ft max, dump/showers/store; on the canal to Seneca Lake. May-Oct. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Havana Glen Park
note: Town of Montour municipal park. 14 RV sites (30A electric + water) + 10 tent, dump; Eagle Cliff Falls swimming area. Phone reservations, May-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Myers Park
note: Town of Lansing park on Cayuga Lake. ~19-20 RV sites, 30A electric + water, dump, marina/boat launch/beach; some sites have water views. Phone/online reservations. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Chautauqua County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Chautauqua County Fairgrounds, Dunkirk. 80+ sites; year-round/non-fair-week public camping via separate application (NOT event-only). Reserve via Fair Office. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Greenwood County Park
note: Broome County. 50 electric sites, $30/night, choose-your-site online reservations; spring-fed lake w/ beach (loop set back from the lake). Mid-May-Columbus Day. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Upper Lisle Park
note: Broome County (Whitney Point Lake area). ~25 drive-in tent & RV sites, first-come-first-served (no formal reservations), no electric, restrooms/showers/dump; rustic. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Smith Point County Park
note: Suffolk County bayside campground (Great South Bay). 50 full-hookup + 120 electric sites, big rigs to ~50 ft; bayfront sites. Requires a Suffolk Green Key (non-resident available $55/yr), 1-week max. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Nickerson Beach Campground
note: Nassau County oceanfront park (run by Dover Group concessionaire). 74 RV sites: water + 30/50A electric, 66 with sewer, max 40 ft; open paved lot set back behind the dunes. Non-residents may book. Apr-Nov. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Indian Island County Park
note: Suffolk County park at the Peconic River/Flanders Bay. ~100-150 tent & trailer sites, some electric, showers, dump; some water-view sites. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: General Clinton Park
note: Town of Bainbridge park on the Susquehanna River. 50 W/E sites (30/50A), 38 pull-thru, showers; first-come-first-served, no reservations. May-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Southaven County Park
note: Suffolk County park on the Carmans River. Tent & RV sites, ~51 with electric, showers; loops set back in pine-oak woods. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Cathedral Pines County Park
note: Suffolk County park, Middle Island. 152 sites; ~10-20 with electric (20A) + water, dump; wooded tent + open-field pull-through RV sites. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Blydenburgh County Park
note: Suffolk County park near Smithtown. ~50 sites, water + electric, showers, dump; accepts big rigs but ~2 mi of rough access road w/ low branches. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Cedar Point County Park
note: Suffolk County park on a bay peninsula, East Hampton. ~200+ tent & trailer sites (no electric reported), bathhouse; wooded loops, walk to beach. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: McCann Campground
note: Village of Greenport park (North Fork). ~40 sites, water/electric + some full hookups, showers, dump; transient + seasonal, online reservations (open to non-residents). RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Morningside Park
note: Town of Fallsburg park on Morningside Lake. ~136 grass sites, full-hookup to primitive (30A E/W/S), pool, bathhouse; rigs to ~50 ft. Open to public. RV Life 1*. --Claude

name: Sears Bellows County Park
note: Suffolk County park on Bellows Pond, Hampton Bays. 40 trailer + 30 tent sites (verify hookups; some report primitive), water fill + dump, showers; some sites at the pond. Green Key required (non-resident available). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Wilcox Memorial Park
note: Dutchess County park (Milan). 27-site campground = 10 RV sites (water+electric) + 17 tent, open May-Sep; advance registration required (no walk-ins), reserve online/phone. RV Life ~3.5*. --Claude

name: Chenango County Cook Park
note: Chenango County park (Greene). 40 sites, 20/30A electric + water, dump, bathhouse; 18-acre pond w/ boat launch. Phone reservations, ~Memorial Day-Columbus Day. Some sites tight/sloped. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Webster Park
note: Monroe County park on Lake Ontario. 46 sites all electric (no sewer); RV/large-trailer sites for 26 ft+ (to ~40 ft); loop set back above the lake. Out-of-county +$10/night. Online/phone reservations, May-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: North Bay Campground
note: City of Fulton public campground on Lake Neatahwanta. 42 daily + 36 seasonal sites; 31 electric, all water; wooded/clearing/waterfront sites; store, beach, boat launch. Mid-May-mid-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: St. Johnsville Marina & Campsite
note: Village of St. Johnsville marina campground on the Mohawk River/Erie Canal (~1 mi E of Lock 16). ~28 sites, 30/50A full hookups + 7 pull-throughs, dump, wifi; May-Oct. Daily + seasonal. Active rail line adjacent (train noise). RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: St. Regis Falls Scenic Campsite
note: Town of Waverly campground (Franklin County) on the St. Regis River (since 1967). RV, tent, waterfront, lean-to, cabin sites; sites C1-C11 transient, C12-C29 seasonal. Phone reservations May-Sep. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: East Sidney Lake Park
note: Town of Sidney-operated campground on USACE East Sidney Lake (Franklin, NY). ~85 RV sites with electric + 14 tent; day/month/season reservations online; boat launch, beach. Lakeside loop. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Hickories Park
note: Town of Owego municipal park on the Susquehanna River. 65 W/E RV sites, boat launch, dog park; first-come-first-served (no reservations), cash/check only. Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Sprague Brook Park
note: Erie County park (Glenwood), the county's only camping park. ~72 sites (electric & non-electric), dump; online reservations via Erie County system, $23-30. Seasonal May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Winding Hills Park
note: Orange County park (Montgomery). 51 wooded sites (electric +$5 & non-electric), dump, no individual sewer; first-come-first-served except holiday weekends (reservations). Resident/non-resident pricing. May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Lake Placid Volunteer Fire Department Campground
note: Run by the Lake Placid Volunteer Fire Dept (community nonprofit), by the fire station; walk to downtown. 15 full-hookup sites (30/50A water+sewer), fits ~45 ft, $40/night (proceeds to VFD). Phone/online reservations, May 15-Oct 15. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Lisbon Beach & Campground
note: Town of Lisbon campground on the St. Lawrence River. 56 serviced + 10 tent sites, W/E and full hookups (30/50A), lifeguarded beach; some riverfront sites. Cheap ($22-28). Online reservations. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Little Wolf Campground
note: Town of Tupper Lake campground on Little Wolf Pond. ~45 RV sites (E/W/S) + 8 tent, lifeguarded beach; waterfront rows (many seasonal). Reserve online. ~Memorial Day-Sep 30. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Ilion Marina & RV Park
note: Village of Ilion marina RV park on the Erie Canal. ~14-16 full-hookup sites (W/S/E, cable, wifi) directly on the canal, $37/night; opens May 1. Transient via Dockmaster. Small. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Wayne County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Wayne County Agricultural Fair (Palmyra). 100 sites (some 50/30A full-hookup, 28 W/E, 50 tent); daily reservations Jun-Sep (other times by reservation), closed ~late Jul-mid Aug for the fair. Pay 14 days ahead, no drop-ins. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Cayuga Wine Trail Campground & Cabins
note: Finger Lakes wine-country campground (ex-Sned Acres), Ovid. Full-hookup shaded RV sites, big-rig accessible, online booking (Campspot); on-site fishing pond. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Camp Bell Campground
note: Near Corning. 96 full/partial-hookup big-rig sites, heated pool, laundry; online + phone booking. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Country Hills Campground
note: Marathon (between Binghamton & Syracuse). 110 full-hookup sites (double-slideout capable), pull-throughs; online daily reservations. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Top-A-Rise Campground
note: Falconer. Mostly seasonal but takes transients: 4 large 30x75 pull-thrus + full-hookup sites, 50A; online booking via CampLife, ~$35-45. Limited nightly inventory - call ahead busy weekends. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Elkdale RV Resort
note: Salamanca, near Allegany SP. Full-hookup 50A transient RV park, nightly $45; 7-day reservation notice. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Camp Elmbois
note: Hammondsport, near Keuka Lake wine trail. Small (~8 sites) full/partial-hookup big-rig-friendly campground; private 2-acre lake w/ paddle boats; online + phone, $30-50. ~May-Oct. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Pope Haven Campground
note: Randolph (Amish Trail). 152 sites, full-hookup + W/E, 20/30/50A; phone/email/online reservations year-round, open May-Oct. Pool, fishing pond. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Tumble Hill Campground
note: Cohocton (Stetson's Tumble Hill). ~45 sites, most full-hookup 20/30/50A; online/Campspot reservations, May-Oct. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Brookside Beach Campground
note: Westfield, near Lake Erie. 70 sites (55 full-hookup, 15 electric, 10 pull-thru), 30/50A, large-rig OK; private Lake Erie beach accessed across Rt 5 (sites not waterfront). May-Oct. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Skybrook Campground
note: Dansville (off I-390). 350+ W/E sites (20/30A), pull-throughs, dump; nightly/weekly/seasonal, daily sites available, phone booking. Weekend-activity oriented, ~May-Oct. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Sugar Creek Glen Campground
note: Dansville. ~131 sites, 100 electric (30A, few 50A), max 38 ft, dump; wooded gorge w/ creeks & waterfalls (tent sites are creekside, not RV). Seasonal Apr 30-Columbus Day. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Houghton/Letchworth KOA Holiday
note: Houghton (formerly Mariposa Ponds), near Letchworth SP. ~75 sites, full hookups (50A), max 36 ft; on-site ponds w/ some pondside sites. Online booking. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Creekview Campsites
note: Trumansburg (Finger Lakes). Tiny family park, ~6 sites, 30A electric, water-to-site, dump; level sites. Website under development - call ahead. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Southern Shores Campground
note: Conesus Lake. 65 sites, 30A full & partial hookups, max ~30 ft; private lakefront beach/dock across E Lake Rd (RV sites upland w/ lake views). Daily $35 full/$25 electric + seasonal. May-Oct. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Wildwood Acres Campground (Bemus Point)
note: Bemus Point, ~1 mi from Chautauqua Lake & Long Point SP. Cement pads, 30A water/electric, dump, heated pool, stocked pond; RV $40-55. No staffed office - reserve via website/voicemail. RV Life $/5*. --Claude

name: Susquehanna Trail Campground
note: Oneonta (off I-88), near Cooperstown. 40 full-hookup (30A) sites, pool, playground; reservable online/phone. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Crazy Acres Campground (Beaver Spring Lake)
note: Davenport (Catskill foothills). 125+ sites, 90+ full hookups; overnight + seasonal; 15-acre spring-fed lake (some lake-overlook sites). Book via Campspot. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Alps Family Campground
note: Averill Park (near Albany). All sites water/electric, many full hookup; overnight ~$45+, reservations required, 2-night min weekends. May 1-Oct 15. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Riverside RV Camping (Bainbridge)
note: Bainbridge (off SR-206 at I-88 exit 8), on the Susquehanna River. ~17 full-hookup (30/50A) sites, some right on the riverbank, RV-only, fits large rigs. Mid-Apr-mid-Oct, phone reservations. Some highway noise. No standalone website. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Aqua Vista Campground
note: Petersburgh, on the Little Hoosic River. ~150 tent/RV sites (some riverfront), 30/50A W/E, pool; nightly transient + seasonal, ~May-Oct. Densely packed per reviews. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Blackthorne Resort
note: East Durham (Catskills). ~150 RV/tent sites, full W/E hookups, nightly ~$45, dump; family entertainment resort (restaurant/bar/events), some long-term campers, main road bisects grounds, dated per reviews. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Waterfalls Campsite
note: Middleburgh. 45 power sites, dump, nightly $35 (W/E $40), online booking; Little Schoharie Creek + 2 waterfalls run through, some creekside sites. ~May-Oct. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: The Outpost Resort
note: Pond Eddy, on the Delaware River. Riverfront full-hookup RV sites up to 50' (20/30/50A W/S/E), bookable via Campspot; on-site restaurant, rafting/tubing, ~70 acres. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Lizzie's Campground
note: South New Berlin, on the Unadilla River. Full hookup $45, W/E $35, dry $30; back-in + pull-thru 30A; opened 2020, pet-friendly, RoverPass booking. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Kittatinny Campground
note: Barryville, on the Delaware River. 350 sites/250 acres, RV sites W/E (20/30/50A), creekside + riverfront open-grass sites, river rafting; online booking via Campspot. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Niagara Hartland RV Resort
note: Gasport (midway Rochester-Buffalo). Family-owned since 1985, nightly full-hookup pull-thru ~$50, online reservations; 8-acre Lake Charles (swim/fish/kayak), some lake-view sites. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Genesee Country Campground
note: Caledonia. RV sites $30/night (+seasonal), 30A pull-thru buddy + 50A back-in, W/E + gray-water; par-3 golf, themed weekends, online booking. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Niagara County Camping Resort
note: Lockport (~27 mi to Niagara Falls). 64 acres, pull-through 30/50A RV sites + cabins, dump, pool, mini-golf; online/phone reservations, ~$59. Swim/fishing pond. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Streamside RV & Golf
note: Pulaski (salmon country). 60 full-hookup sites (20/30/50A) among a 12-hole golf course, restaurant/bar; nightly from ~$30, rigs to 60 ft. Apr 15-Oct 15. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: J & J Campground
note: Mexico, NY. ~84-90 sites, 30/50A full-hookup pull-throughs + big-rig access, tent sites, cabins, pool, store, laundry; phone reservations. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Black River Bay Campground
note: Dexter, on Black River Bay near Lake Ontario/Thousand Islands. ~100 sites (~60 seasonal) but nightly transient available: full-hookup/waterfront $50, elec+water $45; boat ramp, dockage. May 1-Oct 15. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Birch Haven Campground
note: Clayton (Thousand Islands). ~80 sites mostly seasonal but overnight full-hookup welcomed $30/night, 30/50A, rigs to 50 ft. 50% deposit. May 1-Oct 15. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Riverside Acres Campground & Cottages
note: Clayton (Thousand Islands). 66 sites, 48 full-hookup, 5 pull-thru, 40 ft max, 30/50A, pool, cottages; inland off Rte 12E. May-Oct, phone/email reservations. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Shady Shores Campground
note: Fair Haven, on Lake Ontario/Blind Sodus Bay (now Shady Shores RV Resort, rjourney). Mostly full-season leases but nightly RV from ~$45; waterfront sites, beach, boat ramp. Legacy domain dead - book via operator. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Up the Creek Campground
note: Henderson/Woodville (1.5 mi to Southwick Beach SP). ~52 full-hookup sites (30/50A), hot showers, wifi; reservations via Facebook/phone. No official website (beware same-name parks in MN/TN). May-Oct. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Cherry Hill Campground
note: Darien Center, next to Darien Lake theme park. Rustic (no-hookup) RV sites to 40 ft, $47/night, water fill + dump; online booking (Campspot). Concert-night minimums. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Schroon River Escape Lodges & RV Resort
note: Diamond Point (near Lake George). ~1 mi of Schroon River frontage; nightly waterfront-deluxe RV sites $75 (W/E) + full-hookup $70, rigs to 65 ft, pool, beach, kayaks; online booking (Campspot). May-Oct. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Massena International Kampground
note: Massena. Family-owned, 134 sites incl. pull-thrus, 50/30A full-hookup; ~May-Oct. Was dark for 2025; official site confirms 2026 reopening (May 1). Reserve via website/phone. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Plattsburgh RV Park
note: Plattsburgh (off I-87 Exit 39). 200+ full-hookup big-rig sites, heated pool; online booking (Campspot). Lake Champlain beach walkable but sites not waterfront. Opens ~May 15. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Adirondack Gateway Campground & Lodge
note: Caroga/Gloversville area. 166 acres, big-rig pull-thrus, full hookups (20/30/50A), 3 ponds (amenity); ~mid-May-Oct 15. Reserve by phone; also Hipcamp. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Thornbush Acres RV Park
note: Near Indian Lake. 30 full-hookup sites, RV-only, 60 ft max; ~May 15-Oct 15, phone reservations w/ deposit, cash/check. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Spruce Creek Campground
note: Dolgeville, on Spruce Creek (stocked trout stream). ~78 sites (68 RV-capable), full-hookup 50A pull-thrus, bookable Creekside sites; nightly reservations. Apr-Oct. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Royal Mountain Campsites
note: Johnstown. ~50 sites (E/W), 60 ft max, ~30 seasonal; instant online booking (Spot2Nite) + phone. Stocked fishing pond (amenity). Apr 1-Nov 30. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Treasure Isle RV Park
note: Blossvale, on Fish Creek near Sylvan Beach/Oneida Lake. 100 large sites, mostly seasonal w/ limited nightly ($50/night W/E, holiday 3-night min); some sites on the water. Reserve by phone (confirm a transient waterfront site). RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Rancho Pines Campground
note: Near Lake George, on the Schroon River. ~54 sites, full-hookup 30A, RVs to 35 ft; canoe/tube launch; online booking (Campspot) + phone. ~May-Oct. (Verify which sites are riverfront.) RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Riverbend West
note: Blossvale, on Fish/Wood Creek. Year-round, 200+ sites, heavily seasonal w/ gated retiree section but books daily/weekly transient via Campspot; beach + boat slips. Confirm a transient site. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Jellybeans Riverside Campground
note: Brasher Falls, on the St. Regis River. ~65 sites (40 full-hookup), nightly from $38, RVs to ~40 ft; most sites riverfront. May 1-Oct 30. (Campendium's closed tag is stale; official site active 2026 - phone-confirm.) RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Lake Chalet Campground
note: Bridgewater. Motel + campground; 17 W/E RV sites (big-rig friendly, 10 pull-throughs) + cabins, dump; nightly $40-50; 6-acre private lake on-site (swim/paddle). RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Stysh's Brown Barn Campground
note: Boonville, on the Black River. Daily/weekly/seasonal rentals, grass + wooded sites, full-hookup RV sites on a rise (not waterfront). Official site has a TLS cert issue - phone to reserve. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Butternut Hill RV Camping
note: Bouckville (US-20). Electric/water hookups (no sewer; dump station), daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal; hilltop valley views. ~May 15-Oct 15, online booking. Hosts the Madison-Bouckville antique shows. RV Life $$/4.5*. --Claude

name: Cedar Creek Campground
note: Lowville (Tug Hill). 86 acres, 175 sites (seasonals + pull-throughs + wooded), from $30/night, W/E + dump (no full hookups); swim/fishing pond, ATV-friendly. RV Life $$/4*. --Claude

name: Hidden Meadows Campground
note: Boonville. ~11 full-hookup RV sites (30/50A), free wifi, family-owned, newer. No official website (Facebook + directories only), thin reviews - phone to book. RV Life $$/5*. --Claude

name: Ausable Point Campground
note: DEC campground on Lake Champlain (Peru). Sandy shoreline, many sites near the water, no hookups; 14-night max, May-Oct. ReserveAmerica. (RV Life mislabeled it commercial; it is NY State DEC.) RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Red House Ranch Camp
note: Hipcamp on a 200-acre farm, Van Etten. 3 widely-spaced pull-through sites overlooking a pond, fits large RVs. Booked via Hipcamp. RV Life 5*. --Claude

name: Columbus-Belmont State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks. ~35 sites (31 standard electric + group overflow); electric/water, some full hookups (50A), back-ins to ~53 ft, dump station; on a bluff above the Mississippi River. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. Year-round. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: John James Audubon State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Henderson). ~64 standard-electric RV sites + tent; electric/water, some full hookups, dump, back-ins 25-53 ft. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. ~Mar 15-Nov 10. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Lake Malone State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks. ~24 standard-electric sites (back-in + pull-through), 30A electric/water, no sewer, dump, back-ins to ~70 ft; loop set back from the lake. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Taylorsville Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks. ~44 standard-electric RV sites (electric/water, back-in + pull-through) + 10 equestrian + tent/group; bathhouse, laundry, dump; loop set back from the lake (no lake access from sites). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Dale Hollow Lake State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks. ~121 standard-electric sites in pod/spoke loops, some full hookups (50A), back-ins to ~41 ft, + ~24 equestrian; set back ~2 mi from the lake. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: General Burnside Island State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks. Island state park (drive-in via bridge) in Lake Cumberland. ~94 sites (18 full hookup + 74 electric/water), 30/50A, back-ins to ~50-54 ft, 2 bathhouses, laundry, dump; loop in the island interior near the golf course. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Green River Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks. Large lakeside campground; ~157 standard electric/water sites + tent, dump, showers, laundry, grocery; some sites sit on Green River Lake. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. ~Apr-Oct. (Was closed early 2026 for flood electrical repair, target reopen ~mid-March 2026 - confirm open.) RV Life 4.5*. --Claude

name: Pennyrile Forest State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Dawson Springs). ~56 sites, 30/50A electric + water (some full hookups), paved pads, dump, cable; equestrian section has level pull-through hookup sites usable by any RVer. Campground set back from the 56-acre lake. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Big Bone Lick State Historic Site
note: Kentucky State Parks (Union). ~62 paved hilltop sites, electric + water (30A, some full hookup), all back-in, dump, showers, laundry, camp store, playground. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. ~Apr-Oct, 14-day max. Some sites need long water hoses. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: General Butler State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Carrollton). ~104 sites, electric/water (some full-hookup 30/50A pull-throughs), paved/level pads, dump, showers; campground separated from the 30-acre lake. Year-round, reserve via Kentucky State Parks. Some sites narrow; adjacent-highway road noise. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Carter Caves State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Olive Hill). ~89 RV sites, electric (30/50A), some full hookups, pull-thrus, big-rig access, dump, WiFi, + primitive tent and 8 equestrian sites; wooded near the caves/lodge. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Blue Licks Battlefield State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Carlisle). Single loop ~49 modern sites, electric + water (no sewer), level, back-ins ~32-50 ft, dump, showers, laundry; RV loop uphill off US-68 (only 6 primitive tent sites along the Licking River). Year-round (water off & office closed mid-Nov-Mar). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Greenbo Lake State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Greenup). ~58 equipped sites (electric-only + full-hookup w/ sewer, 20/30/50A), back-in + pull-through, gravel pads, RVs to ~50 ft, 3 dump stations, showers, pool, grocery, + ~36 primitive; wooded, lake frontage is by the lodge. Year-round, reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Natural Bridge State Resort Park - Whittleton Campground
note: Kentucky State Parks (Slade). ~40 single-family sites; RV/electric sites 1-3 and 24-37 (30/50A, water at most), dump, showers, camp store; Whittleton Branch creek runs behind many camper/tent sites. Tight loop best for rigs under ~30 ft (a 23-ft fits). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Yatesville Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Louisa). Pleasant Ridge Campground: 27 RV sites, water + 15/30/50A electric (some double-wide), max ~35 ft, dump, showers, laundry; on a ridge above Yatesville Lake (separate boat-in/hike-in primitive sites). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Kentucky Dam Village State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Gilbertsville). ~221 paved back-in sites, electric throughout w/ water at shared pedestals (bring long cord/hose), 20/30/50A, two dump stations, grocery, four bathhouses; set back behind the resort/marina on Kentucky Lake. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. Some short pads. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Barren River Lake State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Lucas). ~99-101 sites; sites 1-9 full hookup (W/E/S), rest water+electric (30/50A), pull-throughs, max ~60 ft, dump, two playgrounds; wooded peninsula on Barren River Lake (some sites have lake views). Sloped - leveling needed. ~late Apr-mid Nov. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: My Old Kentucky Home State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Bardstown). 39 sites (8 pull-through), 5 full hookup, rest water+electric (30/50A), max site length 24-55 ft, dump, bathhouse; golf-course/historic-home setting. Underwent a $4M rebuild (closed 2025, reopening ~spring 2026 - verify open). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Natural Bridge State Resort Park - Middle Fork Campground
note: Kentucky State Parks (Slade). ~36 water+electric RV sites (20/30/50A, 8 pull-through, max ~45 ft) + ~11 tent-only; B-section sites back to Middle Fork Creek. Dump, showers, camp store. ~Mar-Oct. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Cumberland Falls State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Corbin). Ridgeline loop ~49 sites with 21 paved RV pads, water + 10/30/50A electric, dump, grocery, laundry, playground; max RV ~30 ft (a 23-ft fits). Clifty section (~9 sites) is tent-only. On a ridge set back from the Cumberland River. ~mid-Mar-mid-Nov. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. NOTE: the campground is closed for the 2026 season for renovations (reopens 2027); park/reservations otherwise via ReserveAmerica. --Claude

name: Grayson Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Grayson). 71 sites, 30/50A electric + shared water spigots (bring 35-50 ft hose), no full hookups, max RV ~45-60 ft, single dump, showers; on a ridge set back from Grayson Lake (lake access via trail), adjacent to the golf course. Sloped - leveling needed. ~Apr-Oct. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Kincaid Lake State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Falmouth). ~84 standard-electric RV sites (+ 75 tent), 20A electric + water, gravel pads, back-in & some pull-through, large rigs OK, bathhouse, dump; RV loops set back in woods (only some tent sites near the 183-acre lake). ~Mar-Nov 15. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Carr Creek State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Sassafras). 39 sites, electric (30A) + water, back-in, max RV ~30-60 ft, bathhouse, dump; adjacent to the longest sand beach in the KY park system on 750-acre Carr Fork Lake (some RV sites have lake views). ~Mar-Nov. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3.5*. --Claude

name: Lake Barkley State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Cadiz). ~78 sites (73 standard electric 30A + water, 6 no-hookup), paved back-in (some steep/unlevel), max RV ~32-58 ft, two bathhouses, dump, campers' boat ramp; wooded ravine with many sites overlooking Lake Barkley fingers. ~Apr-Oct. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Kenlake State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Hardin), on the W shore of Kentucky Lake. Reopened Mar 2026 after a $9.3M renovation: 63 full-service sites (sewer), pull-throughs, new electric/water, new bathhouse, more ADA sites; many sites have Kentucky Lake views (RV pads on a wooded bluff). Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3* (pre-renovation). --Claude

name: Lake Cumberland State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Jamestown). Campground on a wooded peninsula/bluff between Pumpkin Creek and Lake Cumberland: ~87 sites (11 full-service + electric, plus tent/primitive), back-in, max RV ~30-35 ft, dump; water off Nov 1-Mar 15. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 3*. --Claude

name: Fort Boonesborough State Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Richmond), on the Kentucky River. 166 sites, electric/water (18 full hookup), paved/level, back-in + pull-through, big-rig friendly, 3 dump stations, showers, laundry, WiFi; a few sites have river views, none directly on the water. CLOSED for flood-damage repairs through 12/31/2026 - scheduled to reopen Jan 2027; verify before relying on it. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 4*. --Claude

name: Jenny Wiley State Resort Park
note: Kentucky State Parks (Prestonsburg), on Dewey Lake. ~121 sites, electric/water/sewer (20-50A), site lengths ~19-95 ft (most 40-50), boat ramp nearby; some sites overlook the lake. CLOSED for a major renovation, scheduled to reopen ~March 2027; verify before relying on it. Reserve via Kentucky State Parks. RV Life 2*. --Claude

name: Kendall Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland. Tailwater campground on the Cumberland River just below Wolf Creek Dam (Jamestown), next to Wolf Creek NFH. 116 sites, water+electric (20/30/50A), two heavily wooded loops, 2 bathhouses, 2 dump stations; back-ins + some pull-throughs, pads to ~50 ft (some listed longer). Sites overlook the river (scenic tailwater); not directly on the water. Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Waitsboro Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland (Somerset). Small: ~17 RV sites (electric) + 5 tent, 8 sites on the waterfront; dump, showers, laundry, boat ramp, playground. Tight exit turnaround - 5th-wheels over 32 ft are warned off, but a 23-ft rig is fine. Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Fishing Creek Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland (W of Somerset). 26 RV sites + 20 tent, water+electric; boat ramp, dump, showers, laundry. Wooded; many sites sit near/on the water at summer pool (steep inclines down to the lake between areas, sharp entrance). Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Fall Creek Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Cumberland (Monticello). Small lakeside loop: 10 RV sites (water+electric, ~29-70 ft), all waterfront, + dump, boat ramp, playground, showers. Reserve online. ~mid-May to mid-Oct. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Baileys Point Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Barren River Lake (Lucas/Scottsville). Largest of the lake's Corps campgrounds: 215 sites across 5 loops, most water+electric, bathhouses, dump, laundry, two playgrounds, sand volleyball, amphitheater, boat ramp; loops B & C have many sites facing the lake (some on private coves for mooring). Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: The Narrows Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Barren River Lake (Lucas), near the marina/boat ramp. 92 sites in two loops, water+electric (electric ~May-Sept); Loop B sites let you pull a boat right up to the site (on the water). Sand beach, amphitheater, nature trail. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Tailwater Campground (Barren River Lake)
note: USACE Nashville District, Barren River Lake (Glasgow). 48 spread-out sites along the Barren River just below the dam; water + 50A electric in peak season (~May-Sept), primitive off-season (dump, potable water, pit toilets). Boat ramp and riverside paths, but no sites have direct water access. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Canal Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Barkley (Grand Rivers), on the canal connecting Lake Barkley and Kentucky Lake at the north end of LBL. ~115 sites, water+electric (some full hookup), densely wooded; 3 dump stations, showers, laundry, playground, beach, boat ramps. Many sites are waterfront on one of the two lakes. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Hurricane Creek Campground (Lake Barkley)
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Barkley (Eddyville). 51 water+electric sites (6 walk-to), lakefront camping with direct lake access; showers, laundry, playground, boat ramp. Max site ~40 ft; a 23-ft rig is fine. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Eureka Campground
note: USACE Nashville District, Lake Barkley (Kuttawa/Eddyville). Small: 26 sites, all water+electric, some with direct lake access; showers, playground, boat launch, swimming area, picnic shelter. Chestnut Oaks Trail starts here. Peak ~late Apr-mid Oct. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Dog Creek Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Nolin River Lake (Cub Run). 70 sites (50 water+electric to 50A), well-spaced; many sites along the shoreline (waterfront), modern bathhouse, dump station, showers, beach. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Moutardier Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Nolin River Lake (Leitchfield/Sweeden). Large: 167 sites, water+electric (50A, some pads to ~75 ft), flush toilets, showers, dump, picnic shelters, boat ramp, marina; sites along the shoreline (waterfront). Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Axtel Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (McDaniels/Falls of Rough). Large: 158 sites, most water+electric, sits directly on the lakeshore with lake views from nearly all sites (waterfront sites book fast); swim beach, boat ramp, dump, showers, playground, fish-cleaning station. Reserve online. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Laurel Branch Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (McDaniels). 62 sites (several electric), most waterfront (rest a short walk to the lake); flush toilets, showers, playground, boat ramp, beach, fish-cleaning, nature trail. ~Apr 14-Oct 30. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Cave Creek Campground (Rough River Lake)
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (Falls of Rough); Corps land run for the 2026 season by concessionaire BrightSky Campgrounds. 65 sites (36 electric/water; RV section tiered down the hillside, pads to big-rig length); dump, showers, boat ramp, fishing pier, disc golf, playground. Hilltop basic sites have no water access; lower RV sites overlook the cove (most not directly on the water). ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: North Fork Campground (Rough River Lake)
note: USACE Louisville District, Rough River Lake (McDaniels); Corps land run for the 2026 season by concessionaire BrightSky Campgrounds. 81 sites (50 electric); several sites on the waterfront, others a short walk to the lake; dump, showers, beach, boat ramp, fishing pier, two group shelters, playground. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Pikes Ridge Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Green River Lake (Campbellsville); Corps land run for the 2026 season by concessionaire BrightSky Campgrounds. Shoreline campground: 60 sites (20 water+electric), several waterfront; boat ramp, courtesy/tie-up docks, fishing pier, swim beach, playground, showers, hiking trail. ~Apr-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Smith Ridge Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Green River Lake (Campbellsville). 80 sites, most with electric (many water+electric), set along the lake perimeter with many waterfront-access sites; flush toilets, showers, playground, hiking trail; marina swim beach/disc golf within ~1 mi. Big, well-spaced sites; weak cell signal. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Holmes Bend Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Green River Lake (Columbia/Cane Valley). 125 sites (40 electric-only, 62 water+electric), pads to ~70 ft, set along the lake perimeter with many waterfront-access sites; flush toilets, showers, two playgrounds, interpretive trail, paved roads good for biking; swim beach/boat ramp/fishing pier marina-managed within ~1 mi. Reserve online. --Claude  RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Buckhorn Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Buckhorn Lake (Buckhorn), eastern KY Cumberland Plateau. ~30 paved standard-electric sites + walk-in tent and boat-in; 14 waterfront sites at the water's edge. Flush toilets, hot showers, dump, laundry, WiFi, playground, swim area, boat ramp. RVs to ~50 ft. Open ~May 1-Sep 30. Reserve online. RV Life 4*/$. --Claude

name: Littcarr Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Carr Creek (Carr Fork) Lake (Littcarr/Vicco), SE KY mountains. 45 water+electric sites, waterfront sites with direct lake access; flush toilets, showers, laundry, dump, two playgrounds, picnic shelter, horseshoe/basketball courts. RVs to ~54 ft. Reserve online. Distinct from Carr Creek State Park on the same lake. RV Life 2.5*/$. --Claude

name: Trace Branch Campground
note: USACE Louisville District, Buckhorn Lake (near Hazard), eastern KY. Mostly-waterfront sites with electric/water (some 50A, some sewer), pads to ~70 ft; shower house, dump, playground, fishing area. Mix of reservable and first-come sites. Open ~May-Sept. --Claude  RV Life 3.5*/$ (auto 6/2026).

name: Blue Heron Campground
note: NPS, Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area (Stearns), on the plateau near the Blue Heron historic mining community. 45 sites, all water+electric (30A), back-in + pull-through, pads to ~61 ft; flush toilets, hot showers, dump station. Wooded (not on the water; the river gorge is below the rim). ~Apr-Nov. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$. --Claude

name: Twin Knobs Campground
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Cave Run Lake (Salt Lick/Morehead). Large: 200+ sites over 10 loops, electric to full-hookup (premium), paved spurs to ~70 ft, dump, bathhouses, camp store, boat ramp, sandy swim beach; outer-loop sites sit on the lakeshore (waterfront sites book early). ~late Apr-early Nov. Reserve online. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Clear Creek Campground
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Clear Creek Recreation Area (Salt Lick), E of Cave Run Lake. Small primitive ~20-site campground along Clear Creek (tent/RV pads, no hookups), vault toilets, drinking water; boat ramp onto Clear Creek Lake + picnic area nearby. Max RV ~30 ft (a 23-ft rig fits); first-come, first-served, no reservations. ~Apr 15-Nov 29. RV Life 3.5*/$. --Claude

name: Holly Bay Campground
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Laurel River Lake (Keavy/London), ~2 mi N of Laurel River Dam. ~75 drive-in sites (single + double family), 20/30/50A electric + water, paved pads to ~50 ft, flush toilets, showers; near Holly Bay Marina/boat ramp. Many sites have water views but none directly on the shore. Run by concessionaire American Land & Leisure. ~early Apr-mid Oct. Reserve online. RV Life 5*/$$. --Claude

name: Grove Campground (Laurel River Lake)
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Laurel River Lake (Corbin). Drive-in campground on a wooded ridge above the lake: single + double family sites, 30A electric (some full-hookup), paved pads to ~50 ft, flush toilets, showers, drinking water. Reservable ~Memorial Day-Labor Day (else first-come). Separate from the Grove boat-in campground. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Bee Rock Campground
note: USFS Daniel Boone NF, Bee Rock Recreation Area (off KY 192, ~18 mi W of London), along the Rockcastle River; renovated 2022. 25 wooded first-come sites, no hookups, vault toilets, water spigots. North loop is the RV-friendly side; best for compact rigs - a 23-ft EKKO-sized motorhome fits, but call the London Ranger District before bringing anything bigger (access road has narrow/bumpy stretches near the river). Pulaski-side loop ~mid-Apr-mid-Oct, Laurel-side year-round. RV Life lists it under 'state' but it is USFS. RV Life 3*/$. --Claude

name: Energy Lake Campground
note: USFS Land Between the Lakes NRA (Golden Pond), on Energy Lake (a quiet Lake Barkley embayment). 35 wooded sites, 33 with electric (30/50A), water along the loop road, dump station, restrooms/showers, swim beach, playground, kayak rentals. Some sites have lake views with trails down to the water (none directly on the shore); rigs over ~30 ft fit only some sites - call ahead. Reserve via LBL's reservation system, not Recreation.gov. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Redd Hollow Campground
note: USFS Land Between the Lakes NRA (Kentucky Lake / west side). Primitive self-service campground: pea-gravel pads, fire rings, picnic tables, vault toilet, boat ramp, lake access; no hookups or potable water (dump/water at Golden Pond). Good-condition gravel access road (no 4WD). Some sites sit right on the water, others higher with lake views; a few pads are big enough for a small RV (best for compact rigs like a 23-ft EKKO). First-come, self-pay ($10). RV Life 4.5*/$. --Claude

name: Taylor Bay Campground
note: USFS Land Between the Lakes NRA, on Taylor Bay of Lake Barkley (east side, by the waterfowl refuge). Primitive self-service campground: ~8-10 lakefront sites (boats can tie up) + ~8-10 wooded sites, designated pads, picnic tables, grills, fire rings, vault toilets, boat ramp; no hookups or potable water. Accommodates tents and RVs. First-come, self-pay ($10). RV Life 2.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Unhitched Paducah RV Park
note: Private (Paducah). Full-hookup RV park (20/30/50A), pull-through + back-in, daily/weekly/monthly; clubhouse, 2 bathhouses, laundry, propane, dog parks. Catch-and-release fishing pond with dock - some sites sit on the pond. Real transient park. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Duck Creek RV Park
note: Private (Paducah). 89 oversized level full-hookup sites (30/50A), pull-throughs, free WiFi, seasonal pool, stocked fishing pond, dog park/wash, walking trails. Easy in/out for overnighters; some highway noise near the front sites. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Birdsville Riverside RV Park
note: Private (Smithland), on the Ohio River near the Cumberland confluence. 34 full-hookup riverfront sites (30/50A), big-rig friendly, shade trees, small dock; Harvest Hosts. Seasonal operation, transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Stagecoach Station Campground
note: Private (Benton), near Kentucky Lake / LBL. Water+electric (50A) sites, pull-through + back-in to ~70 ft, dump station (no individual sewer; weekend pump-out), bathhouse, game room, WiFi. Transient, friendly owners. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Tween the Lakes Campground
note: Private (Grand Rivers), easy on/off I-24/I-69 between Kentucky Lake and Lake Barkley. Full-hookup (50A) sites, concrete pads; a straightforward overnight stop. No on-site bathhouse/showers as of recent reports (permit pending). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Tanglewood Campground & RV Resort
note: Private (Hardin/Aurora), ~1 mi from LBL, walking distance to Kentucky Lake. 60 full-hookup sites (30/50A), tents to Class A; laundry, showers, fish-cleaning station, WiFi, pavilion, fishing pond, playground. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Birmingham Point Campground
note: Private (Benton), on Kentucky Lake. 76 sites, water+electric (20/30/50A), boat ramp, fishing pier, swim beach, playground; lake-view sites. Mostly seasonal campers with ~10 sites kept for short-term/transient. Tight exit - rigs over ~38 ft struggle (a 23-ft rig is fine). RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Kamptown RV Resort
note: Private (Cadiz), near Lake Barkley. 93 full-hookup gravel sites, swimming pool, playground; short- and long-term stays. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Lake Cumberland RV Park
note: Private (Bronston), ~5 min to a Lake Cumberland boat ramp with free shuttle to State Dock. 102 sites, electric/full hookups, wooded rolling terrain; reservations + monthly options. RV Life 4*/$$. --Claude

name: Somerset KY RV Park
note: Private (Somerset). Small, well-kept full-hookup park across from a golf course; friendly, quiet, well-run. Transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$. --Claude

name: Wolf River Resort & Marina
