note: USACE Truman Lake campground atop an Ozark bluff, 56 electric sites (25 50A w/water, 7 50A, 24 30A) plus 4 primitive; reservable online, seasonal. --Claude

name: Sparrowfoot
note: USACE Truman Lake campground on the Grand River Arm near Clinton, 83 electric sites (56 50A w/water, 27 30A) with a dump station; reservable online, seasonal. --Claude

name: Talley Bend
note: USACE Truman Lake campground near Lowry City, 64 sites with 30A electric and water, showers and a dump station, RVs to ~60 ft; reservable online. --Claude

name: Thibaut Point
note: USACE Truman Lake campground on a point near Warsaw, 39 individual 50A electric+water sites plus 5 primitive and a separate group camp; reservable online with weekend/holiday minimums. --Claude

name: Cedar Ridge
note: USACE Stockton Lake campground on the Little Sac arm, 54 sites (~21 electric, rest non-electric incl. some tent-only) with several sites on the lake shore; year-round, overnight-only, reservable online (water/electric mid-Apr to Sep 30). --Claude

name: Crabtree Cove
note: USACE Stockton Lake campground with an electric loop and a non-electric loop (~58 sites, 30A), some sites right on the lake edge; year-round, reservable online. --Claude

name: Hawker Point
note: USACE Stockton Lake campground (62 sites, mostly electric) on a hill above the lake with good water views; reservable online (water/electric Apr 16 to Sep 30). --Claude

name: Orleans Trail
note: Largest Stockton Lake COE campground (~118 sites North+South, ~31 electric) with three shower houses, two dump stations and a swim beach; lake views but no shore access from sites; reservable online. --Claude

name: Ruark Bluff East
note: USACE Stockton Lake campground on the Big Sac arm (~91 sites, ~28 electric) with a boat ramp and beach; year-round, reservable online (water/electric Apr 16 to Sep 30). --Claude

name: Ruark Bluff West
note: Forested USACE Stockton Lake campground (74 sites, ~48 with 30A electric) with showers, dump station and boat ramp; reservable online (water/electric mid-Apr to late Sep). --Claude

name: Damsite
note: Largest Pomme de Terre Lake COE campground (87 sites, 80 electric+water) just above the dam; reservable online, full season mid-Apr to Sep 30 with an electric-only shoulder season. --Claude

name: Lightfoot Landing
note: Small quiet COE peninsula campground on Pomme de Terre Lake, all 35 sites electric+water; reservable online, full season mid-Apr to Sep 30 with an electric-only shoulder season. --Claude

name: Nemo Landing
note: USACE Pomme de Terre Lake campground on the Lindley Creek arm (~89 sites: 38 electric+water, 25 electric, 15 non-electric) with hot showers, swim beach and boat ramp; reservable online, seasonal. --Claude

name: Outlet Park
note: Small USACE campground below the Pomme de Terre dam, ~19 electric+water sites along the Pomme de Terre River; reservable online, seasonal. --Claude

name: Wheatland Park
note: USACE Pomme de Terre Lake campground (41 sites, 35 electric+water) with a sandy swim beach, boat ramp and two shower houses; reservable online, seasonal. --Claude

name: Aunts Creek
note: USACE Table Rock Lake campground on the James River Arm, 55 electric/water sites with hot showers and a dump station; reservable online (open May-mid-Sep). --Claude

name: Baxter
note: USACE Table Rock Lake campground near Lampe on the White River Arm, 49 electric/water sites with several pads right at the lake's edge; reservable online. --Claude

name: Big M
note: USACE Table Rock Lake campground near Cassville on the White River Arm, 23 of 61 sites with 50A electric+water plus an adjacent marina and swim area; reservable online. --Claude

name: Campbell Point
note: Large USACE Table Rock Lake campground at Shell Knob (76 sites, most electric+water) with a sandy beach and marina; waterfront sites book early; reservable online. --Claude

name: Cape Fair
note: USACE Table Rock Lake campground near Cape Fair, 45 electric sites a good number of them along the waterfront; reservable online. --Claude

name: Eagle Rock
note: USACE Table Rock Lake campground south of Eagle Rock with paved lakeside electric RV pads on the lake's east side plus a marina and boat ramp; reservable online. --Claude

name: Indian Point
note: Large USACE Table Rock Lake campground ~8 mi from Branson (74 sites, mostly electric+water) with many lakefront sites, a swim beach, boat ramp and marina; reservable online Apr-Oct. --Claude

name: Mill Creek
note: USACE Table Rock Lake campground near Lampe on a peninsula surrounded by water on three sides, 67 electric sites many adjacent to the water's edge; reservable online. --Claude

name: Viola
note: USACE Table Rock Lake campground on the Kings River Arm at Shell Knob (~56 sites, ~42 electric) including waterfront pads, plus the Kings River Marina; access roads can be rough for larger rigs; reservable online Apr-Sep. --Claude

name: Beaver Creek
note: USACE Bull Shoals Lake campground on the Beaver Creek arm near Forsyth, 33 electric/water sites (paved spurs to ~42 ft) with a swim beach; reservable online Apr-Sep. --Claude

name: River Run Park
note: USACE campground just below Powersite Dam near Forsyth, 32 electric/water sites with immediate White River access; reservable online. --Claude

name: Theodosia Park

name: Greenville Recreation Area

name: Bluff View
note: USACE Clearwater Lake campground on a bluff above the lake, 60 sites many with electric/water, plus a boat ramp and swimming area; reservable in peak season, FCFS off-peak. --Claude

name: Highway K
note: USACE Clearwater Lake campground with 83 electric sites lining both banks of the Black River above the lake, popular for wading and canoeing from the sites; reservable in peak season, FCFS off-peak. --Claude

name: Webb Creek
note: Small USACE Clearwater Lake campground (31 sites, electric and non-electric) on a wooded hillside with a boat ramp and swimming area; reservable in peak season, FCFS off-peak. --Claude

name: Piedmont Park
note: USACE Clearwater Lake campground near Piedmont, 97 sites many with electric/water, plus a boat ramp and swimming area; reservable in peak season, FCFS off-peak. --Claude

name: River Road Park
note: USACE Clearwater Lake campground near Piedmont, most of its sites electric/water (some premium full-hookup), with a boat ramp and picnic shelters; reservable in peak season, FCFS off-peak. --Claude

name: Frank Russell

name: Indian Creek
note: Large USACE Mark Twain Lake campground on the north shore, 190 all-electric sites (20/30/50A); reservable online (separate group camp and hike-in sites excluded). --Claude

name: Ray Behrens

name: Lane Spring Recreation Area

name: Red Bluff Recreation Area

name: Pulltite Campground

name: Two Rivers Campground

name: Whetstone Creek Conservation Area

Drive-in via gravel shore roads. RV Life 3*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

Free primitive camping in designated areas only, 14-day max, FCFS — no reservations; three vault toilets, picnic tables and fire rings; no hookups, showers or trash service (KDWP area page). Camping areas on the west, north and east shores reached by gravel roads. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cheney State Park (East Shore)

name: Cheney State Park (West Shore)

name: El Dorado State Park (Walnut River)

Sites sit back from the river/swim pond behind trees — satellite shows no pads at water. Reservable online. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Pomona State Park
note: KDWP state park on the south shore of Pomona Reservoir (USACE lake, state-run park); 142 utility sites with electric+water, some sewer, numerous 50-amp big-rig sites, plus primitive (per ReserveAmerica). Reservable online. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wilson State Park

name: Fall River State Park (Quarry Bay)
note: Northeast-shore area of Fall River SP near the park office (Casner Creek side); utility and primitive spur sites on a point beside the swim beach (ReserveAmerica Quarry Bay/Casner Creek loops; satellite). Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Historic Lake Scott State Park

name: Elk City State Park

Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Geary State Fishing Lake
note: KDWP state fishing lake 10 mi south of Junction City; free primitive camping in designated areas, 14-day limit, FCFS — no reservations; picnic shelters, fire rings, vault toilet, boat ramp, no water or electric (KDWP). Gravel drive-in access. RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Crawford State Park

name: Meade State Park

name: Lovewell State Park
note: KDWP state park on the north shore of Bureau of Reclamation Lovewell Reservoir (RV Life mislabels it commercial 'Lovewell Lake'); Willow, Cottonwood, Cedar Point, Walleye Point and Southwinds campgrounds with ~28 full-hookup, 90 W/E and 36 electric sites plus ~240 primitive per KDWP; marina Apr-Sep; the dense Twin Coves trailer rows are leased mobile-home spaces, not transient sites. Reservable online; remaining sites FCFS. Pinned at Willow utility campground. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Goodman State Fishing Lake

name: Cross Timbers State Park (Toronto Point Area)
note: Main camping area on Toronto Lake's north shore: ~19 full-hookup and ~43 W/E sites across Osage Plains, Timber Ridge, Clovis Point and East Ramp loops plus ~60 primitive sites (Sandstone, Chautauqua Hills) and modern cabins; reservable online (per-site listings). RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cross Timbers State Park (Holiday Hill Area)

note: Small private park off K-16 in Emmett; 50-amp full-hookup pull-through sites, gravel pads, dump, laundry, quiet, $20/night. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Base Camp Humboldt
note: Private 21-acre 'glampground' + RV park, trailhead and bike park in Humboldt near the Neosho River and Southwind Rail Trail; full-hookup pull-through sites (30/50-amp), tiny-home cabins, quarry pond for fishing and paddling. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: 4 Aces RV Park (Kinsley)
note: Private park on US-50 in Kinsley; full-hookup pull-through sites with concrete pads, 24/7 bathhouse, laundry, nature trail and a creek. Clean, well-kept overnight stop. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Champion Ridge Inn & Country RV Park (Kingman)
note: Private RV park on a farmstead E of Kingman adjacent US-54/400 (opened 2015); 9 level full-hookup 50-amp sites, quiet, owner-run. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Rox Landing (Medicine Lodge)
note: Private park near Medicine Lodge in the Gypsum Hills; 12 shaded big-rig full-hookup sites (many pull-through), showers, laundry, dump. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: McPherson RV Ranch
note: Private RV park and horse motel near I-135 at McPherson; big-rig full-hookup sites (30x70 ft), pond, rec hall, laundry. General campground that also boards horses. Some highway/train noise. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hillside RV Park (Oswego)
note: Private park on Hwy 59 at the south edge of Oswego; 24 pull-through + 10 back-in full-hookup 50-amp sites, shade, near the Neosho River. Many long-term campers but orderly and overnight-friendly. RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Triple J RV Park (Russell)
note: Private park off I-70 in Russell; wide full-hookup sites with concrete patios and fire pits, clean bathhouse, dog run, walk to restaurants. Older park; interstate noise. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Southern Winds RV Park (South Haven)
note: Private park just off I-35 near the Oklahoma line; 45 extra-long full-hookup pull-through sites, showers, laundry, very clean and quiet. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lighthouse Landing RV Park & Cabins (South Hutchinson)
note: Private park in South Hutchinson near the Cosmosphere and Strataca salt mine; level full-hookup pull-through sites, laundry, activity room, storm shelter, pond. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Seidels RV Park (Pratt)
note: Private park NE of Pratt; full and partial 30/50-amp sites plus tent sites, shade, petting zoo, dump, $30/night. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Bert's RV Park (Pratt)
note: Small owner-run private park near Pratt; full-hookup big-rig-friendly sites, good water pressure, friendly hosts who guide you in. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Sycamore Springs Resort (Sabetha)
note: Private 60-acre resort/whitetail ranch near Sabetha; two full-hookup RV areas (rigs to 50 ft), creek, fishing pond, plus an indoor wood roller rink, mini-golf and courts. Reservable, $33–55. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: The Rest Stop RV Resort (Seneca)
note: Private park off Hwy 36 at Seneca; full-hookup concrete pull-throughs with large patios, view of a fishing pond, pavilion, laundry, storm shelter, bathhouse. Under new ownership since 2025. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Beaver Creek RV Park (Smith Center)
note: Private park off Hwy 36 at Smith Center (near the geographic center of the US); 9 full-hookup sites, on-site bar & grill, laundry, rooms for rent. Pet- and horse-friendly. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Big Brutus RV Park (West Mineral)
note: RV park at the Big Brutus mining-shovel museum (Big Brutus, Inc., a nonprofit) in West Mineral; 14 full-hookup sites (new in 2024) plus 10 water/electric, 24-hr showers, dump. Reservable. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Still Waters Edge Retreat (Yates Center)
note: Private RV park and cabin retreat near Yates Center; 12 full-hookup 30/50-amp sites, year-round, with canoeing, kayaking, fishing, a zip line and family activities. Reservable. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Ellis Lakeside Campground
note: City of Ellis campground on Ellis City Lake (Big Creek), 7 blocks from I-70 exit 145; 18 graveled pull-through sites, 15 with 30/50-amp water/electric, showers, dump, boat dock, fishing pier, rigs to 50 ft. FCFS self-pay. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wolf Pond Park (Ellinwood)
note: City of Ellinwood park overlooking Wolf Pond; 4 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric and water, rigs to 50 ft, free–$8/night FCFS. Gateway to the Quivira/Cheyenne Bottoms wetlands. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Boyd Lake State Park (Cottonwood)
note: CPW park on the east shore of Boyd Lake near Loveland; Cottonwood Campground has 140 paved pull-through sites with 20/30/50-amp electric, central water and dump, three shower buildings. Loops sit back from the shore behind the marina/swim beach. Open year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). Loops sit ~100yd back behind a shoreline treeline and the paved lake-loop path; edge/perimeter sites have open reservoir views (lakeview); the sandy swim beach is a separate facility N of the campground. --Claude

name: St. Vrain State Park
note: CPW park off I-25 at Longmont, built around a chain of ponds; one contiguous campground (Pelican/Mallard/Sandpiper/Bald Eagle/Red Tail/Killdeer loops, sites 1-87) on level concrete pads to 40 ft. Sites 1-41 electric-only (30/50-amp), 42-87 full hookup. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Jackson Lake State Park (Pelican & Cove)
note: Connected west-shore loops at Jackson Lake reservoir; the Pelican loop fronts the lake directly (sites with a short rocky step down to the waterline), while Cove sits back by the park amphitheater. ~67 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric (Pelican sites 79-82 tent/hammock). One of three loops kept open in winter; reservoir draws down for irrigation by mid/late summer. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Jackson Lake State Park (Lakeside)
note: Southernmost electric loop on Jackson Lake's west shore; the lake-side (east) row of sites sits right on the shoreline per the CPW campground map. ~62 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric (sites 49-52 non-electric tent/hammock), dump station, RVs to ~45 ft. Open year-round; reservoir draws down for irrigation by mid/late summer. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Jackson Lake State Park (Sandpiper/Fox Hills/Northview)
note: Contiguous chain of NW-shore loops at Jackson Lake; ~124 sites total. Northview and Sandpiper have 20/30-amp electric; Fox Hills is the no-hookup loop. Dump station in the complex. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). Sandpiper's east-side sites (esp. 124-132, with 128/130 closest) sit on the wide sandy beach apron on the lake side = lakefront; Fox Hills and Northview loops are set back from the shore. --Claude

name: North Sterling State Park (Elks)
note: Northern campground by the Elks boat ramp at the dam end of North Sterling Reservoir; 50 sites, all 30-amp electric, ~34 pull-through, camper-services building with showers and laundry. One of the park's two winter campgrounds. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: North Sterling State Park (Inlet Grove & Chimney View)
note: Two adjacent loops off the South Entrance at the inlet cove of North Sterling Reservoir; ~89 sites with 50-amp electric. Inlet Grove (lakeside, large pull-throughs) is seasonal mid-Apr-Sep; Chimney View (added 2013, on a rise) stays open year-round (water winterized). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). Inlet Grove's water-facing row (odd sites ~117-131) overlooks the inlet cove from an elevated bench (lakeview); not on a beach apron. Chimney View on a rise, set back. --Claude

name: Chatfield State Park
note: Large CPW campground on the SW shore of Chatfield Reservoir near Littleton; 197 sites (loops A-D), all 20/30/50-amp electric, 146 full hookup mid-Apr to mid-Oct, RVs to ~45 ft, 14-day max. Rigs over 13 ft must use the Plum Creek entrance. Year-round (winter limited to electric C-Loop). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Golden Gate Canyon State Park (Reverend's Ridge)
note: Forested drive-in campground at ~9,100 ft NW of Golden; 97 sites, 59 with 20/30/50-amp electric, flush toilets, hot showers, laundry, dump station. Stays open at limited capacity through winter. (Aspen Meadows is the separate walk-in tent campground.) Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mueller State Park
note: High-country campground at ~9,500 ft west of Divide; 99 sites with 20/30-amp electric plus walk-to tent sites, flush toilets, hot showers, laundry, dump station. ~29 electric sites stay open in winter (dry-dump). RVs to ~60 ft. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (Rocky Ridge)
note: The only electric campground at Eleven Mile Reservoir (~8,600 ft); ~144 sites in loops A/B/D with 30-amp electric, flush toilets and showers, dump station. Set among trees and rocky outcrops above the reservoir. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Loops sit atop a rocky ridge above the reservoir; many sites (incl. exposed electric loops A/B/D) have open reservoir views, ~5-min walk down to shore - lakeview, not on a beach. --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (North Shore)
note: Drive-in north-shore campground at Eleven Mile Reservoir; ~80 single-family sites with its own boat ramp, vault toilets, water, dump station; no electric (Rocky Ridge is the park's only electric loop). Big-rig friendly with pull-throughs. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (Stoll Mountain)
note: Drive-in lakeside loop at Eleven Mile Reservoir; ~24 sites with long pads, water and vault toilets, no electric hookups. ~8,600 ft. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (Cross Creek)
note: Small drive-in lakeside loop (~15 sites) on the west arm of Eleven Mile Reservoir; water, vault toilets, no electric hookups; big-rig friendly. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (Witcher's Cove)
note: Drive-in loop (~21 sites) on the south side of Eleven Mile Reservoir; water, vault toilets, dump station, pull-throughs, no electric hookups. ~8,590 ft. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 2*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (Lazy Boy)
note: Small secluded drive-in lakeside loop (~14 sites) at Eleven Mile Reservoir; water, vault toilets, no electric hookups. ~8,600 ft. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Open-shore cove sites; firsthand reviews report pads right at the water ('really close to the water, perfect for fishing'). --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (Howbert Point)
note: Small drive-in loop (~10 sites) on a peninsula jutting into Eleven Mile Reservoir; water, vault toilets, no electric hookups. ~8,590 ft. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). --Claude

name: Eleven Mile State Park (Rocking Chair)
note: Drive-in lakeside loop (~13 sites) at Eleven Mile Reservoir with 40-70 ft pads; water, vault toilets, no electric hookups. ~8,600 ft. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lake Pueblo State Park (Northern Plains)
note: NW-side campground complex at Pueblo Reservoir; 214 sites in three loops - Prairie Ridge and Yucca Flats are paved 50-amp electric loops, Kettle Creek is the rustic no-electric loop. Water hydrants and dump station; sites set back on the plains. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Loops sit on the plains terrace above a rocky/cliff shore (not sandy beach); closer Yucca Flats sites overlook the lake (lakeview). Named swim beach is a separate day-use area. --Claude

name: Lake Pueblo State Park (Arkansas Point)
note: South-side campground on Arkansas Point ridge above Pueblo Reservoir; 95 sites with 30/50-amp electric, ~35 pull-throughs (big-rig capable), water fill stations, dump station, hot-shower bathhouses. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). West-side Loop B sites overlook the reservoir from the elevated Arkansas Point bench per CPW campground map ('Nice View of Lake'); no beach at the loops (lakeview). --Claude

name: Lathrop State Park (Pinon)
note: Paved campground on the north side of Martin Lake at Lathrop (Colorado's first state park) near Walsenburg; 82 pull-through/back-in sites with 20/30/50-amp electric, dump station, showers; walking distance to the park golf course. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lathrop State Park (Yucca)
note: Rustic non-electric loop on the SE side of Martin Lake at Lathrop; 21 basic sites with table and fire pit, vault toilets, dump station nearby. Largest sites ~25 ft. Open May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Trinidad Lake State Park (Carpios Ridge)
note: Campground on Carpios Ridge above the north shore of Trinidad Lake; 63 sites all with electric, 7 seasonal full-hookup (Apr 16-Nov 14), camper-services center with showers, flush toilets and laundry. Sites 1-23, 62, 63 stay open in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: John Martin Reservoir State Park (Lake Hasty)
note: Campground below the John Martin dam beside small Lake Hasty (not on the main reservoir); 109 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric (1 full hookup), 50 pull-throughs, showers, dump, laundry, fish-cleaning station. Reservation required. ~51 sites stay open in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Arkansas Headwaters Recreation Area (Hecla Junction)
note: CPW-run riverside campground/rafting put-in on the Arkansas River ~10 mi N of Salida; 23 sites on an open bench, vault toilets, no potable water, reached by a steep narrow dirt access road (~12% grade). Reservable Apr-Oct, FCFS in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Arkansas Headwaters Recreation Area (Rincon)
note: CPW-run campground along US-50 on the Arkansas River between Swissvale and Howard; 8 level gravel drive-in sites with a boat launch, vault toilets, no potable water. Adjacent-highway road noise is the main drawback. Reservable Apr-Oct, FCFS in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 0*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Arkansas Headwaters Recreation Area (Ruby Mountain)
note: CPW-run campground at the north entrance to Browns Canyon NM, 6 mi S of Buena Vista on CR-301; ~22 multi-level sites and a boat ramp on the Arkansas River, vault toilets, no running water. Single-lane gravel access narrows near the loop (fine for a 23-ft rig). Reservable Apr-Oct, FCFS in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Arkansas Headwaters Recreation Area (Railroad Bridge)
note: CPW-run campground on the Arkansas River a few miles N of Buena Vista by the historic railroad bridge; ~21 drive-in sites (each with tent pad, grill, table) plus riverside walk-in tent sites, vault toilets, no potable water. Avoid GPS routing large rigs through low-clearance tunnels. Reservable Apr-Oct. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 3*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Clear Creek Reservoir State Wildlife Area
note: CPW State Wildlife Area on Clear Creek Reservoir, ~13 mi N of Buena Vista; ~20 designated drive-in sites with tables and fire rings, vault toilet, boat ramp, reached by ~2 mi of maintained gravel off US-24. Free FCFS, but everyone 16+ must carry a CPW SWA pass or a current Colorado hunting/fishing license to be on the property. RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). Open flats at the Clear Creek inlet; some drive-in RV sites sit right along Clear Creek (creekside), reservoir a short walk east. --Claude

name: San Luis Lakes State Wildlife Area (Mosca)
note: Former San Luis Lakes State Park, now a CPW State Wildlife Area ~15 mi from Great Sand Dunes NP; the Mosca campground still operates as drive-in camping with 51 sites and 50-amp electric (no water/sewer hookups, no showers), seasonal ~Apr-Oct. The lake itself is currently dry. FCFS, but everyone 16+ must carry a CPW SWA pass or current Colorado hunting/fishing license. --Claude

name: Stagecoach State Park (Pinnacle)
note: Largest loop at Stagecoach Reservoir S of Steamboat Springs; 36 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric, dump station and showers in the park. The only Stagecoach loop kept open (and reservable) in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Stagecoach State Park (Junction City)
note: Loop at Stagecoach Reservoir; ~27 sites with 30-amp electric, 8 pull-through, RVs to ~40 ft, dump and showers in the park. Open May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Lakeside arc of the Junction City loop has RV pads on the open reservoir shore/drawdown apron (sites near the water incl. ~10, 62); other sites set back. --Claude

name: Stagecoach State Park (McKindley)
note: Small primitive non-electric loop (~9 sites) centrally located at Stagecoach Reservoir, RVs to ~40 ft; dump and showers elsewhere in the park. Open May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Stagecoach State Park (Harding Spur)
note: Non-electric loop (~16 back-in sites, RVs to ~30 ft) near the Haybro day-use area at Stagecoach Reservoir, sites parallel to the shoreline. Open May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Primitive inlet/cove sites on the open shore (back-in, RVs to ~30 ft, no hookups); reviewers dock boats at their site. --Claude

name: Steamboat Lake State Park (Sunrise Vista)
note: Large campground ~1/4 mi from Steamboat Lake at ~8,000 ft near Clark; 113 single-family sites, sites 76-113 with 30/50-amp electric, potable water and vault toilets in the loop, showers and marina ~1 mi away, dump station. RVs to ~30 ft. Electric loop open in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Steamboat Lake State Park (Dutch Hill)
note: Campground at Steamboat Lake; ~74 sites including the Wheeler electric loop (50-amp), coin showers, laundry and dump station on site, RVs to ~30 ft. No full hookups. Open ~May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Pearl Lake State Park
note: Quiet wakeless-lake campground near Clark at ~8,000 ft; 36 sites (lakeside lower loop + upper loop) plus 2 yurts, no hookups, no dump station, no showers, vault/flush toilets and water hydrants. RVs to ~30 ft. Open ~late May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Yampa River State Park (Headquarters)
note: Developed campground on the Yampa River off US-40 near Hayden; 35 RV sites with 20/30/50-amp electric plus tent, tipi and group sites, dump station and water at the entrance, full camper-services building (showers, flush toilets, laundry). Open ~May-Oct. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Elkhead Reservoir State Park (Pronghorn)
note: Newer campground at Elkhead Reservoir E of Craig; 30 sites with 20/30/50-amp electric, hardened gravel pads, shade shelters and fire pits. No on-site water/sewer (nearest dump at Yampa River HQ). Open ~May-Sep; shares the Yampa River SP office. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 0*/$ (auto 6/2026). Pronghorn outer-loop sites sit on the open bare-shore apron of the reservoir peninsula (drawdown exposes a wide tan band; high-water/beach edge is at the sites). Treeless; shade shelters provided. --Claude

name: Elkhead Reservoir State Park (Bears Ears)
note: Standard non-electric campground (16 sites) near the dam at Elkhead Reservoir; hardened gravel pads, shade shelters, fire pits, no hookups (dump and water at Yampa River HQ). Open ~May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 0*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: State Forest State Park (Bockman)
note: Large loop at ~9,280 ft about 1 mi N of North Michigan Reservoir in State Forest SP (North Park); 52 sites, 13 pull-through, some fitting large rigs, no hookups, vault toilets, hand-pump/spigot water, dump station in park, horse corrals on site. Snow-dependent, ~June-mid-Oct. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: State Forest State Park (North Michigan)
note: Campground on the shores of North Michigan Reservoir at ~9,000 ft; ~48 sites on the north and south sides, south side has shoreline sites with pull-throughs for larger RVs, no hookups, flush toilets/water on the north side, dump in park. Snow-dependent, ~late May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: State Forest State Park (Ranger Lakes)
note: The only electric loop in State Forest SP; 32 sites with 30/50-amp electric off Hwy 14 near Cameron Pass at ~9,240 ft, vault toilets, water, dump in park, short walk to Ranger Lakes fishing. Snow-dependent, ~late May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Highline Lake State Park
note: Campground at Highline Lake NW of Grand Junction near Loma; 56 sites, most with electric, dump station, camper-services building (flush toilets, free hot showers, laundry) open year-round. Reservation required. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: James M. Robb Colorado River State Park (Island Acres)
note: Riverside campground in De Beque Canyon between I-70 and the Colorado River around two stocked ponds (Palisade); 80 sites including full-hookup (some 50-amp), 30-amp electric and basic, showers, laundry, dump. Full-hookup sites close Nov-Mar (winter = electric/tent only). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: James M. Robb Colorado River State Park (Fruita)
note: Campground wrapping Red Rocks Lake within walking distance of downtown Fruita; 57 sites - 22 full-hookup (50-amp), 22 electric (30-amp), plus walk-in tent - showers, laundry, dump. Full-hookup sites close Nov-Mar. Colorado River and trail access. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Vega State Park (Early Settlers)
note: The only Vega Reservoir loop with hookups (~8,000 ft, Grand Mesa); 34 pull-through/back-in sites with electric and water, flush toilets, coin showers, dump station, boat ramp on the SW side. Spring-fall (no winter camping). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Vega State Park (Aspen Grove)
note: Basic loop on the E/NE arm of Vega Reservoir; 28 pull-through/back-in sites, central water pumps, vault toilets, no hookups. Spring-fall (no winter camping). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 0*/$ (auto 6/2026). NE-arm loop overlooks Vega Reservoir; sites 201/202/204/205 have reservoir views, only 205 has lake access, shoreline is marshy (no beach) - lakeview. --Claude

name: Vega State Park (Oak Point)
note: Basic loop on the north shore of Vega Reservoir; ~32 drive-in sites, central water pumps, vault toilets, no hookups (10 sites cap near 20 ft, the rest fit 25 ft+). Spring-fall. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Rifle Gap State Park (Cottonwood & Cedar)
note: Adjacent north-shore electric loops at Rifle Gap Reservoir: Cottonwood (7 sites, incl. 1 full-hookup ADA) and Cedar (14 all-electric, on a rise), water, flush toilets, coin showers. Open year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Cottonwood loop (electric sites incl. 6-8) sits flat on the reservoir beach/shoreline apron — the wide sand gap is drawdown, not elevation; lakefront by beach-apron rule. Cedar loop is on a rise above (lakeview). --Claude

name: Rifle Gap State Park (Lakeview)
note: Full-hookup loop at Rifle Gap Reservoir; 32 paved sites with 30/50-amp electric, water and sewer, large pull-throughs (55-95 ft), water, flush toilets, coin showers. Sits on higher ground N of the reservoir; closes in winter (the only Rifle Gap loop that does). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Lakeview loop sits atop the north mesa with an open overlook of Rifle Gap Reservoir (sites set back/above the water; swim beach is on the west side). --Claude

name: Rifle Gap State Park (Sage & Pinon)
note: No-hookup loops at Rifle Gap Reservoir: Sage (14 sites, generous pull-throughs) and Pinon (5 sites), water hydrants, vault toilets, on higher ground set back from the shore. Open year-round (one Pinon site caps at 20 ft). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Rifle Falls State Park
note: Small campground along East Rifle Creek below the triple waterfall N of Rifle; 13 drive-in electric sites (1-13, 30/50-amp), 11 fitting RVs 29-49 ft, restrooms, water hydrants. No on-site dump (nearest is at Rifle Gap SP). Separate walk-in tent sites 14-20 excluded. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). --Claude

name: Sylvan Lake State Park (Elk Run)
note: Campground set back NW of 42-acre Sylvan Lake along West Brush Creek at ~8,500 ft S of Eagle; 34 sites (16 pull-through), no hookups (the park has none), dump station, water spigots, vault toilets year-round (showers/flush summer only). Closed Nov-Apr. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). --Claude

name: Sylvan Lake State Park (Fisherman's Paradise)
note: Lakeshore loop at Sylvan Lake (~8,500 ft); ~10 sites along the NW shore, no hookups, dump station, water spigots, vault toilets year-round. This is the park's winter-camping loop (open year-round, unlike Elk Run). Reservable online (Camp Colorado). Fisherman's Paradise loop sits directly on the NW shore at the water's edge (sites ~35-46); small alpine lake, no drawdown — lakefront, not set back. --Claude

name: Ridgway State Park (Dakota Terraces)
note: Campground above Ridgway Reservoir with San Juan views; 76 sites (loops A/B/C) with 30-amp electric and shared fill-and-go water, RV dump station, ADA sites. Open year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Ridgway State Park (Elk Ridge)
note: Campground above Ridgway Reservoir; 98 drive-in sites (loops D/E) with 30-amp electric and shared water plus 10 scenic walk-in tent sites (excluded), ADA sites, shared dump with Dakota Terraces. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Ridgway State Park (Pa-Co-Chu-Puk)
note: Premium full-hookup loop on the Uncompahgre River below the Ridgway dam (gold-medal trout water); 81 sites with 50-amp electric, individual water and sewer, year-round dump station, plus 15 walk-in tent sites (excluded). Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Crawford State Park (Clear Fork)
note: Campground at Crawford Reservoir in the West Elk country S of town; 20 reservable sites (15 full-hookup + 5 tent) with water views, flush and vault toilets, showers, RVs to ~40 ft. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). Perimeter loop sites 46-49 (E shore) and 56-61 (N/NW shore) sit on the open treeless peninsula apron at the full-pool waterline (lakefront); inner SW oval (62-66) set back. Drawdown can leave a wide sand band between pad and waterline. --Claude

name: Crawford State Park (Iron Creek)
note: Campground near the boat ramp at Crawford Reservoir; 45 single-family sites, 38 with electric, potable water at each site, no sewer, accommodates tents/trailers/RVs. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Paonia State Park
note: Two small campgrounds (Spruce, wooded by a stream; Hawsapple, near the boat ramp) at Paonia Reservoir off CO-133; ~13 sites, up to 7 electric at Hawsapple per CPW, vault toilets, RVs to ~35 ft. No drinking water in the park - bring your own. Open ~May-Sep. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). --Claude

name: Mancos State Park
note: Ponderosa-pine campground beside Jackson Gulch Reservoir N of Mancos (Mesa Verde country); ~23 RV-capable sites to ~40 ft, no electric hookups anywhere in the park, pull-through and back-in sites. Upper loop stays open in winter, lower loop seasonal. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Navajo State Park (Rosa)
note: Full-hookup campground near the marina on Navajo Reservoir at Arboles; 39 sites with electric, water and sewer, pull-through and back-in to 45-55 ft, camper-services building with showers and coin laundry. Year-round. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Navajo State Park (Carracas)
note: Electric campground above Navajo Reservoir; 41 sites with electric (no water/sewer at site), 28 pull-throughs to ~55 ft, camper-services building, dump station. Closes in winter. Reservable online (Camp Colorado). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Glacier Basin Campground

name: Morefield Campground

RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 17 sites, sites to ~40 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Collegiate Peaks Campground
note: Chalk Creek/Cottonwood (Salida RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 56 sites, sites to ~99 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Spruce Grove Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 26 sites, sites to ~36 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Round Mountain Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 15 sites, sites to ~30 ft. Reservable online. RV Life unrated/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Blue Mountain Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 20 sites, sites to ~35 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Riverside Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 18 sites, sites to ~90 ft. Reservable online. RV Life listed (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Happy Meadows Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 7 sites, sites to ~40 ft. Reservable online. RV Life listed (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Springer Gulch Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 14 sites, sites to ~28 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cove Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 4 sites, sites to ~34 ft. Reservable online. RV Life unrated/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lone Rock Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 17 sites, sites to ~40 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 3.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Spillway Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 22 sites, sites to ~45 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Painted Rocks Campground
note: Eleven Mile/Lake George (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 18 sites, sites to ~59 ft. Reservable online. RV Life unrated/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Colorado Campground
note: Eleven Mile/Lake George (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 79 sites incl. electric sites, sites to ~100 ft. Reservable online. RV Life unrated/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Kelsey Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 17 sites, sites to ~60 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 3.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: South Meadows Campground
note: Eleven Mile/Lake George (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 63 sites, sites to ~84 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Buffalo Campground
note: South Park/Tarryall (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 37 sites, sites to ~40 ft. Reservable online. RV Life unrated/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Meadow Ridge Campground
note: Rampart Range (Pikes Peak RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 18 sites, sites to ~99 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Thunder Ridge Campground
note: Rampart Range (Pikes Peak RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 20 sites, sites to ~60 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Geneva Park Campground
note: Guanella Pass S (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 26 sites, sites to ~32 ft. Reservable online. RV Life unrated/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Burning Bear Campground
note: Guanella Pass S (South Platte RD) (Pike-San Isabel NF); 13 sites, sites to ~50 ft. Reservable online. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Angel of Shavano Campground
note: 20 sites, vault toilet, hand-pump water, ~9,200 ft, on gravel CR 240; Salida RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Flat Rocks Campground
note: 19 sites, vault toilets, potable water in season, ~8,170 ft on Rampart Range Rd near Devils Head; closed in winter. FCFS. --Claude

name: Halfmoon East Campground
note: 5 sites on Halfmoon Creek, vault toilet, no water, ~9,900 ft; lower FSR 110 (2WD to the CG); Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Lake Creek Campground (Westcliffe)
note: 11 sites (several creekside), vault toilet, no potable water, ~8,230 ft on gravel CR 198; San Carlos RD near Westcliffe. FCFS. --Claude

name: Tabor Campground
note: 15 sites set back in lodgepole on the paved Turquoise Lake loop, vault toilet, no water in 2026, ~9,900 ft; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Weston Pass Campground
note: 14 sites, vault toilet, no water (pack out), 10,200 ft; east approach (CR 5/22 from Fairplay) 2WD-passable; South Park RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Parry Peak Campground
note: 25 sites (gravel pads), vault toilets, no potable water, ~9,430 ft on Lake Creek near Twin Lakes; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Halfmoon West Campground
note: 17 sites on Halfmoon Creek, vault toilets, ~9,900 ft; FSR 110 2WD to the CG (4WD section is beyond); Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Iron City Campground
note: 15 sites in Chalk Creek Canyon, vault toilet, ~10,000 ft, walkable to the St. Elmo ghost town; CR 162 paved-then-gravel; Salida RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Dexter Campground
note: 8 large-RV + 11 tent sites at the Dexter Point boat ramp on Twin Lakes off paved Hwy 82, vault toilets, no water; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Matchless Campground
note: 20 sites on the paved Turquoise Lake loop by the boat ramp, vault toilet, no water in 2026, ~9,900 ft; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Hayden Creek Campground
note: 11 sites, vault toilets, no potable water, on gravel Hayden Creek Rd (FR-6) off US-50 at Coaldale; base of the Sangres; San Carlos RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Goose Creek Campground
note: 10 sites, vault toilets, hand-pump water, on graded gravel FR-211 (Matukat Rd) near the Lost Creek Wilderness; South Platte RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Selkirk Campground
note: 15 sites, vault toilets, no water (pack out), 10,500 ft near Como off Boreas Pass Rd (narrow/bumpy 2WD); South Park RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Cottonwood Lake Campground
note: 25 sites, vault toilets, no potable water, ~9,600 ft on gravel CR-344 SW of Buena Vista; lake/creek trout fishing; Salida RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Elbert Creek Campground
note: 17 sites at ~10,000 ft by the Mt. Elbert & Mt. Massive trailheads on graded Halfmoon Rd (FR-110, 2WD to the CG); vault toilets; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Meridian Campground
note: 18 sites with parking spurs, vault toilets, hand-pump water, ~9,000 ft on gravel Park CR-43 near Bailey/Harris Park; South Platte RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Springdale Campground
note: 13 sites, vault toilets, no potable water, ~9,200 ft on gravel Rampart Range Rd near Rampart Reservoir E of Woodland Park; Pikes Peak RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Twin Peaks Campground
note: 35 sites at 9,600 ft above Lake Creek W of Twin Lakes on paved CO-82 (below the 35-ft limit), vault toilets, no water; Leadville RD. FCFS. --Claude

name: Jumbo Campground
note: USFS campground at ~9,800 ft in the Mesa Lakes area on the north slope of Grand Mesa (Hwy 65, paved access). About 26 single/double sites; rec.gov lists RVs/trailers from 20 up to ~50+ ft, most sites with electric hookups, vault toilets, drinking water. Mix of reservable (online) and first-come-first-served (booked in person). Open roughly late June to late September, weather permitting. --Claude

name: Matterhorn Campground
note: USFS campground at ~9,500 ft along Hwy 145 (paved access) about 7 mi south of Telluride. 28 single-family sites, max spur ~45 ft; some sites have full hookups, others non-electric. Flush and vault toilets, showers, and drinking water. Mostly reservable online with some first-come-first-served sites booked in person. Open summer through early fall, weather permitting. --Claude

name: Sunshine Campground
note: Small single-loop, paved USFS campground at ~9,500 ft on Hwy 145 about 8 mi south of Telluride. 15 sites with max spur ~35 ft; metal tables and fire grates, vault toilets, hand-pump drinking water, no hookups. First-come-first-served only (pay on-site via the rec.gov app; cell service can be limited). --Claude

name: Rosy Lane Campground
note: USFS campground at ~8,600 ft in Taylor River Canyon (paved access off CR 742), about 17 mi NE of Gunnison. 20 single-family sites, RVs to ~30 ft; one site (008) has 15-amp electric, the rest non-electric. Vault toilets and potable water. River reached by a trail from the campground (sites are set back, not on the bank). Mix of reservable online and first-come-first-served sites booked in person. --Claude

name: Lake Irwin Campground
note: USFS campground at ~10,300 ft on the west shore of Lake Irwin, about 9 mi from Crested Butte via a gravel/dirt road that is dusty and washboarded the last 2 miles but passable by a normal RV (campers report 27-34 ft rigs fitting). ~25-32 non-electric sites with gravel spurs, tables, fire rings; 3 vault toilets, no potable water (bring your own). Mix of reservable online and first-come-first-served. Open roughly mid-June to early October. --Claude

name: Amphitheater Campground
note: USFS campground at ~8,400 ft on a steep hillside about 2 mi south of Ouray off Hwy 550. 35 sites total; designated RV sites (roughly 3-15) cap at ~35 ft, but the access road and loops are steep with tight turns and narrow, generally small sites - fine for a 23-ft rig, not for large motorhomes or long trailers. Vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Mix of reservable online and first-come-first-served sites booked in person. --Claude

name: Woods Lake Campground
note: USFS campground at ~9,400 ft reached via Fall Creek Rd (FR 618), a ~7-8 mi dirt/washboard road that is dusty with some rough rocks but passable by a normal RV. 41 sites across 3 loops including some pull-throughs; vault toilets and potable water, no hookups. First-come-first-served only. Woods Lake is a short walk from the sites (sites are set back from the shore). Open roughly late spring to early fall. --Claude

name: Deer Lakes Campground
note: Small USFS campground at ~10,400 ft near Lake City, reached via FR 788 then FR 458 (dirt forest roads, a bit tight to maneuver but passable by ~30-ft rigs per campers). 12 sites, longest reported vehicle 30 ft; vault toilets, no hookups. Lower sites overlook the small Deer Lakes. First-come-first-served (pay on-site via the rec.gov app). Open roughly June through September. --Claude

name: Dinner Station Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground 32 miles up the Taylor River canyon NE of Gunnison, ~9,000 ft. 22 single sites, vault toilets, potable water, no hookups; reservable on recreation.gov. Late spring to early fall, ~$18/night. Some sites accommodate larger rigs; improved dirt access road suitable for a 23-ft RV. --Claude

name: Cottonwood Lake Campground (Grand Mesa)
note: Grand Mesa NF (GMUG) campground on the Grand Mesa near Collbran at ~10,100 ft. 36 mostly-shaded sites, 42-ft max spur, vault toilets, NO potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served (scan-and-pay on-site), ~$18/night, open roughly late June to late September. --Claude

name: Cobbett Lake Campground
note: Grand Mesa NF (GMUG) campground along the Grand Mesa Scenic Byway near the visitor center, ~10,300 ft. 20 shaded standard non-electric sites, 42-ft max spur, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Mix of reservable (recreation.gov) and in-person FCFS sites; ~$22/night. Cobbett Lake allows non-motorized boats. --Claude

name: Silver Jack Campground
note: Uncompahgre NF (GMUG, Ouray RD) campground near Silver Jack Reservoir, ~18 miles up Cimarron Rd (CR 858) from US-50, ~8,900 ft. 60 heavily-wooded sites in three loops, vehicles up to 45 ft, vault toilets, drinking water; some sites have electric. Mix of reservable and FCFS via recreation.gov; ~$20 non-electric / $30 electric. --Claude

name: Soap Creek Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground in the Soap Creek drainage north of Blue Mesa Reservoir, reached via FR 721/824 off CO-92, ~7,700 ft. 21 sites, vault toilets, potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served (scan-and-pay), ~$18/night. Dirt access road is passable to RVs when dry but can become impassable after heavy rain. --Claude

name: Slumgullion Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground near Slumgullion Pass, ~9 miles south of Lake City off CO-149 via 0.1 mi of FR 788 (dirt), ~11,300 ft. 21 sites, vehicles up to 30 ft, vault toilets, potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served. High-elevation, short summer season. --Claude

name: Almont Campground
note: Gunnison NF (GMUG) campground 1 mile SW of Almont on CO-135 along the Gunnison River, ~8,000 ft. 10 primitive sites but 5 are tent-only; the ~5 RV-capable sites are small and rigs over ~35 ft may struggle in the turnaround, so a 23-ft EKKO fits the larger ones. 2 vault toilets, hand-pump potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served, ~$18/night, open ~late May to early October. --Claude

name: Cement Creek Campground
note: GMUG (Gunnison RD) campground ~3.8 mi up Cement Creek Rd (FR 740, fairly wide dirt with some washboard) off CO-135 near Crested Butte South. 13 FCFS sites, no hookups, pit toilets, no potable water (creek only); $18/night. Max RV ~28 ft but loop is narrow with tight turns, so a 23-ft rig is comfortable while 30+ ft is discouraged. Sites 3-9 sit along the creek. Open ~May 23-Oct 1, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Dorchester Campground
note: GMUG (Gunnison RD) campground at ~9,800 ft at the upper end of Taylor Park, reached via the maintained dirt Taylor Canyon Rd / FR 742 (CR 742) past Taylor Park Reservoir - long (~39 mi from Almont) but a graded improved-dirt road passable by normal vehicles, not 4WD-only. 10 FCFS sites with dirt/gravel pads (max RV ~35 ft), vault toilets, no potable water, free. Popular OHV/fishing area. Open ~late May-Oct 1, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Buckeye Recreation Area (Pioneer Campground)
note: Manti-La Sal NF (Moab RD) campground at Buckeye Reservoir, ~7,600 ft, ~13 mi of winding gravel road (FR 0371) from Paradox. Pioneer Campground on the reservoir's north side has 30 reservable single-family sites with graveled spurs, vault toilets, no potable water; $15/night, plus 18 FCFS dispersed sites around the lake. Reservable online. Open ~mid-May to September. Reservable Pioneer sites sit back from the water (dispersed sites have the across-water views). --Claude

name: Erickson Springs Campground
note: GMUG (Gunnison NF, Paonia RD) campground on Anthracite Creek off Kebler Pass Rd (CR 12). 18 primitive sites in spruce/fir forest, RVs up to 35 ft, some pull-throughs; vault toilets, no potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served; $14/night. Elevation ~2,065 m (6,780 ft); late spring to early fall. --Claude

name: Gold Creek Campground
note: Small, remote GMUG (Gunnison NF) campground 7 mi north of Ohio City on FR 771. 6 dirt-pad sites, 25 ft max length (small rigs only - a 23-ft Class C fits the larger spots); vault toilet, no potable water, no hookups. Free, first-come, first-served. Access is well-maintained dirt but narrows to 1-1.5 lanes past the gold mines; elevation ~3,050 m (10,000 ft), open ~late May-Sept. --Claude

name: Gothic Campground
note: GMUG designated camping area on Gothic Rd (NFSR 317) past Gothic townsite above Mt. Crested Butte; sites G1-G14, vault toilet, no water, picnic tables, $12/night, first-come first-served (claim in person). Only the lower sites are RV-suitable - the road beyond about site 4 turns rough/narrow and high-clearance 4x4 territory, so a 23-ft rig should stay to the first few sites. Elevation ~2,937 m (9,640 ft); late May-Sept, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Iron Springs Campground (Uncompahgre Plateau)
note: GMUG (Uncompahgre NF, Ouray RD) campground on the Uncompahgre Plateau near Divide Rd along the Rimrocker Trail. 8 sites for tents and small/medium trailers (campers report rigs up to ~32 ft fitting), vault toilet, no potable water, no hookups. First-come, first-served; $16/night. Access is via wide, smoothly graded gravel forest road; elevation ~2,919 m (9,580 ft), late spring to early fall. --Claude

name: Lodgepole Campground (Taylor River)
note: GMUG (Gunnison NF) campground in Taylor River Canyon, 12 mi up Taylor Canyon Rd (CR 742) from Almont. 16 sites (max ~30 ft), drinking water (hand pump) and vault toilets, no hookups; $18/night, mix of reservable (recreation.gov) and first-come first-served sites. Sits across the road from the Taylor River - good trout fishing/rafting access but sites are not on the bank. Elevation ~2,694 m (8,840 ft); open ~May 23-Oct 1. --Claude

name: Junction Creek Campground
note: USFS campground about 5 mi NW of Durango near the Colorado Trailhead, set a quarter-mile above Junction Creek. ~34 sites; 14 have electric hookups and some accommodate RVs up to ~60 ft. Vault toilets and drinking water; open roughly May-September. Mostly reservable online with some first-come, first-served sites. --Claude

name: McPhee Campground
note: Large USFS campground on a mesa about 500 ft above McPhee Reservoir near Dolores, ~7,400 ft. 71 sites on two paved loops, 24 with electric hookups; vault toilets and drinking water. Boat ramp and fish-cleaning station in the complex. Open roughly May-September; ~50 sites reservable online plus walk-ins. --Claude

name: Mavreeso Campground
note: USFS campground along the West Dolores River on West Dolores Rd (FR 535), ~20 mi NE of Dolores. ~18-19 sites, several right along the river; about 7 have electric hookups and there are RV pull-throughs (sites max ~30-42 ft). Vault toilets and drinking water; open roughly mid-May to September. Mix of reservable and first-come sites. --Claude

name: West Fork Campground
note: Wooded USFS campground near the West Fork of the San Juan River off US-160 between Pagosa Springs and Wolf Creek Pass (FR 648). 28 sites, max RV ~35 ft; some sites are near the river but reviews report no sites directly on the bank. Vault toilets and drinking water; open mid-May through September. 15 reservable sites plus first-come, first-served. Access road is gravel with some rough patches but passable for a 23-ft rig. --Claude

name: Target Tree Campground
note: USFS campground in pinon-juniper/ponderosa about 7 mi east of Mancos off US-160, ~7,640 ft. ~28-30 sites, max RV ~45 ft with a couple of pull-throughs; composting toilets and drinking water (trucked in, no RV fill). No water body on site. Open roughly May-September; reservable online. Well-maintained access road suitable for a 23-ft RV. --Claude

name: Williams Creek Campground
note: USFS campground on Williams Creek about 23 mi N of Pagosa Springs via Piedra Rd (FR 600/631/640), ~1 mi below Williams Creek Reservoir. ~61 sites across several loops, max RV ~45 ft; some sites sit on the creek bank and a few have water/sewer hookups. Vault toilets and drinking water; no dump on site. Open May-September; mix of reservable and first-come sites. Washboard forest-road access. --Claude

name: Ute Campground
note: First-come, first-served USFS campground on a gentle south-facing slope just off US-160 across from Chimney Rock, ~15 mi W of Pagosa Springs. 26 sites (max RV ~35 ft; many sites on a slope, larger rigs limited); potable water and vault toilets. No water body on site. Open May-September; not reservable online. --Claude

name: Teal Campground
note: First-come, first-served USFS campground on FR 640 overlooking Williams Creek Reservoir, ~1 mi N of Williams Creek Campground. 16 sites set above the water (short walk to shore); open and grassy with some ponderosa shade. Max RV ~35 ft but only a few sites suit larger rigs-best for a 23-ft rig and tight; potable water and vault toilets. Open May-September; not reservable online. --Claude

name: West Dolores Campground
note: USFS campground on the West Dolores River, ~21 mi north of Dolores via Hwy 145 and FR 535 (the campground is reached before the steep/narrow upper-road section, so it's fine for a 23-ft rig; max RV 35 ft, some sites larger). 18 sites, 7 with electric, 10 reservable plus FCFS overflow. Potable water, composting toilet, no dump. Open roughly May-Sep; reservable online. --Claude

name: Golden Horn Designated Camping Area
note: Free designated dispersed camping area in San Juan NF along FR 585 (South Mineral Creek Rd), ~5 mi from Silverton off US-550, ~9,600 ft. Bumpy but all-vehicle-accessible road; sites large enough for most rigs including a 23-ft RV. Vault toilets and fire rings, no water/hookups; FCFS, 14-day limit, not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: Little Molas Lake Campground
note: Free FCFS USFS campground at ~10,900 ft beside Little Molas Lake, reached by ~1 mi of dirt road (FR 584) off US-550 just north of Molas Pass; road is washboarded but 2WD/passenger-car passable. 10 sites, 5 of which fit RVs. Pit toilets, no water, no hookups; 14-day limit, not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: House Creek Campground
note: USFS campground above McPhee Reservoir near Dolores, set in a gently sloping meadow near the high-water line (closest sites ~300 ft from water; the reservoir draws down well below the campground, so pads are set back). 22 renovated sites in several loops, 12 reservable and 12 with electric, plus FCFS sites; vault toilets, drinking water, boat ramp on site. Open roughly May-Sep; reservable online. --Claude

name: South Mineral Campground
note: USFS campground at ~9,800 ft along South Mineral Creek, reached via ~4 mi of FR 585 off US-550 west of Silverton; the road to the campground is 2WD-drivable (it only turns 4WD beyond the campground), fine for a 23-ft rig. 26 mostly level FCFS sites (no reservations), potable water, vault toilets, no hookups. Trailhead for the Ice Lake Basin hike is across the road. --Claude

name: Pine Point Campground
note: USFS campground on the shore of Vallecito Reservoir, ~13 mi north of Bayfield via CR 501/501A; midway up the lakeshore with several lakefront sites. 28 sites (about half reservable, ~13 FCFS), max RV length around 30-45 ft depending on site; vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Open roughly May-Sep; reservable online. --Claude

name: Bradfield Campground
note: San Juan NF (Dolores RD), in the Dolores River Canyon below McPhee Dam ~30 mi NW of Dolores via Hwy 184/US 491/FR 505. 22 sites, large and open, FCFS only (not on recreation.gov). Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables, fire grates; limited/no services Sept-May. Boat ramp busy during the short Dolores rafting release (around Memorial Day). No hookups; sites set back from the brushy river bank. --Claude

name: Burro Bridge Campground
note: San Juan NF (Dolores RD), on a sloping hillside above the West Dolores River ~2 mi NE of Dunton. 14 sites, FCFS only, max RV 35 ft. Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables, fire grates; two sites have horse corrals. Access via West Dolores Rd (FR 535) gravel; approach from the Dolores/Hwy 145 side - the steep, narrow last 6 miles 'not recommended for trailers' are the upper segment toward Rico, beyond the campground. Mid-May through Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Cimarrona Campground
note: San Juan NF (Pagosa RD), beside Cimarrona Creek near the head of the road above Williams Creek Reservoir, ~27 mi NW of Pagosa Springs on FR 640. 21 sites, FCFS only; a few sites fit large RVs (max 35 ft). Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables, fire grates. Adjacent Cimarrona Trailhead; short walk to creek/reservoir fishing. Open roughly May-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Ferris Canyon Campground
note: San Juan NF (Dolores RD), on the Lower Dolores River ~3.5 mi below McPhee Dam on FR 504, ~6,500 ft. Small primitive barrier-free CG, 7 graveled easy-access sites, FCFS; max RV ~30 ft. Popular with catch-and-release fly fishers. No potable water - bring your own; no hookups. Lightly used. --Claude

name: Florida Campground
note: San Juan NF (Columbine RD), along the Florida ('Floor-ee-da') River above Lemon Reservoir, reached via CR 240/243. 20 shaded individual sites, all FCFS; a reservable group site exists. Max RV 25 ft, only a few short pull-thru/back-in sites take larger rigs (not big-rig friendly); a 23-ft fits. Vault toilets, potable water, picnic tables. Open roughly May-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Graham Creek Campground
note: San Juan NF (Columbine RD) on the east side of Vallecito Reservoir, reached via CR 240/CR 501/FR 603. 25 sites (15 reservable on recreation.gov + 10 FCFS), max RV 35 ft; entrance/exit roads are steep but pads take large rigs. Hillside sites overlook the lake ~100 yd above the high-water line. Drinking water, vault toilets, trash. Mid-May to mid-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Kroeger Campground
note: San Juan NF (Columbine RD) in La Plata Canyon near the La Plata River, ~5 mi up CR 124 from Hesperus (US 160). 10 shaded sites, FCFS, max RV 25 ft - most sites suit smaller rigs but at least one long-RV site and one pull-thru exist, so a 23-ft fits. The campground is reached by 2WD gravel; CR 124 turns to 4WD only farther up-canyon past the CG. Potable water, vault toilet, picnic tables, host; pack out trash, bear-safe storage required. Late May-Sept. No hookups. --Claude

name: Mogote Campground
note: USFS campground on the Conejos River off Hwy 17, ~13 mi W of Antonito; 40 sites in Upper and Lower loops (21 pull-through), no hookups, vault toilets, drinking-water hydrants. Per-site max length is 25 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits but larger rigs won't. Open Memorial Day weekend through the second Monday of September; reservable on recreation.gov with some first-come, first-served sites. Lower loop sits along the river. --Claude

name: Lower Beaver Creek Campground
note: USFS campground in the Beaver Creek drainage, 1 mi south of South Fork then 4.5 mi south on gravel Forest Rd 360. 18 sites (15 dead-end spurs, 3 pull-through), max spur 45 ft though not all sites fit that. No hookups, vault toilets, seasonal hand-pump drinking water. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day (5 sites stay open year-round, no services). FCFS, not reservable; pay at site. --Claude

name: Cross Creek Campground
note: Small USFS campground straddling Cross Creek just above the Beaver Reservoir inlet, ~6.4 mi up Beaver Creek Rd (FR 20, gravel after 4 mi) from US 160. 8 sites, max spur 25 ft and not all sites fit that length. No hookups, vault toilet, no drinking water. Concessionaire season Memorial Day-Labor Day; accessible year-round if snow allows. FCFS, not reservable. --Claude

name: Highway Springs Campground
note: USFS campground in two loops just off US 160, 4.1 mi west of South Fork toward Wolf Creek Pass. 13 sites (3 pull-through) with adequate RV turnaround/parking; no posted max length, no hookups, 2 vault toilets, no drinking water. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day. FCFS, not reservable; pay by envelope. --Claude

name: Bristol Head Campground
note: USFS campground ~21 mi from Creede off Hwy 149 (Silver Thread Scenic Byway) via a short maintained gravel road. 15 sites (11 pull-through), site lengths 35-55 ft so big rigs fit easily. Vault toilets, potable water, no hookups. Sits in a meadow on a bench above South Clear Creek. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day, 14-day limit. FCFS, not reservable (not on recreation.gov). --Claude

name: Buffalo Pass Campground
note: 19 large, well-spaced sites (4 pull-through) in a single loop in a ponderosa grove/grass meadow at ~9,100 ft; max vehicle 30 ft (a 32-ft fifth wheel has fit per a review). Reached via 27.6 mi west on Hwy 114 from Saguache then 1.7 mi gravel road. Vault toilets, no potable water, no hookups. FCFS, not reservable; $10/night. Open mid-May to mid-November. Sites 16-18 on a no-turnaround dead-end spur. --Claude

name: North Crestone Creek Campground
note: 13 FCFS sites (one pull-through) strung 0.7 mi up the canyon along North Crestone Creek at ~8,300 ft near Crestone; no posted length limit but described as best for smaller rigs, so a 23-ft Class C should aim for the pull-through. No reservations; $10/night, vault toilets, bear lockers, no potable water. Reached via unpaved county road / Alder St north of Crestone. Open mid-May to mid-November. --Claude

name: Comstock Campground
note: Small, remote 7-site FCFS campground (remodeled 2010) on the South Fork of Rock Creek SW of Monte Vista at ~9,600 ft; dead-end spur sites run 35-40 ft (max vehicle 30 ft) so a 23-ft rig fits, though maneuvering room for large trailers is limited. Reached via ~12 mi of County Road R then ~6 mi of graded dirt Forest Road 28 (passenger-car passable, 4x4 not required). Vault toilet, no water, no posted fee. Thinly reviewed - verify season/road with the Divide Ranger District. --Claude

name: Big Creek Lakes Campground
note: Routt NF campground near Big Creek Lake at ~9,000 ft; 54 sites (24 reservable online, 30 FCFS). Mostly large gravel pull-throughs; site-level max RV length ranges from 20 ft up to 40 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits the larger sites. Vault toilets; NO potable water currently (spigots capped, bring your own). Short season ~early June to mid/late October. Reach via FR 600 (gravel). --Claude

name: Hahns Peak Lake Campground
note: Routt NF campground in spruce/fir forest just uphill of 160-acre Hahns Peak Lake at ~8,500 ft; 26 sites (23 single + 3 double), reservable on recreation.gov. Sites are spacious and wooded, set back from the shore (~5-min walk to the lake, no waterfront sites). Confirmed RV lengths to 25 ft with larger sites longer, fitting a 23-ft rig. Vault toilets, NO potable water on site. Season mid-June to mid-October; non-motorized/electric-motor boats only. --Claude

name: Blacktail Creek Campground
note: Small Routt NF campground of 8 first-come, first-served sites right off paved CO Hwy 134 between Kremmling and Yampa; $20/night, cash or check only (no credit cards), not on recreation.gov. Vault toilet, potable water (June-Sep, weather dependent), no hookups. Open roughly June-October. Open-meadow sites along Blacktail Creek; a reviewer fit a 33-ft rig, so a 23-ft RV is fine (sites are unlevel). --Claude

name: Chapman Reservoir Campground
note: Primitive Routt NF campground at Chapman Reservoir off the Flat Tops Trail Scenic Byway west of Yampa, reached via FR 16/FR 940 (2WD, typically open Memorial Day to mid-October). 12 first-come, first-served sites, $20/night cash or check only; 22-ft max trailer length (a 23-ft Class C fits marginally), vault toilet, camp host on site in summer, NO potable water (bring your own). Elevation ~9,300 ft. --Claude

name: Dry Lake Campground
note: Routt NF campground on lower Buffalo Pass Rd about 8 mi NE of Steamboat Springs; rebuilt and expanded in 2024 from 8 to 23 sites (reopened 2025). For 2026 a majority of sites are reservable on Recreation.gov (~June 15) with the rest first-come, first-served; ~$26 standard/$50 double. Vault toilets, bear boxes, no potable water; open late June into October. The access road is good up to the campground (the rough/4WD stretch is past it toward Buffalo Pass). 'Dry Lake' is a shallow seasonal puddle, not a real water feature. --Claude

name: Freeman Reservoir Campground
note: 18 sites (pull-through + back-in), max RV ~25 ft so a 23-ft rig fits but sites run small; vault toilets, no hookups. FCFS, exact cash/check only, NOT on recreation.gov; open ~mid-June through Oct (weather dependent). Access is 8.5 mi of maintained gravel/dirt off Hwy 13 / Moffat CR 11 (steep/washboard in spots, fine for a careful Class C). Reservoir (non-motorized boating only) and boat ramp/picnic area are a separate facility at the lake; most sites are set back in meadow/forest, one loop has lake views. Forest Service resumes management in June 2026 (formerly run by Moffat County). General campground that also offers horse corrals. --Claude

name: Hidden Lakes Campground
note: Small 9-site campground at ~9,100 ft on a small lake near Buffalo Pass; 3 sites are RV-capable. FCFS, $20/night cash or check; vault toilet, no potable water, no trash (pack it out). Open ~mid-June to mid-Oct (weather). Official FS page lists trailers up to 50 ft and gives no high-clearance warning; a reviewer reached it in a 2WD car. Access: CR 24 west of Hebron to Forest Road 60, then FR 20 south ~6 mi (forest gravel). Satellite shows the loop set back ~30-50 m from the lake in thinned forest, not on the shoreline. --Claude

name: Deer Creek Campground (Bailey)
note: Heavily wooded Pike NF campground at ~9,170 ft near Bailey where Deer Creek and Camp Creek meet. 13 single sites, RVs up to 30 ft; no hookups, no potable water, vault toilets. First-come, first-served only (pay on-site, including Scan & Pay via the Recreation.gov app) - not reservable. --Claude

name: Kenosha Pass Campground
note: Small Pike NF campground at Kenosha Pass on US-285, in lodgepole and aspen near the Colorado Trail. 25 sites with parking spurs 16-30 ft (some fit a 23-ft rig); no hookups, hand-pump water, vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov; season roughly Memorial Day into the fall. --Claude

name: Jefferson Creek Campground
note: Pike NF campground at ~10,100 ft in the Jefferson Lake Recreation Area, on a ridge overlooking Jefferson Creek. 17 sites, parking spurs 25-40 ft but max RV length 25 ft; no hookups, potable water, vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov (unreserved sites go walk-up); $11 one-time rec-area entrance fee; mid-May to mid-October. Access via graded but bumpy dirt CR-37 (passenger-car/RV passable, not 4WD). --Claude

name: Lodgepole Campground (Jefferson)
note: Largest of the Jefferson Lake Recreation Area campgrounds (~9,900 ft) in a valley along Jefferson Creek, 4 miles north of Jefferson. 34 sites, parking spurs 20-40 ft, max RV length 30 ft; no hookups, drinking water, vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov; $11 one-time rec-area entrance fee; mid-May to mid-October. Access via graded dirt CR-37 (bumpy but passenger-car/RV passable, not 4WD); tight maneuvering reported for larger rigs. --Claude

name: Timberline Campground (Kenosha)
note: Pike NF campground at ~9,700 ft near Kenosha Pass, about 7 miles southwest of Grant, in an aspen-forested area within a mile of the Colorado Trail. 30 individual sites in two loops (RVs to ~36 ft reported); no hookups, no potable water (available at nearby Kenosha Pass CG), vault toilets. Reservable on Recreation.gov; access is rutted gravel but passable in a passenger car, not 4WD. --Claude

name: Michigan Creek Campground
note: 12-site drive-in USFS campground about 4 miles NW of Jefferson off CR 35/CR 54 at 10,100 ft, max trailer length 25 ft. FCFS only ($22/night) - no reservations; pay on-site via Recreation.gov Scan and Pay. Vault toilets, tables, fire rings; no potable water or trash service. Open mid-May to mid-October, weather dependent. --Claude

name: Aspen Campground (Jefferson)
note: 12-site single-loop USFS campground in the Jefferson Lake Recreation Area, along Jefferson Creek at 9,900 ft, reached via gravel CR 35/CR 37 from Jefferson. Gravel parking aprons fit two vehicles, max trailer length 25 ft. Reservable on recreation.gov (up to 5 days out; unreserved sites become FCFS walk-ups); $11 rec-area entry fee per stay applies. Vault toilets and a hand-pump water well; open mid-May to mid-October. --Claude

name: Devil's Head Campground
note: 21-site drive-in USFS campground in two loops at ~8,800 ft on the Rampart Range, at the base of Devil's Head and its fire-tower trail. Sites take RVs up to 30 ft; vault toilets and a hand water pump (no potable hookups, no dump). FCFS only -- no advance reservations; pay on-site via the rec.gov Scan-and-Pay QR. Reached via ~20 mi of graded gravel Rampart Range Rd (2WD-passable, no 4WD needed), open ~late May to late September, road closed by Dec 1. The creekside lower loop near Jackson Creek has been closed pending sediment removal; the upper loop is a dry forested ridge. --Claude

name: Coffee Pot Spring Campground
note: Small USFS campground (~10 sites) on a south-facing aspen/spruce slope at about 10,100 ft, roughly 16 miles up Coffee Pot Road (FDR 600) from the Dotsero exit off I-70. Sites have level gravel driveways that accommodate RVs/trailers up to about 30 ft; picnic tables, fire rings, vault toilets, and no potable water. FCFS, no reservations; access is via a long, narrow one-lane gravel road (drive slowly), but it is passable for a 23-ft rig. Season roughly mid-summer through early fall. --Claude

name: Homestake Valley Campground
note: Designated-site Forest Service campground (44 single + 2 group sites, formerly the informal Blodgett/Homestake established-dispersed sites) spread along the lower gravel of Homestake Road (FSR 703) and Missouri Creek Road southwest of Red Cliff, overlooking Homestake Creek at ~8,900 ft. Per the USFS some sites are RV-accessible while others fit personal vehicles only, so a 23-ft rig works at some sites but not all; no on-site restrooms or potable water, fire rings and tables. FCFS for the 2026 season (June 15-Oct 3, $20/single); about half the singles plus the group sites become reservable on recreation.gov starting 2027. The graded lower road is passable for most vehicles (the rough 4WD pitch is the last mile to the reservoir, well past the campground), but turnarounds are limited so pick an RV-capable site. --Claude

name: Marshall Park Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~7 mi SW of Creede on Hwy 149 at ~8,800 ft. 16 sites (7 paved pull-throughs, 9 gravel spurs) in a meadow on the Rio Grande; sites 1-10 reservable on recreation.gov, 11-17 FCFS. Vault toilet, no drinking water (bring your own). No hookups. Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: River Hill Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~9.5 mi west on FR 520 (Rio Grande Reservoir Rd) off Hwy 149, ~9,250 ft, on the Rio Grande just below Rio Grande Reservoir. 20 sites (13 reservable on recreation.gov, 7 FCFS); max spur 55 ft but not all fit it, most take up to ~30 ft. Two vault toilets, well hand-pump water, a few sites have electric hookup. Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: Thirty Mile Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~11 mi west on FR 520 off Hwy 149, ~9,300 ft, ~1 mi below Rio Grande Reservoir in a wooded meadow between the Rio Grande and Grizzly Creek. 35 sites (28 reservable on recreation.gov, 7 FCFS); max spur 40 ft, not all sites fit it, 9 pull-throughs. Vault toilets, seasonal potable water (Memorial Day-Labor Day). No hookups. --Claude

name: North Clear Creek Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~2.1 mi north on FR 510 off Hwy 149 (~24.5 mi west of Creede), ~9,900 ft, adjacent to North Clear Creek (firsthand reviews put sites ~50 yd from the creek, not on the bank). 21 sites; max spur 40 ft, not all fit it. Two vault toilets, well potable water, camp host. No hookups. FCFS - not on recreation.gov. Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: Road Canyon Campground
note: Rio Grande NF, ~6 mi west on FR 520 off Hwy 149 (~20 mi SW of Creede), ~9,300 ft, in an open treeless meadow at the head of Road Canyon Reservoir (sites not at the shoreline; boat launch nearby). 6 units plus an undeveloped overflow area; max spur 50 ft, not all fit it. Vault toilet, no drinking water, no hookups. FCFS - cannot be reserved. Peak Memorial Day-Labor Day. --Claude

name: Rito Hondo Campground
note: Rio Grande NF dispersed camping area (signed 'Rito Hondo Campground') near Rito Hondo Reservoir, ~3 mi northwest on FR 513 off Hwy 149, ~10,200 ft. Designated drive-in gravel pads set back from the lake; pit toilet, no water, no hookups, no fee, FCFS. Access is ~2-3 mi of washboard gravel passable to a normal vehicle; RVs to ~31 ft reported by campers. Open range (cattle). --Claude

name: Rock Creek Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Monte Vista RD) on Rock Creek, ~9,200 ft. 10 sites (7 dead-end spurs, 3 pull-through), spurs 20-40 ft, RVs up to ~30-35 ft though not all sites fit. Vault toilets, no potable water. FCFS, not reservable; $10/night. Access via CR 28, dirt and single-lane after 3.1 mi but passable by passenger vehicle. Some sites sit next to the creek but no per-site map/satellite confirmed a pad at the waterline. --Claude

name: Mix Lake Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Conejos Peak RD), 1 mi west of Platoro at ~10,000 ft overlooking Mix Lake. 22 sites with trailer spaces (RVs to ~40 ft), 3 vault toilets, potable water. FCFS, not reservable. Season roughly Memorial Day-Labor Day; forest road snow-closed Mar 15-May 15. Access point for South San Juan Wilderness. --Claude

name: Poso Campground
note: Rio Grande NF (Saguache RD) on a hillside above the South Fork of Carnero Creek. 8 sites, max trailer length 25 ft, two vault toilets, no potable water. FCFS, not reservable. Season ~May 19-Nov 18. Access via gravel Carnero Creek Rd (FR 690) then FR 675; passenger-vehicle passable. No horses/pack animals in the campground. --Claude

name: Spectacle Lake Campground
