name: Grundy County Lake
note: Grundy County Conservation Board campground at the SW corner of Dike (completed 2008). 59 electric sites with 30/50-amp service and water at each site; modern shower house, dump station; FCFS, no reservations ($20/night). A 40-acre fishing lake is a short paved-trail walk away but the camp loop is separated from it by a road and a wide treed buffer, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Halvorson Park
note: Mitchell County Conservation Board park on the Cedar River, ~1.5 mi S of St. Ansgar. ~50 non-designated electric sites (50/30/20 amp) with water, dump station, modern showers, swim beach and boat ramp. Self-register at the entrance; FCFS. Open ~May-mid Oct. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Hannen Park
note: Benton County CB park on a 45-acre man-made lake (Iowa's first CCB lake). ~69-83 campsites, electric, FCFS with self-register at the kiosk within 1 hr of arrival. Beach, shower houses, boat ramp/kayak launch, rustic cabin. Open mid-April to end of October. RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Hickory Hills Park
note: Black Hawk County Conservation park near La Porte City around 39-acre Casey Lake (camp loops sit inland in the hardwoods, not on the lake). ~69 camping units, 47 with electric on gravel pads; some full-hookup (water/sewer) sites. Water hydrants throughout, dump station at the exit, showers and flush toilets. Three group camp areas also available. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com (check-in 3pm, out 2pm); rates from ~$13. Closes Nov 1, reopens in spring; gate hours 6am-10:30pm. Note: Casey Lake drawdown/excavation work has caused periodic disruptions. RV Life unrated, price $. --Claude

name: Hoefle-Dulin Recreation Area
note: Benton County Conservation river park on the Cedar River minutes from Vinton. 11-12 sites, 9 with electric + 2 primitive; $16/night electric. FCFS, self-register at kiosk. Season Apr 15-Oct 31. Open-air shelter, pit toilet, playground, river/kayak access; no showers. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Howard County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Howard County Agricultural Society fairgrounds with a real public RV campground (not event-only): ~42 RV sites, water + 50-amp electric, on-site dump station, self-register. Open ~May-Oct. FCFS except the week before and week of the county fair, when it's reserved/closed to transients; long-stays (>5 days) need pre-approval, tent camping by pre-approval only. Big-rig friendly. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Independence RV Park & Campground
note: City of Independence Parks & Rec RV park on the Wapsipinicon River. 42 full-hookup sites (sewer/water/50-amp, picnic table, fire ring), pull-through and back-in angled pads that fit big rigs. Tent $10, RV $35/day, $150/wk, $400/mo (senior 60+ $30/$140/$365); season Mar 1-Nov 15. Showers/restrooms, dump, swimming pool, playground, WiFi. Reserve by phone (host 319-440-0472, off-season Parks & Rec 319-334-6711); some sites kept FCFS. River access for canoe/kayak/fishing but pads sit behind a continuous wooded riverbank treeline, not on the water. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Jefferson County Park
note: Jefferson County Conservation park, 254 acres on the SW edge of Fairfield. Original 24-pad electric campground (most with water) plus the newer Prairie Ridge full-hookup loop (14 sites, for self-contained rigs); two camping cabins. Electric $20 / non-electric $15. Open May 1-Oct 31. Nature center, 7 mi of trails. Campground sits in timber; the park's ponds/wetlands are separate from the sites (not waterfront). RV Life 4.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: Klock's Island Park
note: City of Fayette park on an island formed by the Volga River; drive-in via bridge (not boat-in). ~30 sites with electric (110V, 30 & 50 amp) plus water hookups; big-rig friendly open grassy loops fit a 23-ft rig. Modern restroom/shower house (water on Apr-Oct), $2 dump station, 14-day stay limit. Reservable online (parkwith.us/Campspot); ~$25/night RV per city page. Volga River is paddling/tubing access only - camping loops sit back from the water behind a treeline, not riverfront. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Lake Belva Deer Park
note: Keokuk County CB on 260-acre Lake Belva Deer, ~5 mi NE of Sigourney. 56 modern sites with concrete pads, electric (30-amp), water and a dump station; accommodates RVs up to 45 ft. Primitive sites also available. $20/night modern, $12 primitive. FCFS, no reservations. Shower house with flush toilets. Camping loops sit in an open clearing screened from the lakeshore by a treeline (not on the water). RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Lake Iowa Park
note: Iowa County CB on Lake Iowa. 122 gravel sites, all with electric (mostly 30-amp; sites 117-122 are 50-amp) and water hookups throughout. FCFS, no reservations (set up early by registering an erected camper/tent). $20/night May-Sep, $10/night Oct-Apr. Two shower houses. Camping loops sit back from the lake behind a band of woods (not on the water). Big-rig friendly gravel pads. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Lake Meyer Park and Campground
note: Winneshiek County Conservation park between Calmar and Fort Atkinson on 38-acre Lake Meyer. 27 electric + 8 non-electric sites; $20/night electric, $15 primitive. FCFS, no reservations. Season ~Apr-Oct. Dump station, flush toilets, showers, drinking water; nature center, electric-motor boating, hiking. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Lake Miami Park
note: Monroe County Conservation Board park on the 135-acre Lake Miami, 5 mi E of Lovilia. Full-hookup, electric, and primitive sites; restrooms, showers, potable water, dump station. Open ~Apr 1-Nov 1. Reservable via mycountyparks.com. Camp loops sit back from the lake behind trees/grass (boat ramp + day-use area on shore), not on the water. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Lake Sugema Campgrounds
note: Van Buren County CB park on 579-acre Lake Sugema between Keosauqua and Cantril. ~28-29 sites: ~11 full-hookup hard pads, ~10 water/electric, ~7 gravel electric. 21 reservable on mycountyparks.com (5 days ahead); 7 gravel sites FCFS. Hard-surface pads, ADA shower house, dump station, boat ramp. Water on ~May-Oct. RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Lelah Bradley Park Campground
note: Appanoose County Conservation Board park on the Centerville upper/lower reservoirs. 15 full-hookup (50-amp, water + sewer) sites, FCFS. Seasonal shower house, boat ramps/docks, kayak launch, trails. Camping area set back from the water. Open seasonally (closed winter). RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Lime Creek Park (Buchanan County)
note: Buchanan County Conservation park NE of Brandon (RV Life tags it Benton/'Brandon' but it is a Buchanan CCB park). Small campground tucked in the Lime Creek wooded corridor; electric and non-electric sites, playground and shelter house in the campground. $15/night electric, $10 non-electric. FCFS, no reservations. Season Apr 15-Oct 1. Lime Creek runs through the park but camp pads are not at the creek bank (dense canopy, no per-site map) so not waterfront. RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Maynard City Park
note: City of Maynard park on the Volga River; ~21 grassy water+electric sites with some pull-throughs (big-rig friendly). Dump station; vault/restroom toilets, no showers. River/canoe access for paddling (sites sit inland near the ballfields, not on the riverbank). FCFS, no reservations; fee paid to city. Contact city office 563-637-2269. RV Life: unrated (star_rating 0), price_level 1 ($). --Claude

name: McFarlane Park
note: Black Hawk County CB on the Cedar River, 150-acre park (62-acre hunting area). 64 electrical sites; 28 of the main-campground sites are reservable (call the CB), remainder FCFS. Modern restrooms/showers, dump station, drinking water, WiFi. Camp loops sit back from the Cedar behind a wide treeline (not on the water). Open 6am-10:30pm; closes Dec 1, reopens spring. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: McGowen Recreation and Wildlife Area
note: Davis County Conservation Board, 290-acre rec/wildlife area near Bloomfield. 13 sites with water + electric (30/50-amp), 8 pull-through, accommodating 35-70 ft rigs; restrooms/showers and dump station on site; 2 modern cabins. Reservable via mycountyparks.com, $25/night (tent $10). Gravel access road, drive-in/big-rig passable. Several ponds in the area but camp loops are tree-buffered, not on the water. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Middle Amana Community Park
note: Municipal (city) park campground in Middle Amana village (Amana Colonies), camping under old oak trees. RV and tent sites with electric and drinking water, dump station and laundry on site. Sites from ~$15/night. Lily Lake (a 170-acre lotus marsh) lies south of the village but is separated from the camp area by a wide marsh/field buffer and is not RV-accessible shoreline -> not waterfront. RV Life 2 stars, $. --Claude

name: Minne Estema Park
note: Historic river park on the Cedar River ~8 mi north of Vinton; DNR-owned, operated by Benton County CB. 12 sites (10 electric, 2 primitive), FCFS, $16 electric. Open April 15-Oct 31. Open-air shelter, pit restroom, shoreline fishing and boat/river access. Sites are in riverbank timber; satellite is canopy-blind and no per-site artifact confirms pads at the water, so left not waterfront. RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Morris Park
note: Van Buren County Conservation Board campground; 15 sites, modern + primitive, ~48x11 ft gravel pads (fits a 23-ft rig) with 20/30/50-amp electric, water hydrants, dump tank, shower house. Open year round (reduced Nov-Apr: electric only, no water). Reservable online (mycountyparks.com, 5+ days ahead) or FCFS drive-in; 3-night minimum on holiday weekends. Stocked pond on-site but campsites are set back in the woods, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: North Cedar Park
note: Bremer County Conservation park east of Plainfield on the Cedar River: 40+ electric sites (20 & 30 amp) plus tent sites, dump station, well water, flush toilets, showers; boat ramp/river access. No designated sites - FCFS with self-registration kiosk. Open May 1-Oct 31, ~$20 electric/$15 basic. Camping is in timber set back from the river (not on the bank). RV Life 3.5 stars / $. --Claude

name: North Woods Park
note: Bremer County CB park, 82 acres ~1 mi north of Sumner. Electric campsites with 20/30/50-amp service, modern restroom/shower facility, no designated sites, FCFS with self-register at kiosk (no reservations). Open May 1-Oct 31. No lake at the campground (two ponds are a short walk in the adjacent Wilson Grove Wildlife Area). RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Oelwein City Park Campground
note: City of Oelwein park campground on the south edge of town beside Lake Oelwein (~24-55 acre no-wake lake). 42 electric+water sites incl. 50- and 30-amp and 3 pull-through, plus primitive tent area; modern restroom/shower house, dump station. FCFS, no reservations. Season Apr 15-Oct 15. Camp loops sit behind a wooded buffer from the lake (boat access only), not on the shore -> not waterfront. RV Life 3.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Osage Spring Park
note: City of Osage park on the Cedar River. Primitive/basic riverside camping, pull-through sites accept big rigs up to ~40 ft, toilets and drinking water (from the namesake spring); minimal hookups - confirm electric on arrival. FCFS. Open ~April-Nov. Wooded riverbank - sites not confirmed at the waterline. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Ottumwa Park Campground
note: City of Ottumwa campground in Greater Ottumwa Park (340 acres) near the Des Moines River. 100+ electric/water sites (20-amp), many pull-through and big-rig friendly (sites run a bit small; a 30-ft trailer fits site 50). Dump station, shower house/restrooms, WiFi. FCFS, no reservations. Open ~April-October. $20/night ($25 effective July 1, 2026). Sites sit in city-park green space near the river but not on the water. RV Life $$ / unrated. --Claude

name: Pioneer Ridge Nature Area
note: Wapello County Conservation Board nature area (HQ + nature center), just north of the Davis/Wapello line on Hwy 63. 9 electric/water hookup sites (gravel pad, fire ring, picnic table) plus 3 primitive sites; also cabins. Online reservation only via mycountyparks.com. Season Mar 15-Nov 1; 14-day max stay. Showers May 1-Sep 30 only; no dump station. Camp loop sits in mowed/timber ground; small stocked ponds nearby but sites not on the water. RV Life unrated. --Claude

name: Riverside Park Campground (Spillville)
note: City of Spillville park campground on the Turkey River. Sites with 30/50-amp electric, picnic tables, fire rings; bathrooms, showers and dump station within walking distance. Canoe landing, disc golf, ballfield. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Riverview Park Campground
note: City of Marshalltown Parks & Rec campground in Riverview Park near the Iowa River (camping loops sit well inland of the river). 10 full-hookup (50-amp) sites, 30 electric (50/30/20 amp) sites and 17 parking pads; drive-through sites fit large RVs. Shower house, flush toilets, dump station. Reservable online; open mid-April-end of Oct. RV Life 3.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Rodgers Park
note: Benton County CB park, 207 acres just outside Vinton on a 21-acre lake. 52 electric + 5 non-electric sites, FCFS with self-register at kiosk within 1 hr of arrival. Shower house at park shop, dump station, boat ramp/kayak launch, beach (note: water hookups/shower house at sites were out of service per the CCB page). Camping areas overlook the lake. Open mid-April to end of October. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Sharon Bluffs Park
note: State-owned park on the bluffs above the Chariton River E of Centerville, maintained/operated by the Appanoose County Conservation Board since 1981. 6 campsites with 30-amp electric, FCFS (no reservations); pit latrines, fill/dump station at park entrance. Open year-round. Campground sits on the wooded bluff top, well back and above the river, not on the water. RV Life 3.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Siggelkow Park
note: Black Hawk County CB, off Fairbank Rd north of Dunkerton, with canoe/kayak access to the Wapsipinicon River. Small campground with electric sites, water, dump station and drinking water; no picnic tables or showers. River is screened by woods (sites sit by the entrance road/pond, not on the water). Quiet river park; FCFS. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Split Rock Park
note: Chickasaw County CB; largest park in the system on a 7-acre lake. Camping loop with electric (50-amp) sites, $18/night with electric / $12 without. General sites FCFS; the camping loop can be reserved by calling the CB office at least two weeks ahead. Modern shower/restroom, dump, swimming beach, boat ramp. Open-grass loop fits a 23-ft rig. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Turkey River Campground (Vernon Springs Park)
note: Howard County CB at Vernon Springs Park on the Turkey River, near Cresco. 46 sites (17 pull-thru), each with water and 50/30/110-amp electric. $25/night incl. hookups. FCFS, no reservations; self-register within an hour of arrival. Modern shower/restroom, playground. Campsites are in an open field set back from the river and a small adjacent lake behind treelines (not on the water). Big-rig friendly with pull-thrus. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Twin Bridges Park
note: Small (17.5-acre) Fayette County Conservation Board park at the confluence of the south & west branches of the Volga River, 3 mi N of Maynard. Drive-in camping on the south side: ~2 electric sites plus several primitive sites in an open mowed grassy area (fits a 23-ft rig on grass). Pit toilets, shelter house, playground, dump station; no showers. Open year round. FCFS per the CCB camping page ($15 electric / $10 primitive); mycountyparks.com shows a 'Book Now' link but the CCB lists county parks as first-come. RV Life unrated (star_rating 0), price $. --Claude

name: Waubonsie Trail Park
note: City of Cantril park, 23 acres. 16 sites with water + electric (some 50-amp), two with sewer; rustic and ADA camping cabins. Sewer dump station, ADA shower house, free wifi, disc golf, covered bridges. Reservable by phone. Only water feature is a decorative 1-acre trail pond (not at the sites). RV Life 5 stars / $. --Claude

name: West Idlewild Campground
note: DNR-owned river-access tract along the Cedar River NW of Floyd, leased and operated by the Floyd County Conservation Board. 15 primitive drive-in sites, FCFS via self-registration ($10/night); vault toilet, picnic shelters, canoe ramp. Sites sit in a clearing screened from the river by a band of riverbank trees, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Wildcat Bluff Recreation Area
note: Benton County Conservation Board park, ~2 mi S of Urbana on the Cedar River. 26 campsites (23 electric @ $20/night, 3 non-electric @ $10/night); season mid-April to end of October. All sites FCFS, self-register at the kiosk within 1 hr of arrival (no online reservations). Showers and pit restrooms; no dump station (nearest CCB dump is at Rodgers Park). Boat ramp, river fishing access, hiking trails, disc golf. Campsites sit on an elevated cleared bench set back from the river behind a treeline. Big-rig fit not posted; loop has drive-in pull-in pads suited to a 23-ft rig. RV Life 4 stars / $. --Claude

name: Wilder Park
note: City of Allison park on Hwy 3 just E of town; ~52 sites including 30/50-amp electric and full-hookup, with back-in and pull-through spots that fit big rigs. Drinking water, flush toilets, showers, dump station, WiFi, on-site camp hosts. Two fishing ponds in the park but campsites sit uphill/back from them behind trees, not on the water. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: 4th Pumping Plant Recreation Area
note: Des Moines County Conservation park on a Mississippi River backwater lake near Oakville (operator is Des Moines County, not Louisa). 46 sites: 22 electric ($25), 24 primitive ($10); central water + RV dump station, no showers, 3 outhouses, boat ramp/dock. FCFS self-register, no advance reservations; 14-day max stay. Gravel access road (a reviewer hauled a rig in for a week) but passable. RV Life unrated (0 stars), price $. --Claude

name: Bankston Park
note: Dubuque County Conservation park near Holy Cross. ~50 sites, electric (30-amp, slated to upgrade to 50-amp), max RV ~30 ft; toilets, tables, grills, playground. Open Apr-Oct. Partial online reservations via mycountyparks.com, remainder FCFS. Has a restored fishing pond but campsites sit back from it. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Bennett Park
note: Cedar County Conservation park 2 mi E of Bennett on Hwy 130. ~10-17 sites with 50/30/20-amp electric and water nearby, gravel back-in and pull-through pads; shower house w/ flush toilets (seasonal), pit toilet year-round, RV dump station. Electric $15/night. Has a small fishing pond but campground loop sits inland from it. RV Life 5-star, $. --Claude

name: Big Hollow Recreation Area
note: Des Moines County Conservation campground on 178-acre Big Hollow Lake, ~10 mi N of Burlington. 32 full-hookup sites (electric/water/sewer, $30) + 18 electric-only ($25) + 16 wooded primitive ($10); sites have room for camper plus tow/boat trailer, accommodates a 23-ft rig. Modern restroom/shower house and central dump station. Open year-round; 14-day max stay. Half the RV sites reservable up to a year ahead via mycountyparks.com, rest FCFS. RV pads sit back in trees, not on the lakeshore (satellite at 40.9535,-91.2255: campground loops separated from lake by a treeline; nearby water is small ponds). RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Big Spring Campground (Clayton County)
note: Clayton County Conservation primitive campground on the Turkey River, ~10 mi NW of Elkader by Big Spring trout hatchery. 7 primitive sites, $15/night, one RV or trailer per site (no length posted; small river-bottom sites). Pit toilet, fire ring, picnic table; no dump/showers. Year-round; may close at flood stage. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com (3 days advance, weekend 2-night min). RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Bloody Run County Park
note: Clayton County Conservation park in a limestone-bluff valley along Bloody Run trout stream, just W of Marquette. ~19-20 primitive (no-hookup) campsites, one RV/trailer per site, quiet generators OK; FCFS, $15/night with 2-night weekend / 3-night holiday minimum. Open seasonally May-Oct (closed Nov-Apr). No dump/showers. Low valley sites can flood after heavy rain. RV Life unrated (0 star) / $. --Claude

name: Buffalo Creek County Park
note: Linn County Conservation Board (Walnut Grove Campground) near Coggon. 31 sites in two loops: 18-site A-loop reservable online via mycountyparks.com ($30/night, concrete pads, electric+water, fire ring, table, campground host) plus 13-site B-loop first-come-first-served, all electric/water. Electric service 20/30/50 amp - 50A loop fits big rigs. New (2023) shower house/restroom; dump station near park entrance. Seasonal: park roads closed to vehicles late Oct-Apr 15. RV Life 4-star / $$. --Claude

name: Central Park (Jones County)
note: Jones County Conservation park on a 25-acre lake + 7-acre pond. ~73 numbered sites: 18 modern (30A, sewer/water/wifi), 7 semi-modern (50A, water/wifi), 13 electric (30A), ~35 primitive. FCFS self-register at kiosk, no reservations; availability posted Thu-Sun in season. Water/electric on Apr 15-Oct 15 (weather dependent), primitive camping year-round; dump station and showers on site. Satellite at 42.110,-91.138: RV loops sit south of the lake behind a grass/tree buffer, not on the shoreline -- not waterfront. RV Life 4 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Chatfield Park
note: Lee County Conservation park on a 9-acre lake N of Keokuk. 14 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp, water, sewer) plus 10 primitive; full-hookup pads are pull-through ~60x22 with concrete, fits a 23-ft rig and larger. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 31 (gates close after first snow). Reservable online via mycountyparks.com, $26/night; 2-night weekend / 3-night holiday minimum. Dump station and showers. Campground sits up the slope back from the lake (wooded buffer), so not on the water. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Coffins Grove Park
note: Delaware County Conservation Board park 4 mi NW of Manchester. 18 sites: 15 with 30-amp electric + water, 3 primitive. Drive-in loop, dump station, vault toilets, no showers; well water. FCFS with self-registration within 1 hr of setting up (not reservable). Open mid-April to mid-October. Sites sit in open mowed clearings; Coffins Grove Creek runs through a wooded buffer to the north (stream fishing). RV Life unrated (0 stars), $. --Claude

name: Eden Valley Refuge
note: Clinton County Conservation refuge near Baldwin, in a wooded valley on Bear Creek. ~28 semi-modern electric/water sites (50/30/20-amp), $25/night; clean restrooms with seasonal showers, playground. Drive-in pull-in pads on the loop; primitive/hike-in sites also exist across the road. Sites sit in the wooded valley, not on the creek. RV Life 5-star, $$. --Claude

name: F.W. Kent Park
note: Johnson County Conservation campground in F.W. Kent Park. 86 electric sites (30 & 50 amp), blacktopped pads with picnic table and fire ring, in two wooded loops (North & South); sites up to ~42 ft confirmed by big-rig reviews. $20/night, first-come-first-served (no reservations; pay online via mycountyparks.com or call the office). Open seasonally ~Apr 15-Oct 15 (campground hosts mid-May to mid-Sep); park open year-round. Shower house with flush toilets, additional vault/pit toilets, sanitary dump station, firewood for sale, playground, storm shelter. Secluded/wooded loops set back from the 27-acre lake (not lakeside). RV Life 4.5 stars, price $ (1/4). --Claude

name: Fillmore Recreation Area
note: Dubuque County Conservation 116-acre rec area NE of Cascade on Hwy 151, on Whitewater Creek. Drive-in RV/tent sites with pull-in pads; modern shower/restroom (closed ~Nov 1-May 1). Partial online reservations via mycountyparks.com (up to a year ahead), remainder FCFS. Whitewater Creek runs the park but pads sit back behind a treeline. RV Life 2-star, $$. --Claude

name: Finley's Landing Park
note: Dubuque County Conservation park on the Mississippi (Pool 11) ~10 mi NW of Dubuque (legacy 'city park' name; county-operated). ~24 sites, 8 electric-only plus electric/water hookups, 1 pull-through, max RV ~30 ft; shower/restroom, boat ramp, sandy swimming beach. ~30% online-reservable via mycountyparks.com, ~70% FCFS. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Flaming Prairie Park
note: Louisa County Conservation park (mailing Muscatine), ~10 mi E of Grandview near a Mississippi River backwater. ~24 electric sites (20/30/50A), some pull-through gravel pads; water Apr-mid-Oct, modern pit toilets, picnic shelter, primitive boat ramp to the river. Open year-round but low-lying -- frequently closed by Mississippi high water, so call ahead in spring/high-water periods. Satellite at 41.270,-91.118: sites are in an open mowed flat set back from the backwater behind a continuous treeline (river access is a short walk/drive, no shoreline pads) -- not waterfront. RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Fountain Springs Park
note: Delaware County Conservation Board park 2 mi NE of Greeley, 239 acres on Elk Creek (stocked trout stream). Free primitive drive-in camping in an open meadow (no hookups), fire rings, picnic tables, vault toilets; open year-round, FCFS (self-register). Sites sit in the meadow set back from the creek behind a treeline. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Frenchtown County Park
note: Small Clayton County Conservation park 3 mi north of Guttenberg on a Mississippi backwater. 3 free primitive sites, no hookups/dump/showers, vault toilet, boat ramp; FCFS. Drive-in (cross a railroad track to enter); RVs do use it per RV Life reviews (rated ~8/10, ~15 reviews; price $) and satellite showing parked campers, but it is bare-bones and seasonal (spring-fall, closes during flooding). --Claude

name: Frieden's Park
note: Clayton County Conservation primitive campground 5.5 mi NW of Elkader along the Turkey River. 3 basic sites, $15/night, one RV or trailer per site (small canoe-access sites; no length posted). Pit toilet, fire pit, picnic table; no dump/showers; generators prohibited. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com (weekend 2-night/holiday 3-night min). RV Life unrated / $. --Claude

name: Gilbertson Campground and Education Area
note: Fayette County Conservation Board campground on the Turkey River near Elgin. 27 electric (30-amp) sites plus 8 primitive/non-electric and 7 walk-in tent sites; vehicles up to 35 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits. Bathhouse with showers/flush toilets and a dump station; water fill available. All sites first-come-first-served (typically fills only on holiday weekends) — not reservable. Electric $15/$20, non-electric $10/$15 per night. Open seasonally (summer; nature center/petting zoo Memorial Day-Labor Day). RV Life unrated (0 stars), price $. --Claude

name: Heritage Campground & Pond
note: Dubuque County Conservation Board park 3 mi N of Dubuque off US 52 at the eastern Heritage Trail trailhead. 20 gravel back-in sites, 65 ft long, 50-amp electric + water; dump station, pit latrine (no showers). Reservable on mycountyparks.com (4-day advance min) or FCFS. 9-acre trout pond on site but camp loops sit back behind a road and treeline, not on the water. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Hills Access
note: Johnson County Conservation park on the Iowa River, 1/2 mi E of Hills. 20 sites: 13 electric (30/50-amp), 7 primitive. Electric $15, primitive $10. Pit toilets, dump station, picnic tables, fire rings, playground, boat ramp. Seasonal: closed Oct 1-Apr 15. Sites reservable or instant-pay online via mycountyparks.com. RV Life 3 stars / $. --Claude

name: Horseshoe Pond Campground
note: City of Maquoketa park at 905 S Main on the south edge of town. 22 electric sites (19 back-in, 3 pull-through); main sites have 50/30/20-amp, four pond-side sites 30/20-amp. No water/sewer at sites but dump station w/ potable + non-potable water; no showers. Sites 1-11 & 20 reservable online (Firefly), 12-19 FCFS; ~$25/night. 2-acre pond on site ringed by trees with camp loop set back, so not on the water. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Hubinger Landing
note: City of Keokuk park on the Mississippi River. ~16 drive-through sites with water + 20/30/50-amp electric for self-contained RVs; restrooms/pool/playground nearby in adjacent Victory Park. Reservations via the George M. Verity museum (319-524-4765, alt 319-524-3553). RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Indian Lake Park
note: City of Farmington municipal park on a 44-acre no-wake lake. 35 RV/camper sites: 13 full-hookup ($30), 14 water/electric ($25), 8 electric-only ($20), plus 8 tent sites ($10). Coin-op shower house Apr-Oct (weather permitting), $1/use. Reservations accepted/recommended by phone but not required (FCFS welcome); 2pm checkout. Drive-in loop with full-hookup pads fits a 23-ft rig. RV Life 4-star, $$. --Claude

name: Jakway Park
note: Buchanan County Conservation Board campground in Jakway Forest, south of Aurora on the east side of Buffalo Creek. Shaded oak campground on an oval drive-in loop with 30-amp electric and non-electric sites; fee $18/night electric, $12-15 non-electric. Modern shower house and RV dump station. Season ~Apr 15-mid-Oct. First-come-first-served (only Fontana Park reserves online; Jakway/Lime Creek are FCFS). Disc golf, playground, fishing on Buffalo Creek/quarry pond, historic Richardson-Jakway House. RV Life unrated (0 stars), price level $. --Claude

name: Kalona City Campground
note: City of Kalona campground in the city park; 38 sites, all with water + electric (50-amp service per RV Life; one Campendium reviewer reported 30-amp). Paved pads, level, includes some pull-throughs; big-rig friendly (a reviewer fit a 39-ft fifth wheel without unhooking), so EKKO's 23 ft is no problem. Open Apr 15-Oct 15; $25/night, FCFS (no advance reservations); dump station $10/use; showers, restrooms, and a city swimming pool/water park in the same park (pool not included in fee). Register within 1 hr of occupying a site. RV Life 4 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Louisa County Fairgrounds
note: Louisa County fairgrounds in Columbus Junction with a real transient RV campground (not event-only): 22 electric, big-rig-friendly sites open ~April 1 to Nov 1, plus showers/restrooms/potable water. Electric sites are reservable via louisacountyfair.org. RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Marr Park
note: Washington County Conservation Board's main park near Ainsworth. ~40-41 campsites, all with 50-amp electric + water spigots. First-come, first-served only; $20/night. Dump station (free for campers) and shower house. Big rigs fit (open pad rows). Two small fishing ponds set back behind a buffer, not at the pads. RV Life 4.5 star / $. --Claude

name: Massey Marina Park
note: Dubuque County Conservation Board park ~5 mi S of Dubuque off Hwy 52 / Great River Road on Mississippi River Pool 12. 45x12 ft concrete sites with 50-amp electric + water, plus primitive/tent sites, alongside a marina (fuel dock, slip rentals, concession). Showers/restrooms in the restaurant lower level; trailer dump station. ~30% of sites reservable (mycountyparks.com, 4-day advance), 70% FCFS. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Miller Riverview Park & Campground
note: City of Dubuque 20-acre park on the Mississippi River next to Q Casino, ~5 min from downtown. 97 full-electric sites (20/30/50-amp), pull-through and back-in; city water, dump station, restrooms/showers, playground, paved trail. Open mid-April to the 4th Sun of October (river-level dependent). Reservable up to 240 days out incl. same-day. Recently rebuilt; a treeline strip screens the river from the sites, so not waterfront. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Morgan Creek Park
note: Linn County Conservation family campground in 352-acre park. 35 modern sites with electric + water; RV Life lists 50-amp, pull-through, big-rig friendly. Fire-pad + picnic table each; nearby showers/restrooms; dump station for Linn County campers. Season ~Apr 15 - Oct 15, ground conditions permitting. FCFS with on-site self-registration/pay (becomes reservable via mycountyparks.com in 2027). Firewood at campground host site. RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Oakland Mills Park - South Shore
note: Henry County Conservation Board, on the Skunk River. 30 sites: 24 with concrete pads + 50A electric and water nearby, plus 6 primitive walk-in sites. Shower house/restroom, dump station, playground, river fishing access. 50A pad sites fit a 23-ft rig. Season mid-April to mid-October. Reserve via Henry County Conservation Rec1 portal (no longer mycountyparks). RV Life 2.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Oakland Mills Park - Water Works
note: Henry County Conservation Board, on the north shore of the Skunk River. 20 semi-modern sites (50A electric, pressurized water) plus 5 primitive sites; dump station, restroom, resident host. Electric sites listed at ~32 ft, fit a 23-ft rig comfortably. Season mid-April to mid-October. Reserve via Henry County Conservation Rec1 portal (no longer mycountyparks). RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Osborne Campground
note: Clayton County Conservation; new campground opened May 2025 at Osborne Park (CCB offices/nature center) S of Elkader off Hwy 13. 20 electric+water sites on 80-ft gravel pads (sites 1-14 pull-through, 15-20 back-in) plus 7 tent sites; 80-ft pads easily fit a 23-ft rig. Shower house and dump station on site; 14-day max stay. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com or in person. Volga River runs through the park but campsites sit back near the nature center, not on the bank. RV Life 5 star / $. --Claude

name: Pollmiller Park
note: Lee County Conservation park on a small lake E of West Point. ~29-30 full-hookup sites (20/30/50-amp, water, sewer); reservable site #14 measured ~58x30, big-rig friendly and fine for a 23-ft rig. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 31. Reservable online via mycountyparks.com ($26/night + $4 fee) with FCFS walk-up sites ($25, pay at kiosk); 2-night weekend / 3-night holiday minimum. Dump station, showers, swim beach (Mem Day-Labor Day, $2). RV Life 4.5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Riverview Park
note: City of Clinton park on the downtown Mississippi riverfront. 27 sites incl 2 ADA + 3 pull-throughs for 35'+ rigs; back-in sites fit trailers to 40'. 30-amp + water (no full hookups); pump-out station near entrance, restroom/shower house. FCFS, no reservations; open year-round. Campground loop sits behind a road/parking/ballfield strip, set back from the water. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Scott County Park - Bald Eagle Campground
note: Scott County Conservation, Scott County Park near Eldridge (18850 270th St). Bald Eagle has full-hookup (electric/water/sewer, 50-amp) big-rig-friendly sites; restroom/shower building, some ADA sites. Open year-round (seasonal hours); limited sites reservable May-Sep (mycountyparks.com), FCFS otherwise. Inland wooded loop, no water adjacent. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Scott County Park - Woodside Campground
note: Scott County Conservation, Scott County Park near Eldridge (18850 270th St). Woodside has full-hookup (electric/water/sewer, 50-amp) big-rig-friendly back-in and pull-through pads; restroom/shower building, some ADA sites. Open mid-April to mid-October; reservable May-Sep (mycountyparks.com), FCFS otherwise. Inland wooded loop, no water adjacent. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Scout Park
note: Small city park in Lamont (Buchanan County) run by the City of Lamont; the city's own parks page lists Scout Park with a campground with electrical hookups, plus the Scout House shelter, ball field and playground. Directories list ~6 electric sites with water, restrooms and showers; level open grass/gravel sites that fit a 23-ft rig. No online reservation system found — call Lamont City Hall (563-924-2194); effectively first-come/by-arrangement. RV Life unrated (0 stars), price $. --Claude

name: Sherman Park
note: Clinton County Conservation 233-acre park on the Wapsipinicon River near Calamus. 8 modern electric/water sites ($25) + 7 primitive ($15); modern restrooms, shower house, shelter, playground, boat ramp, observatory. Open Apr 1-Oct 31. Campsite loop sits in trees back from the river. RV Life 5-star, $. --Claude

name: Snively Access Campground
note: Louisa County Conservation campground on the Lake Odessa / Mississippi backwater complex, 3 mi from Wapello off the Great River Road. 31 electric sites (20/30/50A) + 1 tent site, graveled drives, pit toilet, water hydrants; nearest dump at North Park/Rodeo Grounds in Wapello. FCFS, self-register within 30 min at the kiosk, ~$12-17/night; year-round but no winter maintenance on the access hill. Access is a winding, steep gravel descent off the bluff -- doable but go slow; sites are generous (Site 19 is 56x13 ft) so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Subject to river flooding -- call ahead in flood season. RV Life 3 stars / $. --Claude

name: South Sabula Lake Park
note: Jackson County Conservation park on an island/spit in South Sabula Lake (Mississippi backwater) at the south end of South Ave, Sabula. 40 electric+water sites; max rig 35 ft. FCFS, no reservations. Open May-Oct (pending river levels). Showers/restrooms, trailer dump, boat ramp/dock. NOTE: only access road passes under a railroad bridge with 12'5" clearance. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Spruce Creek Park
note: Jackson County Conservation park on a Mississippi River backwater harbor 2 mi N of Bellevue. 80+ sites, all electric (20/30/50 amp). FCFS, no reservations; $25/night. Open May-Oct (opening/closing pending river levels). Showers/restrooms (water off Oct 15-Apr), trailer dump, boat harbor. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Turtle Creek Park
note: Delaware County Conservation Board park on the Lake Delhi impoundment of the Maquoketa River. 26 sites: 24 with water + electric, 2 primitive; dump station, vault toilets, boat ramps, beach. Open ~mid-April to mid-October; reservable via mycountyparks.com. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: Virginia Grove Recreation Area
note: Louisa County Conservation Board's first park, 106 wooded acres near Morning Sun. 11 campsites with 20/30/50-amp electric and central water hydrants, pit toilet, modern playground; a small 5-acre fishing pond sits in the woods (sites not on the water). Open year-round (water off ~mid-Oct to mid-April); reservable via mycountyparks.com. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Wanatee Park
note: Linn County Conservation Board park near Marion (formerly Squaw Creek Park). 69 sites in two loops: A Loop (24 sites, reservable online, full hookup 50/30/20A electric + pressurized water & sewer, concrete drive-thru pads ~17x35 ft) and B Loop (45 FCFS sites, electric + water, no sewer). Open mid-April to mid-October. Modern restroom/showers and two dump stations. Big rigs fit. Cedar River is a couple miles off, not adjacent to sites. RV Life 5 star / $$. --Claude

name: Wapello Fairgrounds Park
note: Small fairgrounds campground in the city of Wapello (Louisa County fairgrounds). 10 electric-only sites with water, restrooms/showers, and a dump station; open Apr-Oct, FCFS (non-reservable). Operates as a normal-season campground, not fair-week-only (fills with exhibitors during the late-summer fair). Satellite (41.1875,-91.1925) shows the camping area on the fairgrounds with no adjacent lake -- a walking trail leads to a small pond a short distance off. RV Life 3 star / $. --Claude

name: Waukon City Park
note: City of Waukon park campground. 26 sites with water + 50-amp electric (handles large RVs) plus 4 tent sites. Shower house/restrooms, free dump station, free firewood/wifi, fire pits, picnic tables, playground; Indian Springs pond at park's south edge connected by trail (not adjacent to sites). RV $35, tent $20. Reservations by phone; walk-ins OK but call to hold a spot. RV Life 4.5 stars / $$. --Claude

name: Welter Recreation Area
note: Des Moines County Conservation area along the Skunk River. Sycamore Loop is the developed camping loop: 18 sites (12 electric), central water, dump station, shelter, outhouses; plus ~22 primitive sites. All sites FCFS (no advance reservations): non-electric $10, electric $25, full-hookup $30. Camp loops sit behind a wooded buffer, set back from the river. RV Life unrated, $. --Claude

name: West Lake Park - Park Terrace Campground
note: Scott County Conservation, West Lake Park ~5 mi W of Davenport. Park Terrace has full-hookup (50-amp) RV sites plus 13 non-electric tent sites; showers, restrooms, dump station, playground. Open year-round (seasonal hours). Mix of reservable (mycountyparks.com) and FCFS sites; senior/county-resident discounts. Big-rig friendly wooded loop set well inland from West Lake, not on the water. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: West Lake Park - Summit Campground
note: Scott County Conservation, West Lake Park ~5 mi W of Davenport. Summit has electric + water (50-amp) drive-through/back-in RV pads; showers, restroom, dump station, playground. Open mid-April to mid-October. Reservable (mycountyparks.com) and FCFS sites. Big-rig friendly wooded loop inland from West Lake, not on the water. RV Life 4.5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Amana RV Park & Event Center
note: Large 80-acre park in the Amana Colonies with 450+ campsites, many full-hookup 50-amp on 75-ft level pads (big-rig friendly), plus tent sites and a 20,000+ sq ft event center. Open Apr 1-Nov 1; online (Firefly) and phone reservations. Long-term sites exist but transient sites are bookable online. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Black Hawk Park
note: Black Hawk County Conservation Board park on the Cedar River near Cedar Falls; ~1,490-acre Cedar River greenbelt. Two campgrounds: Fogdall (21 full-hookup, gravel pads) and Cedar (176 electric sites + some no-amenity sites), ~197 sites total. FCFS, no reservations. Campgrounds close Dec 1, reopen when weather permits in spring. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Briggs Woods Park
note: Hamilton County park on Briggs Woods Lake (70-acre lake, electric-motor only). 81 campsites: 30 full-hookup + 51 electric-only, accommodates large RVs; modern shower house, dump station, swim beach, golf course, trails. Camping season ~Apr 15–Nov 1. Satellite (42.4315,-93.8056): RV loops sit back from the Boone River behind a treeline — pads not on shore. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Camp Crescent
note: Municipal RV park run by the City of Lake View on the shore of Black Hawk Lake; ~161 reservable + 28 non-reservable sites, 86 full-hookup (water/sewer/50-amp) plus 30-amp and electric-only loops, lakefront and non-lakefront sites, beach, boat access. Open April 15-October 1 (limited service in October). Reservable online. RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Camp at the Woods
note: Private RV park along the Shell Rock River SE of Mason City. Full hookups (water/electric/sewer, concrete patio at each site); pull-through sites available. Open year-round; nightly, weekly, and monthly rates (also cabin/camper rentals; no tent sites). Heated pool, playground, river trails. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Cenla RV Park and Campground
note: Private RV park near Center Lake in the Iowa Great Lakes, off Hwy 71; full-hookup sites (water/sewer/50-amp), big-rig pull-throughs to ~75 ft, swimming pool, rec room, restrooms/showers, laundry. Seasonal (mid-April to mid-October). Satellite shows the pads set a block back behind a residential strip from Center Lake, so not on-water. RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Country Court RV Park
note: 12 full-hookup RV sites (50/30/20-amp, individual sewer, water, cable) with pull-throughs; short- and long-term stays, open year-round; on-site storm shelter, 2 blocks south of Hwy 34 W. Call ahead for overnight rates/availability. RV section is separate from the adjoining mobile-home park. RV Life 5★, $ --Claude

name: DeSoto RV Campground
note: Small private full-hookup RV park on US-30 near Missouri Valley (~14 sites; soft water, 30/50 amp, sewer riser at each space, WiFi). Open year-round, transient overnighters welcome (~$35/night reported). Thinly reviewed and no dedicated website. Satellite: compact inland lot with RV pads, no water adjacency. RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Galvin's Park Campground
note: City of Sheffield park. 10 RV sites with electric (30-amp) hookups, plus a modern shower house, shelter with fireplace, and a SafeT Home rental cabin; adjacent to a golf course. Reservable through Sheffield City Hall (2-night weekend minimum); ~$15/night. Open April 1 to October 15. RV Life 4 stars, $. --Claude

name: Gehrke's Lake and Campground
note: Private family campground/RV park beside Gehrke's Lake in rural Hardin County, open Apr 1-Oct 31. Mix of nightly/weekend transient RV sites and seasonal spots (seasonals may store campers over winter); also bookable on Hipcamp. Dump station, WiFi (on request), paddle boats on the lake, walking and ATV trails. Online reservations via official site. RV Life 5 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Griff's Valley View RV Park & Campground
note: Private RV park NW of Altoona; 142 full-hookup concrete pads (30/50-amp), 21 pull-through, big-rig friendly. Pool, playground, camp store, laundry, small fishing pond, adjacent paved bike/walk trail. Leans long-term but takes shorter-term/transient stays; reservations required. Satellite (41.6776,-93.5195): pull-through pods around a small decorative pond — not lakefront. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Gypsum City OHV Campground
note: Webster County Conservation campground at an 800-acre former gypsum mine with 60 mi of OHV/ATV trails. ~33 sites, mix of full-hookup (50/30/20A, water, sewer) and electric/water-only; all level gravel pull-throughs (100-ft pads), big-rig friendly. FCFS plus reservable online. Open Apr 15–Oct 15; FHU ~$30/night + $4 fee, 2-night weekend min. Restrooms/showers on site. RV Life 4.5★, $$ --Claude

name: Hidden Valley Campground (Vinton)
note: Family-owned campground on the Cedar River ~1 mile east of Vinton, operating since 1966. Electric & water or primitive sites, boat ramp. Seasonal, open May 1-Oct 1; reservation-only by phone. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Hitchcock Nature Center RV Campground
note: Pottawattamie County Conservation park in the Loess Hills. 18 modern RV sites with 50-amp electric, water hydrants, shower house with flush toilets, dump station; picnic table + fire ring per site. First-come, first-served (no reservations); ~$47/night. Water on April-November; host may be absent in the off-season. RV Life 5.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Hutchinson Family Farm Campground
note: Working beef/quarter-horse farm campground on the Upper Iowa River, ~10 min from Decorah. Spacious natural riverside sites with electrical and primitive options, plus cabins; on-site canoe/kayak rental and watercraft shuttle. Free farm tours. RV Life 5.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: I-29 Hwy 34 Campground
note: Small private full-hookup RV park at the I-29/Hwy 34 interchange ~14 mi south of Council Bluffs; herringbone of pull-through, big-rig-friendly sites (satellite-confirmed). City water, WiFi, dump station, restrooms/showers, laundry. RV-only (no tents). Daily/weekly/monthly rates; cash or check only; first-come, first-served. RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Indian Hills RV Park
note: 20 full-hookup (30/50A) pull-through gravel sites on US-34 attached to the Indian Hills Inn hotel; big-rig friendly (sites reported plenty long). Year-round; guests get inn amenities (indoor pool, hot tub) and the on-site White Buffalo Restaurant. Daily/weekly/monthly rates. Near Rathbun Lake. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Ingebretson Park
note: Cerro Gordo County Conservation campground on 23 acres along a branch of Beaver Dam Creek in Thornton. 22 electric sites with central dump station, water, showers/flush toilets, open-air shelter; disc golf on site and the town public golf course adjacent. Drive-in angled RV pads (satellite-confirmed) fit a 23-ft rig. $20/night, cashless -- reservable online via mycountyparks.com. Open first weekend of May through mid-October (weather depending). RV Life 5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Joe's Campground
note: Small campground on Bear Creek near Dorchester in the Driftless area. 15 sites with electric and water hookups, dump station, picnic tables; tent/no-hookup sites too. Bear Creek runs through the valley but RV pads sit back across a grassy field from the creek (no pads at the water). RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Kellogg RV Park
note: Transient travel park 1/4 mi off I-80 Exit 173. 38 level pull-through full-hookup sites, big-rig friendly; year-round. On-site AMOCO gas/convenience store, Iowa's Best Burger Cafe, refurbished shower house, laundry, dump station, free WiFi. Monthly/long-term also offered but operates as a general transient RV park. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: Lakeshore RV Resort & Campground
note: Family-owned full-hookup park (water/electric/sewer, 30/50 amp, back-in & pull-through) on Lake Oelwein next to Oelwein City Park, NE Iowa. Beach swimming, kayak/canoe, mini & frisbee golf. A shoreline loop fronts the lake; the larger loop is set back. Online reservations. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Little Turkey River Campground
note: Small family campground in the town of Waucoma on the Little Turkey River, NE Iowa. ~23 RV/tent sites with electric (water/sewer at some); reservations accepted. River tubing/canoeing and trout fishing nearby; a sandy river-access beach is on site but the RV sites sit inland behind a treeline. RV Life 5.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Lizard Creek Ranch & Event Center
note: Private ranch/event center just N of Fort Dodge; ~48 full-hookup RV sites (level, open lawn with scattered trees) open all year, transient camping bookable any time (self-registration drop box/QR or pay by phone), not event-only. $25/night ($155/wk, $600/mo). On-site CreekSide restaurant; ATV/hiking trails to Lizard Creek, near Dolliver Memorial SP. Satellite shows RV rows in open farmland away from creek drainage — not waterfront. RV Life 4★, $$ --Claude

name: Oak Ridge RV Campground (Whiterock Conservancy)
note: Run by Whiterock Conservancy (nonprofit land trust). 14 sites — 6 pull-through, 8 back-in — all with electric (no water/sewer at site; 3 hydrants + central dump station, shower house). Ridge-top oak grove with gravel pads; reservable online via Firefly. Also has 17 horse pens but is a general RV campground, not equestrian-only. Access to 40 mi of trails. $20/night electric. Sites sit on the ridge, not on water. RV Life 5★, $ --Claude

name: Oakwood RV Park
note: Private RV park ~2 blocks SE of Clear Lake (not on the water). ~50 full-hookup sites, all 50-amp; many premium pull-throughs, big-rig friendly to ~45 ft. Open Apr 15-Oct 15; nightly, monthly, and seasonal rates. Free WiFi, laundry, showers, dump station; near the Surf Ballroom. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: On-Ur-Wa RV Park
note: Big-rig-friendly transient park just off I-29 exit 112 at Onawa; all full-hookup (water/sewer/30-50 amp), shaded, with long pull-throughs (deluxe sites up to ~100 ft). Clean showers/restrooms, dog park, laundry, propane/RV supplies. Nightly/weekly/monthly. Inland lot, no water adjacency on satellite. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Pammel Park
note: City of Forest City park along the Winnebago River near the suspension bridge. 43 pads, 41 with 30-amp (partial hookups), shower house, dump station; accepts big rigs. FCFS, ~$15/night paid at campground. Satellite (43.2600,-93.6310): pad loop screened from the river by a treeline — not open shoreline. RV Life 7.9/10, $. --Claude

name: Pattee Park
note: City of Perry park; 4 paved drive-in RV sites, all with 20-amp electric, at least 2 with water; dump station on-site, no individual water hookups. $10/night, 5-day max; register with Perry Police Dept (515-465-4636). Easy access, room for big rigs (Campendium reports a 32-ft Class C). Seasonal — closes Nov 1, reopens spring. RV Life 4★, $ --Claude

name: Peters Park (Rodney Pits Recreation Area)
note: Monona County park on a former gravel-pit lake 1 mi east of Rodney; 49 electric/water pads (26 full-hookup) plus tent area, three handicap sites, group area. Two shower houses, dump station, swim beach, boat ramp, playground. Open year-round, FCFS (no reservations). Full-hookup $25, electric $20, non-electric $15. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Prairie Ridge Camping
note: Small family-owned park, 12 RV sites (10 full hookup w/ water/sewer/electric, 2 water+electric), long pull-throughs; shower house + laundry added 2024. On-site lake/3 ponds for fishing, beach, waterslide, golf driving range; tent sites and waterfront camper rental also offered. RV pull-through pads sit in central clearing set back from the lake (not on water). Book online or by phone. RV Life 4★, $$ --Claude

name: Pulpit Rock Campground
note: City of Decorah Parks & Rec campground on the Upper Iowa River beneath limestone bluffs; trout stream splits the Pulpit Rock and Twin Springs sides. 56 back-in + 19 pull-through electric sites (and tent sites), restrooms/showers, dump station, bike path to downtown. Open Apr 1-Nov 1; online reservations. Camping loops sit set back from the river. RV Life 4.0 star. --Claude

name: R Campground
note: Private campground (est. 1999) on ~18 acres along the Cedar River in Charles City, with a 3-acre fishing pond. Open year-round; transient nightly rates ($30 electric / $40 full hookup) alongside monthly options. 50-amp electric, full hookups (water/sewer), big rigs to 50 ft. Canoe rentals, fishing, on a 5-mile paved bike trail. Satellite shows pads set back from the Cedar River and pond behind a continuous ~100m treeline (not waterfront). RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: RV Park at Grand Falls Casino & Golf Resort
note: Resort RV park beside the casino/golf course, 27 pull-through sites with full hookups (water/sewer/30-50 amp); all rig sizes welcome, no showers at the park, 5-day stay limit. Seasonal ~May 1-Nov 1. Check in at the hotel front desk. Satellite: pad rows NE of the casino lots, not near water. RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Rusty Ridge Campground at ATVenture Valley
note: Family-owned RV/UTV park (ATVenture Valley): 14 RV sites + ~5 tent sites, 30/50-amp electric and water hookups, pull-throughs, dump station; max RV length 70 ft, easily fits a 23-ft rig. On-site 200+ acres of groomed UTV/OHV trails (side-by-side rentals available), fishing pond, volleyball, pasture golf. Drive-in via graded gravel farm roads across flat farmland (normal-vehicle passable; the OHV trails are recreation, not the access route). Reservable online via Campspot. 2025 Campspot Award winner (Top Small Campgrounds). RV Life 5★, $$. --Claude

name: Sampson Springs Campground
note: Small private campground 10 min east of Decorah in the Driftless area. 30 full-hookup and tent sites, big-rig friendly with pull-throughs; dump station, hot showers, laundry. A spring-fed stream runs along one edge with a walking trail, but RV pads are set back in the field (no pads at the water). RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Scales Pointe Camping & Boating
note: Privately run campground (formerly Jolly Rogers, sold 2006) on a peninsula on Coralville Reservoir. 91 full-hookup or water/electric sites plus tent sites; marina with boat ramp, courtesy docks, Bobbers Grill on the lake. Seasonal, opens mid-April. RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Shady Oaks RV Campground
note: Privately owned, family-run; operating since 1925 (oldest private campground in Iowa, on the historic Lincoln Highway). 13 full-hookup RV sites under giant bur oaks; all 30-amp, 5 with 50-amp, water/sewer; dump station, laundry, showers. Big rigs OK (max ~40 ft; a 36-ft Class A fit site 16). Seasonal Apr-Oct. On-site attraction: The Big Treehouse (5,000 sq ft). RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Skip-A-Way Resort
note: Large 167-acre resort in the Turkey River valley west of Clermont, between the Turkey River and 18-acre Quarry Lake. Full-hookup sites (water/sewer/electric); many riverfront/lakeside sites are seasonals but transient nightly sites are bookable online, easier midweek. Beach, restaurant, mini-golf, tube/kayak rental. Open ~April 15-Oct 15. RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Sudbury Court Motel & RV Park
note: RV park behind an independent motel off Hwy 6. ~20 sites, 18 full hookups (30/50 amp, water/sewer), 6 pull-throughs, big-rig friendly (sites accommodate large rigs). Fishing pond, dump station, showers, laundry, WiFi. Open ~March-November. Firsthand reviews confirm transient overnight travelers. RV Life 4★, $$. --Claude

name: Sunrise Campground
note: City of Storm Lake campground on the east shore of Storm Lake; ~103 sites, 36 full-hookup (water/sewer/50-amp on concrete pads), many spacious pull-throughs (accepts rigs to 73 ft). WiFi, restrooms, laundry, boat ramp, near King's Pointe Waterpark. Reservable online. Satellite shows RV loops set back behind a treeline buffer from the shore, so not on-water. RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Swede Point Park
note: Boone County Conservation park west of Madrid. 14 electric sites (30/110-amp, shared pedestals; water hydrants for fill, no individual hookups) plus 2 electric ground sites and 10 non-electric tent areas; electric sites reservable online, tent sites FCFS. Open Apr 15-Oct 15, 14-day max stay. Wooded bluff above the Des Moines River (river is a steep hike down into the valley, ~1/4 mi — no river access/view from sites). Hot showers, modern restrooms, sand volleyball, hiking trails. Max RV length not published; verify fit for tight wooded sites. RV Life 4★, $ --Claude

name: Ted's RV Park
note: Family-owned (est. 2020), open year-round; 26 full-hookup sites (17 pull-through up to ~80 ft + 9 back-in) with 20/30/50-amp, gravel pads. Big-rig friendly, just off I-35 ~1 hr S of Des Moines. Also bookable on Campspot/Hipcamp. RV Life 4.5★, $$. --Claude

name: The Grotto of the Redemption RV Park
note: Operated by the Shrine of the Grotto of the Redemption (Catholic shrine nonprofit, not municipal). 53 sites, all pull-through grass/level, 30 & 50-amp electric, central water + dump station, showers; reviews say it accommodates rigs of all sizes. $20/night RV, FCFS, register at gift shop. Open ~April 15–Nov 1. 50-amp loop sits beside a lightly-used rail line. RV Life 5★, $ --Claude

name: Updog RV & Camping
note: Small family RV park in Mount Pleasant (also branded KJ's Camp & RV). 35 full-service sites (water/sewer/electric, limited 50-amp), shady and pull-through options, takes one-night transient stays; clean showers/restrooms, small fishing pond on property. Open ~April 15-Nov 15. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: White's Riverside Bluffton Campground
note: Riverside campground on the Upper Iowa River at Bluffton, near Decorah. RV sites with 30 amp ($30) and 50 amp ($35) electric; canoe/kayak shuttle service and an on-site bar. Caps about 10 reservations/week (transient); book by phone or email. RV Life 4.0 star, $$. --Claude

name: Wild Rose Casino & Resort RV Park
note: Developed 75-site RV park (no tents) on the Wild Rose Casino property, set along its 12.5-acre lake; concrete pads, 20/30/50-amp, full dump service, WiFi, heated bathhouse, swimming pool. Big-rig friendly. Seasonal April-October; nightly rate includes hotel-lobby breakfast. A genuine campground, not an overnight lot. RV Life 4.0 star, $. --Claude

name: Windmill Ridge Campground
note: Private campground in Kalona (Amish country). 80+ electric sites (some with water), swimming pool, small pond/boating, volleyball. Seasonal, open April-September (closed Oct-March). RV Life 5.0 star, $. --Claude

name: Bailey's Ford Park
note: Delaware County Conservation Board park (170 acres) 3 mi SE of Manchester on the Maquoketa River. 70+ campsites: 44 water+electric, 10 electric, 16 primitive; shower/restroom building, dump station. Trout stream and river fishing; Maquoketa River Water Trail terminus. Open ~mid-April through mid-October; self-registration fees, reservable via MyCountyParks. RV Life 5 star, $. --Claude

name: Buffalo Shores
note: Scott County Conservation recreation area on the Mississippi River downstream of Buffalo; 65 sites with 50-amp electric, water, concrete patios, beachfront sites, shower house, dump, boat ramp, free public beach. FCFS (no reservations), open ~late Apr-mid Oct. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Crossroads RV Park
note: 34 full-hookup sites (30/50A, sewer), pull-through, big-rig friendly (37-ft+toad rigs OK); clubhouse, laundry, hot showers, dump station. Year-round; built 2006 at the Hwy 34/Hwy 218 interchange. Heavy train noise (tracks ~1/4 mi). RV Life 9.0* (113 reviews), $$. --Claude

name: Deep Creek Campground
note: City of Charlotte-managed campground in town, bordered by two small creeks. 14-15 sites (13 are 50-amp), all full hookups (water/electric/sewer); FCFS with self-registration envelopes, $25/camper/night or $150/week, 14-day stay limit. Adjacent city park has playground, volleyball, horseshoes, shelters. Satellite at 41.96098,-90.47031: pad rows set back from the small creeks, not at the water's edge. RV Life 4.5 stars, $. --Claude

name: Duck Haven Campground
note: Privately owned RV park on ~20 acres NW of Fort Madison, adjacent to a stocked lake. 30 water+electric sites, accommodates RVs to 50 ft; shower house, sand volleyball, shelter house, bait/tackle. No official website (active reviews/RoverPass booking confirm operating); book by phone or RoverPass. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Hickory Haven Campground
note: Private year-round RV park on 27 wooded acres just north of Keokuk. 53 full-hookup sites (30/50-amp, water/sewer), drive-thru and back-in, accommodates up to 75 ft; tent sites with 20-amp. Showers, laundry, pavilion, small fishing pond. Reservable via RoverPass. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Hidden River Campground
note: Private campground on the Cedar River near Tipton; ~50 sites, full hookups (30/50/15 amp, water), 10 pull-throughs, dump, showers, beach, boat launch. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Lil' Bit of Country RV Park
note: 16 full-hookup sites (30/50A, sewer at every site), big-rig friendly with many pull-throughs; restrooms w/ hot showers, laundry. Year-round; overnight/weekly/monthly. On Hwy 3 at the west edge of Strawberry Point, co-located with the owners' Lil' Bit of Country Stoves shop. RV Life 9.4*, $$. --Claude

name: Little Bear Campground
note: 45 pull-through full-hookup RV sites (20/30/50 amp), all pull-through; big rigs OK (sites to ~70 ft). Plus tent sites and cabins. Swimming pool, playground, laundry, dump station. Open ~Apr 1-Nov 1. Right off I-80 Exit 259 -- convenient interstate overnight stopover. No standalone website; reserve by phone or Facebook Messenger. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Lou Lou's Landing
note: Private (Lou Lou's Landing LLC) tubing/paddling outfitter and campground on the Wapsipinicon River. 25 sites, big-rig friendly with many pull-throughs; electric sites (~$12.50/night) plus primitive woodland sites (~$5). Dump station, hot-shower restrooms, laundry, fire rings. Canoe/kayak/tube rental with free shuttle. River reached via sandbar/boat ramp; campsites sit on a grassy loop screened from the bank by a continuous treeline. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: New Wine Park
note: Dubuque County park on the North Fork Maquoketa River ~5 mi N of Dyersville; 30-amp electric + water sites, RVs up to ~30 ft, shower house, disc golf. Seasonal (Apr-Nov); ~75% reservable online, rest FCFS. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Pine Bluff Campsites
note: Small private campground (~10 full-hookup sites) on the east edge of Waterville along the Big Paint Creek trout stream. Sites have 50/30/20-amp electric, water, sewer; firepits, free WiFi; rigs up to ~50 ft accepted. Open ~Apr 15-Oct 15; FCFS with prepayment, daily ~$35, monthly available. Near Yellow River State Forest and Effigy Mounds. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Pinicon Ridge Park
note: Linn County Conservation park on the Wapsipinicon River. Three campgrounds: Flying Squirrel (83 elec/water, FCFS), Plains (35, some elec/water, FCFS), Firefly (32 full-hookup, online reservation, opened 2025). Level rock pads, big-rig capable. Open Apr 15-Oct 15, ground conditions permitting. Coords pin Flying Squirrel campground. RV Life 5*, $$. --Claude

name: Porth's RV Park & Storage
note: Small private park at the south end of Calamus; gravel sites with full-hookup pull-throughs (water/electric/sewer, 50 amp), central dump, communal fire pit. Transient sites on one side, full-timers on the other; self-contained rigs, registration box (no office). Adjacent railroad (train noise). RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Rock Creek Marina & Campground
note: Clinton County Conservation park on the backwaters of the Mississippi at its confluence with the Wapsipinicon River, southwest of Camanche off Hwy 67. Electric and primitive sites (no full hookups); 50-ft pads, reservable online via MyCountyParks plus FCFS sites. Electric $25-30/night, primitive $15-20. Open year-round (shower house closed Oct-Apr). Boat ramp, fishing pier, marina slips. RV Life 4 stars, $$. --Claude

name: Rodeo Park Campground
note: City of Fort Madison park next to the Tri-State Rodeo arena. ~20 full-hookup RV sites (water/electric/sewer, 30/50 amp) plus electric-only and primitive sites; open grassy area, few trees, big-rig friendly. FCFS via self-service registration box, no advance reservations; weekly/monthly rates offered. Closed roughly two weeks before Labor Day through Sept 30 for the rodeo. RV Life 4.5 star, $. --Claude

name: Sandbar Campground
note: Newly built full-hookup private RV park on the north side of Guttenberg (a few blocks inland from the Mississippi, town between park and river). Gravel and concrete sites, full hookups; mix of nightly transient and seasonal/monthly sites. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Scenic View Campground
note: Private family-run campground on the Yellow River. RV sites with 50-amp electric, coin laundry, showers, modern restrooms, dump station; multiple riverside loops, tube/kayak rentals, trout fishing, swimming. Mix of nightly/weekend transient and seasonal sites; cabins also available. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Spring Lake Campground
note: ~30-site private campground on a spring-fed fishing/kayak lake; gravel sites with electric (some full) hookups, dump station, two bathhouses, swimming beach, on-site Ma's Bait n Tackle. Pull-throughs available. Takes transient overnighters (reservable by phone, fills fast) plus some seasonals. North- and south-shore sites sit right on the lake. RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Swiss Valley Park
note: Dubuque County park on 62 wooded acres along spring-fed Catfish Creek SW of Dubuque; full-hookup (water/electric) big-rig-friendly sites, many pull-throughs, dump, showers, laundry, disc golf. Sites set back from the creek behind a treeline. Seasonal (Apr 1-Nov 1). RV Life 4.5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Tri State Raceway RV Park
note: Dedicated RV park at the NHRA Tri-State Raceway NW of the dragstrip off US-20 (Exit 285). Built-out gravel loop with ~20+ back-in/angled sites plus pull-throughs, big-rig friendly; full hookups (water/electric/sewer), restrooms and showers; tent sites too. Not event-only -- open for transient nightly camping ~Apr-Oct ($50/night, $300/week). Expect drag-racing noise on event weekends. RV Life 4 star, $$. --Claude

name: Upper Iowa Resort and Rental
note: ~40 RV sites on 29 acres along the Upper Iowa River; full-hookup, 50/30A, and water-only sites (per-site max lengths 40-70 ft; RV Life's 20-ft figure is wrong). Accepts overnight transients (nightly $38-60, Hipcamp bookable, 2-night min on summer holiday weekends) plus a separate seasonal section. Spring-fed swimming pool with sandy beach; canoe/kayak/tube rentals and shuttle. Open Apr-Oct. RV Life 9.1*, $$. --Claude

name: Wilson Lake Park
note: Lee County Conservation park. 16 full-hookup RV sites (14 standard + 2 buddy sites), 60x12 concrete pads, big-rig friendly to 35 ft; also 6 primitive sites and 2 cabins. 6-8 acre trout-stocked lake, boat ramp, hiking trails. Reservable via MyCountyParks; ~$20-25/night. RV Life 5 star, $$. --Claude

name: Wyalusing State Park - Homestead Campground
note: Drive-in family campground; 55 sites, 9 electric (30A). Near visitor center and dump station. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. WI State Park vehicle admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Wyalusing State Park - Wisconsin Ridge Campground
note: Drive-in family campground on the ridge; ~55 sites, inner loop electric (30A), outer ridge-edge sites non-electric. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. WI State Park vehicle admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Nelson Dewey State Park
note: Drive-in family campground; 45 sites, 18 electric. Wooded set-back sites fit a 23-ft rig; the 4 Mississippi-overlook bluff sites are walk-to (tent) only. Flush toilets/showers and dump station. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Yellowstone Lake State Park
note: Drive-in family campground; ~128 sites, 38 with electricity. Large main loop plus an east loop and walk-in sites. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Blue Mound State Park
note: Drive-in Family Campground; 78 wooded sites with many electric, plus a separate bike/hike-in loop (walk-in, excluded) and a rustic cabin. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Governor Dodge State Park - Cox Hollow Campground
note: Two-loop drive-in family campground with electric sites, dump station, showers, flush toilets. Part of the park's 269 standard / 80 electric sites (Cox Hollow + Twin Valley). Sites fit a 23-ft rig. Distinct from the separate Hickory Ridge equestrian camp. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Governor Dodge State Park - Twin Valley Campground
note: Large two-loop drive-in family campground with electric sites, dump station, showers, flush toilets. Lower loop is the flatter larger-rig area. Part of the 269-site / 80-electric park total. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. Distinct from the Hickory Ridge equestrian camp. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: New Glarus Woods State Park
note: Lead Circle drive-in family loop, ~18-20 sites marketed for RV/pop-up/tent (DNR). Electric very limited. No dump/fill station. Separate walk-in tent sites and group camp excluded. Standard sites fit a 23-ft rig. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Tower Hill State Park
note: Small drive-in campground; 10 sites, all non-electric, on a loop road off the parking area. Vault toilets and jug-fill water only; no flush toilets/showers. Sites are small and sloped, so the largest sites are the realistic fit for a 23-ft rig. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Lake Kegonsa State Park
note: Drive-in family campground; 96 sites, 29 with electric (incl. a full electric loop). Flush toilets/showers, vault toilets, dump station, drinking water. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. WI State Park admission sticker required. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Mirror Lake State Park - Cliffwood Campground
note: Cliffwood loop: 35 sites, ~29 with electric; open May-Oct. Many sites accommodate larger rigs. Reservable online. CAVEAT: tentatively closed in the reservation system ~Feb 1-early Aug 2026 for a road-construction project (per WI DNR) - verify before relying on it in 2026. --Claude

name: Mirror Lake State Park - Sandstone Ridge Campground
note: Sandstone Ridge loop: 68 sites, mix of electric/non-electric; open year-round (a few electric sites plowed in winter). Reservable online. CAVEAT: tentatively closed in the reservation system ~Feb 1-early Aug 2026 for a road-construction project (per WI DNR) - verify before relying on it in 2026. --Claude

name: Mirror Lake State Park - Bluewater Bay Campground
note: Bluewater Bay loop: 63 non-electric sites near the visitor center/beach; open ~mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable online. (Not affected by the 2026 Cliffwood/Sandstone Ridge construction closure.) --Claude

name: Rocky Arbor State Park
note: 89 wooded sites on paved loops; ~18 sites with 30-amp electric, rest primitive. Showers, dump station. Open Memorial Day-Labor Day. Mix of reservable and FCFS; reservable online. --Claude

name: Mill Bluff State Park
note: 21-site rustic family campground; 6 sites with electric, 15 non-electric. Vault toilets, hand-pump water, dump station, no showers. Max RV length 40 ft. Reservable online (some FCFS availability). Open late May-September. --Claude

name: Roche-A-Cri State Park
note: Rustic family campground ~40 sites (drive-in trailer/tent sites plus 4 low-amp electric and a few walk-in); max RV length 40 ft. Vault toilets, drinking water, dump station near the office. Reservable online; open late spring to early fall. --Claude

name: Buckhorn State Park
note: 69-site drive-in family campground (incl. pull-throughs) on Castle Rock Lake; 17 standard + 3 accessible electric sites, shower building, dump station. Reservable online (approx Apr-Nov). Separate from the park's ~50 cart-in/walk-in backpack sites. A 2026 road-construction project will affect some sites and the boat launch - verify. --Claude

name: Hartman Creek State Park
note: 103-site family campground (separate from the park's horse/equestrian area); many sites have electric (15/30/50A), others non-electric. Flush toilets, showers, RV dump station. Reservable online. Family campground closed Dec 1-Apr 1. --Claude

name: Wildcat Mountain State Park
note: 30-site family campground on the ridge top; drive-in sites (some electric, ~10 reported) plus walk-in/cart-in sites. Flush toilets, showers, dump station. Reservable online. Separate 24-site horse campground and 20 cart-in sites are distinct. Steep access roads up the ridge. --Claude

name: Big Foot Beach State Park
note: ~100-site campground; the upper loop is drive-in RV (gravel pads) with 34 electric sites, remainder non-electric; lower sites are walk-to tent. No water/sewer hookups; dump station, showers. Reservable online. Open seasonally ~late April-mid-October. --Claude

name: Mauthe Lake Campground - Kettle Moraine State Forest (Northern Unit)
note: Family campground in Kettle Moraine SF Northern Unit; 135 sites, 51 with electric. Drive-in, dump station, showers/flush toilets. Reservable online. Equestrian (New Prospect) and group (Greenbush) camps are separate facilities. --Claude

name: Long Lake Recreation Area Campground - Kettle Moraine State Forest (Northern Unit)
note: Family campground in Kettle Moraine SF Northern Unit on the east shore of Long Lake; ~200 sites across the 600s/700s/800s/900s loops, 600s loop has electric. Drive-in, showers/flush toilets, dump station, two beaches and a boat launch. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Ottawa Lake Campground - Kettle Moraine State Forest (Southern Unit)
note: Year-round family campground in Kettle Moraine SF Southern Unit; 100 sites, 65 with electric. Showers/flush toilets, dump station, swim beach on Ottawa Lake. Reservable online. Separate Horseriders' Campground elsewhere in the unit. --Claude

name: Pinewoods Campground - Kettle Moraine State Forest (Southern Unit)
note: Family/RV drive-in campground in Kettle Moraine SF Southern Unit; 101 sites in 3 loops, no electric. Dump station, showers, flush + vault toilets. Reservable online (roughly mid-May to mid-Oct). Separate Group Camp on-site and a separate Horseriders' Campground near Palmyra. --Claude

name: Pike Lake Campground - Kettle Moraine State Forest (Pike Lake Unit)
note: Sunrise family campground in Kettle Moraine SF Pike Lake Unit; 32 sites (24 wooded, 8 open), 11 with electric. Open early April to third weekend of October. Reservable online; unreserved sites FCFS. Showers, flush toilets, dump station. The 3 backpack sites are a separate hike-in area. --Claude

name: Whitewater Lake Campground - Kettle Moraine State Forest (Southern Unit)
note: Family drive-in campground in Kettle Moraine SF Southern Unit; 63 wooded sites in East (600s) and West (700s) loops, only ~2 electric (ADA) sites. Vault toilets, no showers; dump station on site. Open ~mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservable online. Separate from the Horseriders' Campground and Hickory Woods Group Camp. --Claude

name: Richard Bong State Recreation Area
note: Wisconsin state recreation area near Kansasville. Two family campgrounds, Sunrise (east, pinned) and Sunset (west); 217 total sites, 54 with electric. Reservable online. Shower buildings, flush/vault toilets, dump station. Separate group sites, an ATV/off-highway area, horse trails, and a dog-training area are distinct from the family loops. --Claude

name: Harrington Beach State Park
note: 69-unit family campground (31 sites with electric, plus non-electric, 5 walk-in sites, group site, accessible cabin); modern showers/flush toilets, dump station. Reservable online. Paved roads suitable for large rigs. Open roughly May-Oct. --Claude

name: High Cliff State Park
note: 112-site family campground, 32 sites with electric, plus 2 accessible; separate group camp and equestrian area. Flush toilets/showers (May-mid-Oct), vault toilets year-round, dump station. Reservable online; 2-night minimum in season. --Claude

name: Governor Thompson State Park
note: 100 drive-in family sites in three loops (East/North/South); 16 electric sites in the East Loop (the only electric), plus 2 accessible. Central shower/flush-toilet building, vault toilets, dump/fill station. Max RV length ~50 ft. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Council Grounds State Park
note: 52 drive-in family sites, 19 with electric (30-amp); modern showers, flush toilets, dump station (services mid-April-Sept 30; campground open year-round). Separate 3-site group campground. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Perrot State Park
note: 102 single-family drive-in sites, many with electric; flush toilets, showers, dump station (in season), water stations (no site hookups). Sites accommodate big rigs to ~45 ft. Open year-round (winter is hike-in without services). Reservable online. Separate walk-in group sites. --Claude

name: Interstate State Park
note: Camp Interstate / North campground (Wisconsin's oldest state park): ~40 family sites, ~34 with electric (30/50-amp), plus accessible and walk-to sites; showers, flush + vault toilets, dump station. Open May 1-Oct 31 (rustic South campground year-round). Reservable online. --Claude

name: Willow River State Park
note: Drive-in family campground on the south shore of Little Falls Lake; ~150+ sites across the 100/200/300 loops with ~59 electric, showers/flush toilets. Largest sites take big rigs; 23-ft fits easily. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Lake Wissota State Park
note: Wooded family campground; 116 sites, 58 with 50-amp electric, mostly back-in plus a few pull-throughs, showers/flush toilets/dump station. 23-ft fits comfortably. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Brunet Island State Park
note: Two drive-in family loops: South Campground (24 sites, all 30-amp electric, inland) and North Campground (45 sites, no electric, many riverfront). 23-ft fits either. Pinned to the electric South loop. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Governor Knowles State Forest - St. Croix Family Campground
