note: Iron County Forestry & Parks campground, renovated 2009. 14 sites: 10 paved electric (30 amp) + 4 walk-in tent; restrooms/showers, dump station, ATV/UTV wash, pavilion, boat ramp, ATV trail access. Listed big-rig friendly. Open ~May 15-Oct 15; reservations open Jan 1, reservable online (Campspot). (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Weber Lake County Park
note: Iron County Forestry & Parks campground on Weber Lake (61-ac slow-no-wake trout/panfish lake). 11 sites: 9 electric + 2 non-electric; water, showers/flush toilets, boat launch, swimming area, playground. Reservable online (Campspot), reservations open Jan 1; off-season phone 715-561-2697. Sites reported tight/rustic and better for smaller rigs — a 23-ft rig should verify a fitting site before relying on it. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Twin Bear Campground
note: Bayfield County Forestry & Parks campground on a peninsula between Twin Bear Lake and Eagle Lake (Pike Chain of Lakes). 43 sites, ~40 electric; dump station, pit toilets, showers, boat launch, fishing pier, swimming beach, playground, wifi. Official site map lists max camper sizes 25-40 ft (e.g. sites 16/30/35 ~35-40 ft) — fits 23 ft easily. Reservable by phone (715-209-8423 / 715-372-8610). (RV Life 3.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Delta Lake Campground
note: Bayfield County Forestry & Parks campground on Delta Lake, 62020 Delta Park Rd (7 mi S of Iron River then 6 mi E on Scenic Dr). 34 tent/RV sites with electric (20/30/50-amp) and non-electric, showers, vault toilets, dump station, boat launch, swimming beach. Site map lists max camper sizes 30-36 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits. Reservable by phone. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Moon Lake Park
note: Town of Iron River municipal campground on 41-acre Moon Lake (operated by the Town, not a city). North and South loops; electric on most sites, water in campground (not per-site), no sewer; sand beach, boat ramp, dump station, ATV-trail access. Season ~mid-May to mid-Oct. Reservations online. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Harnischfeger Park
note: Dodge County park (NOT Jefferson County) along the Rock River in the Town of Lebanon, W3048 Crawfish Rd, ~8 mi E of Watertown. 8 numbered sites with water + 50-amp electric (lettered tent sites no hookups), 1 non-electric group site; flush toilets, showers. Reservable online (Premier Campground) or by phone. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Jefferson County Fair Park Campground
note: Despite the name, run by the Jefferson County Fairgrounds Association, a non-profit (NOT local government), in Jefferson, WI. 50+ sites: full hookup, partial (power+water), and tent; most powered sites 20-amp, a few 30-amp, no 50-amp. Open to the general public seasonally (reopens Apr 15; winter limited to full-hookup/walk-in), CLOSED during County Fair week in Aug. Online booking required (ParkWithUs); the 360-385-1013 number is the booking line, not a local office. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$$) --Claude

name: DuBay Park
note: Portage County park on 600-acre Lake DuBay (Wisconsin River reservoir), 4501 County Rd E, Junction City (~13 mi NW of Stevens Point). 31 campsites, all with electric; water, two shower buildings, dump station, boat ramp, sand beach. Open May 1-Oct 31; reservations online only. Distinct from the private Lake DuBay Shores Campground nearby. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Big Falls County Park & Campground
note: Operated by Price County (NOT Rusk County), near Kennan on the Jump River at Big Falls. 6 rustic non-electric sites, vault toilets, hand-pump well water; $20/night, open year-round (winter roads unplowed), FCFS (shelter reservable separately). (RV Life unrated, $) --Claude

name: La Farge Village Park
note: Operated by the Village of La Farge, on West Adams St at the north end of this Kickapoo River town. 16 self-service tent/RV sites with electric (new 50-amp hookups), dump station nearby, showers, potable water; $20 RV / $10 tent, FCFS. Sites are smaller (won't fit a 40-ft motorhome with slides) but accommodate a 23-ft rig. (RV Life 4.0 star, $) --Claude

name: Big Bay Town Park
note: Owned/operated by the Town of La Pointe on Madeline Island (Lake Superior), ~6 mi from the La Pointe ferry. 61 sites; 22 drive-in electric sites (#34-55, 20/30/50-amp), rest non-electric; sites 56-61 are hike-in (excluded). Restrooms, showers, potable water. Reservable online. Distinct from adjacent Big Bay STATE Park. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Ravine Park Campground
note: Operated by the Village of Lake Nebagamon (Douglas County), at 11505 Camp Nebagamon Dr near the public boat landing at the lake's south end. 10 rustic sites (6 electric, 4 non-electric), flush toilets, no dump station; $30 electric / $15 tent, Memorial Day-Labor Day, FCFS via self-registration. (RV Life unrated, $) --Claude

name: William G. Lunney Lake Farm County Park
note: Operated by Dane County Parks, south Madison off Lake Farm Rd near Lake Waubesa. 54 sites; 39 electric (50-amp), rest non-electric; showers, wi-fi, dump station. Reservable online (reservedane.com); some walk-in FCFS. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Columbia Park
note: Operated by Fond du Lac County (Planning & Parks) on Lake Winnebago / Calumet Harbor at N10340 Calumet Harbor Rd, Malone (~10 mi NE of Fond du Lac). 40-unit modern campground, all electric (20/30/50-amp), restrooms, RV/boat dump stations, marina/docking. Reservable online (~May-Oct). (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Manitowoc County Expo Center
note: Manitowoc County Expo (county fairgrounds) at 4921 Expo Drive. Real overnight RV camping, not events-only: ~91 elec/water sites (Midway asphalt 54, North gravel 31, Vista grass 6). Water service May 1-Oct 15; camping May 1-Jul 31 and Sep 1-30 (grounds close Aug for county fair). FCFS, reservations not required; showers/restrooms. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Marinette City Park Campground
note: Operated by the City of Marinette (booked via the Community REC Center); distinct from Marinette County Parks. 9 sites with 20-amp electric and potable water, no dump station; shaded, playground, frisbee golf. Reservable by phone or online via the city RecDesk portal. (RV Life 4.0 star, $) --Claude

name: Juneau County Castle Rock Park
note: Juneau County Parks & Forestry park on Castle Rock Lake, ~12 mi N of Mauston near Lyndon Station. ~300 sites, 200+ with electric; showers/flush toilets, dump station, swimming beach, boat landing. Reservable online; electric $25 ($30 premium), non-electric $20. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Babcock County Park
note: Dane County Parks campground on the east side of Lake Waubesa at the Yahara River outflow, near McFarland off US-51. 25-unit campground, electric to all sites; bathhouse/showers, dump station, boat launch, fishing pier. Open ~late Apr-mid Oct. Reservable online or by phone. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Medford City Park Campground
note: Operated by the City of Medford (Taylor County) within the 100-acre City Park along the Black River millpond. 9 sites (4 at 40 ft, 5 at 36 ft); west side 30-50 amp electric, east side 120V; water both sides; dump at the wastewater plant. Open May 1-Sep 30, 14-day max, FCFS, no reservations. (RV Life 4.5 star, $) --Claude

name: Melrose City Park
note: Village of Melrose city park campground in Melrose (Jackson County), along the Black River. Has electric sites incl. 50-amp service; tents allowed. Small in-town park; FCFS/self-register typical for a village park (no online reservation system found). (RV Life 3.5 star, $). --Claude

name: Lake of the Falls County Park
note: Iron County park on the Turtle River between Lake of the Falls and the Turtle-Flambeau Flowage; 32 sites across 3 camping areas, electric on Side 1, lakeside sites on Side 2, showers/flush toilets on Side 3; open ~May 1-Oct 15; reservable online (Campspot). Boat landing. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Camp New Wood County Park
note: Lincoln County park ~10 mi NW of Merrill along the Wisconsin River (Hwy 107), site of a 1930s CCC camp; ~7 sites, hand water pump, pit toilets, boat landing; primitive (no electric). FCFS, $20/night (Lincoln County added online reservations for some parks — verify Camp New Wood). Ice Age Trail / Great Pinery Heritage Waterway. --Claude

name: Russell Memorial Park and Campground
note: Clark County park (NOT Jackson) on the shore of Lake Arbutus, W8180 South Rd, Merrillan; full-hookup (30-amp electric/water/sewer) plus electric-only and primitive sites, dump station, 3 shower houses, boat launch; RVs up to ~25 ft; reservable online May 1-Sep 30, season May-Nov, $24-37/night. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: East Arbutus County Park
note: Jackson County park on the east shore of Lake Arbutus, W7898 Duckhill Rd, Hatfield; 187 campsites (136 electric), ~70 sites by the lake, plus 38 ATV sites (26 electric); showers, dump station, boat launch, swimming beach; reservable online via Jackson County Parks. (RV Life 3 star, $$). --Claude

name: West Arbutus County Park
note: Jackson County park on the west shore of Lake Arbutus just north of the dam, N9548 Thunderbird Ln, Hatfield; 44 campsites (36 electric, 8 non-electric), 15 on the lakeshore; flush toilets, showers, boat launch, swimming beach; reservable online via Jackson County Parks, 3-night min. --Claude

name: Lake Mendota County Park
note: Dane County's Mendota County Park, Town of Westport near Middleton (5130 County Hwy M); 27-30 reservable campsites each with 30-amp electric, showers, dump station; separate 5-unit boat-in area; shallow paddle-craft launch, swimming area; reservable online (reservedane.com). (RV Life 4 star, $$). --Claude

name: Iowa County Fairgrounds
note: Iowa County Fair Association grounds, 815 Fair St, Mineral Point; open for overnight RV camping whenever the fairgrounds are not in use (self check-in), some electric/water hookups; no camping during fair week (early Sept). Camping arranged via fairgrounds form/contact. (RV Life ~4-5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Totogatic Park
note: Washburn County park on the Minong Flowage, N13984 Tota Dr, Minong (~7 mi W of Minong); 105 sites, most electric (30/50-amp), some with water/sewer, nearly half with direct flowage access; sandy beach, boat ramp, showers, dump stations; all sites reservable online; season ~May 1-Nov 30. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Mondovi Tourist Park
note: City of Mondovi 'Tourist Park' (Buffalo County); 6 campsites with electric & water hookups, near the Buffalo River; FCFS, $20/night, 7-day max, season May-Oct 1. Small in-town city campground (city-operated, not county). --Claude

name: Green County Fairgrounds
note: Green County Fairgrounds, 2600 10th St, Monroe; ~100 campsites, most with 30-amp electric and many with water; seasonal camping first Sat of May through last Sat of Sept (no camping during fair week in late July); FCFS self-registration (in-person office or drop box), $30/night. (RV Life 4 star, $$). --Claude

name: Big Eau Pleine County Park
note: Marathon County park on a peninsula in the Big Eau Pleine Reservoir, 3301 Eau Pleine Park Rd, Mosinee; two campgrounds (West + South), ~106 sites, ~60 with electric; boat landings, fishing; mix of reservable and FCFS sites; season May 1-Oct 31; sites $18 (non-electric, no lake view) to $23 (lakefront electric). --Claude

name: Sanders Park Campground
note: Racine County park, 4703 Wood Rd, Mt Pleasant; 19-site campground, largest sites fit a 45-ft rig; fire pit & picnic table per site, no on-site dump (may use Cliffside dump); reservations required; season 2nd weekend Apr-2nd weekend Oct. (RV Life 4 star, $$). --Claude

name: Chute Pond Park
note: Oconto County park on 418-acre Chute Pond, just S of Mountain on Hwy 32/64; 72 reservable daily sites (84-100 are ATV/UTV sites), 30-amp electric on many sites, no water hookups; boat access, beach; reservable online up to a year ahead; $20-30/night. --Claude

name: Victoria Riverside Park and Campground
note: Village of Muscoda city park on the Wisconsin River (Grant County); 45 electric campsites plus 16 seasonal full-hookup (electric/water/sewer) sites and a primitive tent area; boat launch (2 ramps), bathhouse w/ showers, dump station; reservable by phone/email; season ~May-Oct. (RV Life 3 star, $$). --Claude

name: Muskego Park
note: Waukesha County park on a swimming/fishing pond W of Muskego. 17 family sites (9 electric) + 3 group sites; beach, hiking. Reservable online via county system, open ~May-Oct. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Wilderness County Park
note: Juneau County park on the 23,000-acre Petenwell Flowage, ~300 acres. 127 sites, 107 electric; bathhouse w/ showers, dump station, boat launch, swim beach. Reservable; office open Memorial-Labor Day, self-register otherwise. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Snyder County Park & Campground
note: Clark County Forestry & Parks campground on 28-acre Snyder Lake. 35 sites all with electric; boat launch, fishing, heated showers, paved roads; direct ATV trail access. Reservable May 1-Sep 30, self-register off-season. (RV Life unrated, $). --Claude

name: Morris-Erickson County Park
note: Chippewa County park on the W shore of Long Lake (28 sites on ~5 acres of frontage), addr 28561 WI-40. 20/30/50A electric on all sites; swimming, fishing, playground. Reservable Mar-Oct via county system. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Southworth County Park
note: Barron County (Southworth Memorial) park, 17 acres on the E side of Ten Mile Lake near Chetek. 50A hookups; boat landing/docks, fish cleaning house, dump station, coin showers. Reservable online, open May 1-Sep 30; some monthly sites but takes nightly transients. (RV Life 1.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Bay Shore Park Campground
note: Brown County park (RV Life mis-tagged commercial) on the W shore of Green Bay, ~15 mi NE of Green Bay on WI-57. ~117 wooded sites: 85 W/E, 30 rustic, 2 group; asphalt pads, showers, dump, boat launch. FCFS + online reservations, open May-Oct. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Riverside Park
note: City of New Lisbon park along the Lemonweir River, ~50 sites with 20/30A electric + water (no sewer); some riverfront sites. Showers, laundry, dump station, boat launch, pond, playground. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Kennedy Park Juneau County
note: Juneau County park on the Lemonweir River within a 200-acre county forest, 3 mi N of New Lisbon. Rustic camping only (no electric), $15/night, 14-night max; pit toilets, playground, small boat launch. FCFS self-register, not reservable. (RV Life 5.0 star, $). --Claude

name: Hatfield City Park
note: City of New Richmond campground (1321 Jerry Frey Dr) beside the Willow River mill pond. 20 sites: 1-16 full hookup (W/S/50A), 17-20 electric-only; WiFi, dump station. No campfires. Reservable online, open May 1-Oct 15. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Morgan Park
note: Marinette County park on the shore of Timms Lake, NE Marinette County (addr 19882 Timms Lake Rd). 45 electric sites; heated shower bldg, swim beach, boat landing, hiking, overlook. Reservable online per-site, open May 1-Nov 30. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Holtwood Campground
note: City of Oconto park, 68 acres with ~4,000 ft frontage on the Oconto River, 30 min N of Green Bay. 152 sites (15/30/50A) + 9 primitive; showers, laundry, dump/pump-out, store, boat launch. Takes nightly transients + seasonal. Open May-Oct. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: North Bay Shore County Park
note: Oconto County park (RV Life mis-tagged commercial) on the W shore of Green Bay N of Oconto (addr 500 Bay Rd). 34 daily sites + group site; ~6 paved, rest grassy/gravel, all level. Reservable online (2-night min). (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Ojibwa Park
note: Town of Ojibwa park (transferred to township 1990), 366 acres bordered by the Chippewa River and Tuscobia State Trail. 16 RV/camper sites (most electric) + 3 tent; paved pads, water, pit toilets, dump station. (RV Life 5.0 star, $). --Claude

name: Crowley Park
note: City of Owen park (Crowley Campground & Mill Pond Park), Clark County. Flat mixed open/wooded sites; FCFS, $18/night or $120/week, book via City Hall. (RV Life 2.5 star, $). --Claude

name: Smith Lake County Park and Campground
note: Price County park, 300 acres on Smith Lake / N Fork Flambeau River, 3 mi W of Park Falls. 30-unit campground, all sites electric, fits RVs 45+ ft; boat launch, showers (Mem Day-Sep). Some sites reservable online, rest FCFS; open year-round. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Hines Park Campground
note: City of Park Falls park on the E bank of the N Fork Flambeau River, within city limits. 9 paved full-hookup sites (W/S, 20/30/50A) fitting RVs 45+ ft + adjacent tent area; 2 fishing piers, boat launch, playground. FCFS, not reservable, open May-early Oct. (RV Life 4.5 star, $). --Claude

name: Peshtigo Badger Park Campground
note: City of Peshtigo park; 42-site campground in the SW portion of the 60-acre park along ~3,000 ft of Peshtigo River frontage. 35 RV sites with water+electric, 2 cabins, 4 rustic tent sites, 1 group site; showers/flush toilets, dump station. Open ~May-Oct, reservable online (Firefly). ~$27/night. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Solberg Lake County Park
note: Price County park on a 35-acre peninsula in Solberg Lake; ~60 sites (Solberg unit) plus a 40-site addition with water/sewer, all with electric (30/50 amp). 20+ lakeside sites, sand beach, paved boat ramp, showers/flush toilets, dump station. Open year-round; sites 1-9 & 43-60 reservable online, 10-42 FCFS. Max RV ~40 ft. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Chain-O-Lakes Campground
note: City of Phillips campground on Elk Lake; 20 sites all with electric, potable water, coin showers, beach, 8-boat dock. ~$30/night. FCFS only - cannot be reserved. Season not stated (summer). (RV Life 3.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Dexter Park Campground
note: Wood County park 6 mi S of Pittsville on 298-acre Lake Dexter (1,235-acre park). 109 individual sites incl. non-electric, electric, designated waterfront sites, 4 full-hookup (Loop 1), 10 pull-through ATV sites, 3 group sites; beach, showers, fishing docks. Reservable online up to 1 year ahead. 2026 fees $22 non-elec to $40 full-hookup. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Sherwood Park
note: Clark County park (NOT Wood County despite Pittsville mailing address W1251 Dyer Rd, Pittsville). 27 remote wooded sites with electric on Sherwood Lake; boat launch, swimming, shower building w/ flush toilets, vault toilets, RV dump, disc golf. Reservable online May 1-Sep 30 (self-registration off-season). $24-30/night. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Merlin Lambert County Park
note: Jackson County Forestry & Parks (NOT Wood County); ~12 mi N of Warrens on Potters Flowage. 30 rustic wooded sites, no electric hookups; water pump, pit toilets, boat launch, picnic area, ATV access. Open year-round, FCFS with self-registration box. (RV Life unrated) --Claude

name: Nugget Lake County Park Campground
note: Pierce County park near Plum City on a 116-acre lake (electric motors only). 50 campsites with 30/50-amp electric plus 5 cabins; modern comfort station w/ showers, dump station, water fill, boat ramp, fishing pier. No swimming beach. Open ~April 1-Nov 30. Reservable online via CampLife. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Pioneer Park
note: Village of Prairie Farm (Barron County) park on the Prairie Farm Flowage / Hay River. Small rustic municipal campground; The Dyrt lists electric hookups + a dump station, others describe it as a basic open camping area accommodating tents/trailers/small RVs. Restrooms, potable water, fishing, ATV-trail access. Largely FCFS/low-key; call the village office to confirm. (RV Life unrated/$). NOTE: aggregators (Travel WI/snoflo) cross-contaminate this listing with a different 'Pioneer Park' in Ogema (Ogema Millpond, Pine Line Rail Trail, equestrian) - that amenity text does NOT belong to Prairie Farm. --Claude

name: Cliffside County Park
note: Racine County park (233 acres) in the Village of Caledonia, 7320 Michna Rd. 92 back-in sites, all with electric + water, accommodating rigs to ~45 ft; dump station, showers, playfields. Open ~2nd weekend of April-Oct 31. Half the sites (1-41) held for walk-ins FCFS; rest reservable online via Real Racine (~7 days ahead, $12 res fee). (RV Life 4.5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Pier Natural Bridge County Park
note: Richland County park along the Pine River near Rockbridge/Richland Center (10 acres). 6 rustic campsites suitable for tents and small RVs/trailers, pit toilets, shelters, kayak launch. FCFS only - camping permit (minor fee) from the adjacent Natural Bridge Store. (RV Life unrated/$). Small/tight - a 23-ft rig likely fits at least one site but confirm before relying on it. --Claude

name: Hoffman Park Campground
note: City of River Falls campground. 15 RV sites with electric (rigs to ~43 ft) plus 5 grass tent sites; restrooms, showers, water fill, dump station. No campfires permitted. Open ~April 15-Oct 15. Reservable online (electric $25, tent $15; full prepayment required), camp host on site. (RV Life 5 star, $$). --Claude

name: Collins Park
note: Portage County park (21 wooded acres) ~3 mi SW of Rosholt, next to 42-acre Collins Lake. ~27-28 campsites, all with electric ($8/night add-on); picnic lodge, boat ramp, showers, dump station, small swimming beach. Open ~May 15-Sep 30. About half the sites reservable online (7 days-11 months ahead), rest FCFS. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Potato River Falls County Forest Campground
note: Iron County Forest, near Gurney/Saxon. 5 rustic FCFS sites (free, 14-day limit) at the dead-end of Falls Road (1.5 mi good gravel off Hwy 169); pavilion, vault toilet, no potable water. Mostly tent/small-RV but at least one site fits a larger rig, so a 23-ft EKKO works. No reservations — FCFS. Set in a wooded gorge on the Potato River with stair trails to upper/lower falls. --Claude

name: Saxon Harbor Campground & Marina
note: Iron County (only WI county park on Lake Superior), at 10045 County Hwy A, Saxon. ~26-31 RV sites with water and 20/30/50-amp electric, plus ~5 walk-in tent sites; restrooms/showers, dump station, beach, marina (81 slips). Season ~May 15-Oct 15; reservable online via Campspot (opens Jan 1). RV Life mislabels this commercial — operator is Iron County. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Shawano Lake County Park Campground
note: Shawano County Parks, W5785 N Lake Dr, Shawano. ~100 sites — 91-92 with water and 50-amp electric (no sewer), 8 tent-only electric sites, 1 ADA site; dump station, showers, ~800 ft of Shawano Lake shoreline with sand beach and boat launch. Open ~May-Oct; reservable online up to 12 months ahead. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Shell Lake Municipal Campground
note: City of Shell Lake (municipal), 200 W Lake Dr. 47 sites, all with water and 30/50-amp electric; sewer on sites #1-38; 12 pull-thru sites (#13-24) and 12 sites on the lake. Showers, dump station, swim beach, boat launch. Reservable online via the Washburn County Parks system; water may be shut off in cold months. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Beauford T. Anderson Campground
note: Operated by the Village of Soldiers Grove (Crawford County); 35 electric+water sites plus tent camping, dump station, showers; big rigs and pull-thrus noted. FCFS, no reservations - passes bought at the Village Office or Campbell's One Stop. Season opens late April. Electric $25, non-electric $23/night. On the Kickapoo River. (RV Life 5.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Lucius Woods County Park
note: Douglas County Forestry-operated county park on Upper St. Croix Lake at Solon Springs (despite the 'State Park' name in some directories, it is a Douglas County park). ~32 sites: 23 electric/water, 9 non-electric; showers, flush toilets, dump station, swimming beach. Season ~two Fridays before Memorial Day to after Labor Day; reservable online (PremierCampground) starting Feb 1, $5 reservation fee. Electric $25, electric+water $30, non-electric $20/night. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Otter Lake County Park
note: Chippewa County Parks-operated park in the Chippewa County Forest, ~7.5 mi north of Stanley (35337 170th Ave). 22 drive-in sites, all with 20/30/50-amp electric; 15 sites have their own boat dock. Fill station on-site (sanitary dump shared via Chapman Park in Stanley). Season ~first week of May to late October; reservable online through mid-Sept, FCFS self-registration thereafter. $20/night + $7 electric + $5 dock site. (RV Life 3.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Jordan Park Campground
note: Portage County Parks campground 2 mi NE of Stevens Point on 85-acre Jordan Pond/Plover River. 22 sites, all electric (30/50 amp) except 3 canoe walk-in sites; $8/night electric. Showers, dump station, boat ramp, swimming beach. Open May 1-Oct 31. Reservable online (must book 7+ days in advance). (RV Life 5.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Stockholm Village Park Campground
note: Village of Stockholm park campground on Lake Pepin (Mississippi River). 23 sites with 50-amp electric (count reduced during high water); no water hookup at sites (potable water on-site, arrive with tank filled). Privy toilets, swimming beach, boat ramp, 700' fishing pier. Open seasonally ~April-Oct (April limited by flood risk). FCFS, no reservations; fee envelope/drop box at entrance. (RV Life 3.0 star, $) --Claude

name: Goose Island Campground
note: La Crosse County Parks campground 3 mi S of La Crosse on the Mississippi River - largest county campground in WI, ~300+ sites. Electric + water sites; no sewer; newer paved back-in/pull-thru sites in the south lanes handle large rigs (up to ~42 ft). Showers, laundry, dump station, camp store, swimming beach, 3 boat launches. Open mid-April-Oct. Reservable online or by phone. RV Life mis-tags it commercial; it is county-operated. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Crystal Lake Campground
note: Village of Strum (Trempealeau County) municipal campground, 42 sites with water + 50-amp electric + cable, some full-hookup; dump station, showers. Open ~mid-May to October. Reservable by phone/email through the campground manager (no online portal, not on rec.gov). On Crystal Lake (dammed Buffalo River impoundment) with a boat landing. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Harbour Village Resort
note: Large private Door County resort campground/waterpark (~300 acres) in Carlsville. Public nightly RV sites bookable online (wooded/open pull-through + back-in, electric + water, dump station for sewer; no full hookups). Also runs UDI/condo and seasonal memberships, but transient public camping is confirmed across independent sources. Open ~mid-May to mid-Oct. Inland off Hwy 42 with on-site swim/fishing ponds + waterpark, not on Green Bay. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Brown County Reforestation Camp Campground
note: Brown County Parks (E.J. Smith Reforestation Camp), Suamico. New 51-site campground opened 2022: 15 full-hookup (50-amp), 24 water + 30-amp electric, 8 rustic, plus walk-in tent/group/horse sites; gravel pads, dump station. 14-ft entrance height clearance (not a length limit). Open ~May to October. Reservable online via WebTrac only (no FCFS). (RV Life 4.5 star, $$$) --Claude

name: Josie Creek County Park
note: Rusk County Parks campground near Tony, 22 sites with 20/30-amp electric (one pedestal shared per 2 sites) and water at sites 1-12; dump station, no sewer. RV pads ~34x14 ft, vehicles to 35 ft. On the Dairyland Flowage (South Fork Flambeau River impoundment) with a boat launch; also an on-site shooting/archery range. Electric year-round, water off ~mid-Oct. Reservable online 7+ days ahead or FCFS. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$) --Claude

name: Trego Town Park Campground
note: Town of Trego (Washburn County) municipal campground on the Namekagon River at W5665 Trego Park Rd. 50 sites: 25 seasonal, 21 daily RV, 4 tent; RV/seasonal sites have full electric/water/septic hookups (20/30/50 amp), tent sites water+20amp. Dump station, showers, flush toilets, pavilion. Season May 1-Sep 30. Reservable online via Campspot or by phone. One review: very clean, easy to back the camper in. (RV Life 3.0 star, $$). --Claude

name: Turtle Lake Village Park
note: Village of Turtle Lake municipal park (RV Life lists it as 'Turtle Lake City Park'). 6 paved pull-through campsites with electric + water hookups; dump station on the NW/Pine St side. $25/night, FCFS (not reservable per Village page). Contact Village Hall x3. Aggregator claims of 'full hookups, RVs to 50ft, reservable' conflict with the official 6-site FCFS facts; official wins. (RV Life 5.0 star, $). --Claude

name: Village Inn on the Lake Hotel & RV Park
note: Private hotel + transient RV park at 3310 Memorial Dr, Two Rivers, on Lake Michigan. Pull-through RV sites with water, 20/30/50 amp electric, and sewer; laundry, showers, fire pits, pool. Open to overnight RV guests; book by phone or online. Adjacent to Mariners/Ice Age Trail. (RV Life 4.0 star, $$$). --Claude

name: Upson Community Park
note: Community park at Upson (Iron County) on the Potato River near Upson Falls. 31 campsites, 10 with electric (20/30/50 amp incl. a pull-through), rest primitive; flush toilets, hot showers, pavilion, firewood, trout fishing. Electric $28/night ($168/wk); non-electric donation/$23. FCFS; drive-in. Firsthand review: 'amazingly well kept... beautiful campground.' Closes end of October. Phone not verified - omitted. (RV Life unrated). --Claude

name: Sidie Hollow County Park Campground
note: Vernon County park near Viroqua; ~74 campsites mixing rustic tent, electric, and full-hookup RV sites around a 38-acre lake with trout streams and boat launch. Open ~April-October. Reservable online via Campspot (2-night minimum on advance bookings); some FCFS. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$$) --Claude

name: Vernon County Fairgrounds Campground
note: Vernon County Fair Association campground at the county fairgrounds, Viroqua; 56 electric sites (50-amp available), two shower buildings, dump station. Open ~May-October except the week of the Vernon County Fair (a real season-long campground, not event-only). FCFS/contact office; flat grassy fairground sites fit large RVs. (RV Life 3 star, $) --Claude

name: McMullen Memorial County Park
note: Monroe County Forestry & Parks campground at Warrens on 50-acre Lake Wazeda (county's first park, est. 1958); sites 1-88 have on-site electric (50/30/20-amp) and water, sites 89-94 are walk-in primitive tent. 39 reservable + 49 FCFS. Two shower buildings, fishing pier, boat launch. RV Life mislabels it commercial but it is county-operated. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Thompson's West End Park Campground
note: City of Washburn municipal campground on Lake Superior's Chequamegon Bay; 50 paved-road sites all with electric (30/50-amp), flush toilets, pay showers, artesian water, dump/pump-out station, swimming beach and boat launch (17 sites are seasonal). Strictly FCFS — no reservations; self-pay kiosk. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Memorial Park Campground
note: City of Washburn municipal campground on a bluff over Chequamegon Bay (Lake Superior). ~51 sites, most on gravel access roads with 30/50-amp electric, cable TV, drinking water, flush toilets, pay showers. FCFS only via self-serve fee kiosk (no reservations for municipal campgrounds). Campendium-reported rigs up to 28 ft. (RV Life 5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Waupun County Park
note: Fond du Lac County park (103 acres) on NW edge of Waupun. 38-unit wooded family campground plus group area, 30-amp electric, restrooms/showers. $28/night + $6 reservation fee; reservable online at fdlcountyparks.com. Campendium-reported rigs up to 34 ft. (RV Life 4 star, $$) --Claude

name: Evergreen Park & Campground
note: Village of Wausaukee park (~40 acres) on NE edge of village (North Ave). 40 sites: deluxe full-hookup (incl. pull-throughs) and rustic electric sites; new ADA restrooms, coin showers. ~$25-39/night; reservable online via Campspot. Wausaukee River (Class 2 trout stream) and Evergreen Park Pond on site. (RV Life 3.5 star, $$$) --Claude

name: Veterans Memorial Campground
note: La Crosse County park on Hwy 16, 1 mi W of West Salem along the La Crosse River. 100+ sites with electric and water hookups, sanitary dump station, restrooms/showers, canoe landing, La Crosse River State Trail access. Season ~Apr 15-Oct 15; primarily FCFS (county states reservations not accepted/needed), though limited online reservations exist via WebTrac; long stays by phone (608-789-8599). (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Audie Lake Campground
note: Rusk County Parks campground within the Rusk County Forest, on man-made Audie Flowage ~12 mi NW of Bruce. 15 sites; shared pedestals (one 30A + one 20A split between two sites), water at pumphouse (off ~mid-Oct), no dump station on site, ATV parking. Reservable online (7+ days ahead) else FCFS. Fishing/kayaking on the flowage. (RV Life 5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Rock Dam County Park
note: Clark County Forestry & Parks campground in the Clark County Forest on Rock Dam Lake (Town of North Foster), W10666 Camp Globe Rd, Willard. Drive-in RV camping with paved roads, electric and full RV hookup sites, 2 shower houses, dump station, boat launch, flush toilets. Sites ~$20-36/night. Reservable online (Clark County WebTrac/myvscloud) May 1-Sep 30; self-registration FCFS off-season with limited facilities. ATV/UTV trail access. (RV Life 5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Wild Rock County Park
note: Clark County Forestry & Parks campground in the Clark County Forest (Town of South Foster), N7171 Bald Peak Ave, Willard. 27 drive-in campsites with electric and water service to many sites, sanitary dump station, potable water, vault toilets, playground, shelter, day-use area; $24-30/night, reservable online via Clark County WebTrac. Open seasonally (general public welcome year-round w/ self-registration off-season). Horse-oriented (picket lines, manure bunkers, 40+ mi horse trail) but a general drive-in campground that also serves non-equestrian RVers; ATV/UTV access. Confirmed via Clark County official park page. (RV Life unrated) --Claude

name: Village of Wilton Campground
note: Owned/operated by the Village of Wilton (municipal), on the SE side of town next to the Kickapoo River along the Elroy-Sparta State Trail. ~45 sites: sites 1-27 with electric ($35/night for RVs), 28-45 tent-only ($20). Accepts pull-behinds, truck campers, motorhomes, 5th wheels. Restrooms w/ free hot showers, dump station, laundry, hand-pump water; adjacent village pool. Open May 1-Nov 1; primarily FCFS with self-pay box, some online payment. (RV Life 4 star, $$) --Claude

name: South Wood County Park
note: Wood County Parks & Forestry campground on Lake Wazeecha, 6411 South Park Rd, Wisconsin Rapids (~5 mi E of town). 73 drive-in sites (66 electric 30/50-amp, 8 non-electric, 8 waterfront, 1 accessible); no individual water/sewer but dump station and drinking water; blacktop pads, showers, two swim beaches (White/Red Sands), boat ramp, disc golf, trails. Reservable online up to 1 year ahead via Wood County reservation portal. Non-electric ~$22, electric ~$27, waterfront ~$30. (RV Life 4.5 star, $$) --Claude

name: Ely Lake Campground (Allegan State Game Area)
note: Rustic campground, 73 primitive sites (vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric) on no-motor Ely Lake within the DNR Allegan State Game Area; 16 sites open to equestrian camping but it is a general drive-in campground, not equestrian-only. State game-area land (DNR Wildlife Division); day-to-day operation and reservations run through Allegan County Parks (ReserveAmerica, 5 business days ahead) plus FCFS walk-ins. ~$17/night. Narrow internal roads make it tight for long rigs but a 23-ft RV fits. --Claude

name: Fort Custer Recreation Area Campground
note: Modern campground, 219 electric sites (20/30-amp, a few 50-amp; no full hookups) in two loops (Hilltop, Lakeview) beside Eagle Lake with a swim beach and boat rentals; sanitation station between the loops. Sites vary; a few fit up to ~45-ft RVs so a 23-ft rig is no problem. Open year-round; reservable online up to 6 months ahead. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Baraga State Park Campground
note: Modern campground, 95 grassy sites in north and south loops; all sites electric (some 50-amp / full hookups), modern restrooms with showers, sanitation station. Sits on the west side of US-41 just south of Baraga; Keweenaw Bay (Lake Superior) is across the highway via three walkways. Open year-round; reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Bay City State Park Campground
note: Modern campground, ~193 grassy sites in a mature-oak stand with electric (some 50-amp). Sits inland across State Park Drive from the Saginaw Bay (Lake Huron) day-use beach. Reservation-only; reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Brimley State Park Campground
note: Modern campground, 237 sites all electric (48 with 50-amp, rest 20/30-amp) on Whitefish Bay (Lake Superior) with ~2,000 ft of sandy shoreline. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Albert E. Sleeper State Park Campground
note: Modern campground, 226 sites (~223 electric, 30/50-amp) in east and west loops with a sanitation station. Campground is across the park road from the Saginaw Bay (Lake Huron) beach; west loop nearer the beach. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Van Riper State Park Campground
note: Modern campground, 147 electric sites (mix of 30/50-amp, large sites handling rigs to ~85 ft) plus a separate 40-site rustic campground, on Lake Michigamme. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Fisherman's Island State Park Campground
note: Rustic campground, 80 sites in two loops (north/south) along Lake Michigan; no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump water. A few walk-in tent-only sites exist but most loop sites are drive-in and fit a 23-ft rig (compact RVs up to ~35 ft). Open ~April-Nov; reservable online May 1-Sept 14, FCFS Sept 15-Nov 30. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Cheboygan State Park Campground
note: Modern campground, 76 electric sites (some 50-amp, some pull-through), modern restrooms/showers, on Lake Huron / Duncan Bay near Cheboygan. Fits a 23-ft rig. Reservable online. NOTE: shower/restroom building under renovation -- modern campground temporarily closed, expected to reopen ~end of July 2026. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Waterloo Recreation Area - Sugarloaf Lake Campground
note: Modern campground, 164 electric sites (30/50-amp, no water hookup) on the west shore of Sugarloaf Lake; modern restrooms/showers, dump station, swim beach, boat launch. Large level sites handling rigs up to ~50 ft, so a 23-ft rig fits. Open ~mid-May to late Sept; reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Waterloo Recreation Area - Portage Lake Campground
note: Modern campground, 136 electric sites (some 50-amp) in three loops (north/central/south) on a hill above Portage Lake; modern restrooms/showers, dump station, swim beach reached by a paved path to the day-use area. Paved pads with grass, fits a 23-ft rig. Open year-round (Dec-Mar limited, Thu-Sat); reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Waterloo Recreation Area - Green Lake Campground
note: Rustic campground, 25 sites on Green Lake; no electric, vault toilets and hand-pump water, plus yurts. Drive-in sites accommodate ~30-ft trailers so a 23-ft rig fits; quiet wooded setting. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Lake Hudson Recreation Area
note: Semi-modern campground, ~50 sites in two loops, all with 20/30-amp electric (no individual water/sewer; hand pump + vault toilets). Max RV ~40 ft. Open Apr 1-Nov 30; reservable online via MiDNRReservations. Michigan Recreation Passport required for entry. Designated Dark Sky Preserve. --Claude

name: Proud Lake Recreation Area
note: Modern campground, 130 back-in sites on two loops (2537 Glengary Rd), all with 30/50-amp electric, modern bathhouse w/ showers, dump station. Max RV ~45-50 ft. Open year-round; reservable online via MiDNRReservations. Michigan Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Fort Wilkins Historic State Park — West Loop
note: Modern campground West Loop (sites 101-179) on Lake Fanny Hooe, 30/50-amp electric; 50-amp pull-through sites 140-160 fit larger RVs. Max RV ~50 ft. Open mid-May to mid-Oct; reservable online via MiDNRReservations. Michigan Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Fort Wilkins Historic State Park — East Loop
note: Modern campground East Loop (sites 1-81) on Lake Fanny Hooe, 30/50-amp electric. Max RV ~50 ft. CLOSED 2026 for electrical/site upgrades, anticipated reopening ~July 4, 2026 (verify before relying on it). Season mid-May to mid-Oct; reservable online via MiDNRReservations when open. Michigan Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Bewabic State Park
note: Modern campground, ~130 sites on three loops (A/B/C) on Fortune Lake: ~54 sites 50-amp (11 pull-through), ~68 sites 20/30-amp, a few no-electric, plus 4 rustic walk-in (107-110). Modern restrooms w/ showers, dump station. Max RV ~50 ft (Loop C pull-throughs to ~40 ft). Reservable online via MiDNRReservations. Michigan Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Fayette Historic State Park Campground
note: 61-site modern campground (11 pull-through, 50 back-in) near the historic Fayette townsite on Big Bay de Noc (Lake Michigan), Garden Peninsula UP. Electric (20/30-amp, some 50-amp pull-throughs), bathhouse, dump station. Seasonal, ~mid-May to mid-October. Reservable online via MiDNRReservations. RV Recreation Passport required for entry. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. --Claude

name: Hartwick Pines State Park Campground
note: 100-site modern campground near Grayling. Sites 1-64 electric (20/30-amp) with shared water spigots; sites 65-100 pull-through full hookup (water/sewer/50-amp). Flush restrooms, showers, two-lane dump station. Open year-round; up to ~40 ft. Reservable online via MiDNRReservations. RV Recreation Passport required for entry. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. --Claude

name: Wilson State Park Campground
note: 160-site modern campground on the north shore of Budd Lake in Harrison; north and south loops, all sites electric (20/30-amp, some 50-amp / pull-through), ~30 tent-only sites. Restrooms, showers, dump station. Season ~May-November. Reservable online via MiDNRReservations. RV Recreation Passport required for entry. Sites fit a 23-ft rig. --Claude

name: Holly Recreation Area - McGinnis Lake Modern Campground
note: McGinnis Lake Modern Campground: ~144 modern sites with 30/50-amp electric across the Maple, Oak, Aspen, Hickory and Trillium loops, plus a handful of rustic sites; modern restrooms/showers, dump station, 2 mini-cabins. Open seasonally (approx Apr-Oct). Reservable online via MiDNRReservations or by phone; Recreation Passport required for entry. Sites are wooded; some near McGinnis Lake. --Claude

name: Seven Lakes State Park Campground
note: Single modern campground of ~70 sites along Sand Lake (in the SE corner of the park, near Big Seven Lake), opened 1992; 30/50-amp electric on each site, paved roads, modern restrooms/showers, dump station, swimming beach and playscape. Open seasonally (approx Apr-Oct). Reservable online via MiDNRReservations; Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Ionia Recreation Area - Modern Campground
note: Modern campground of ~100 sites (97 modern with 30-amp electric) on a hilltop just east of Sessions Lake; picnic table and fire pit per site, restrooms/showers, dump station, drinking water, camper cabins. Reopened May 1 2026 after infrastructure upgrades. Open seasonally; reservable online via MiDNRReservations; Recreation Passport required. (Separate equestrian sites exist but the modern loop is general RV camping.) --Claude

name: Sleepy Hollow State Park Campground
note: 181 modern sites in two loops (North 100, South 81), 20/30-amp electric, paved pads fitting RVs to 45 ft, dump station, modern restrooms. On 410-acre Lake Ovid but the campground is set back inland with a footpath/short drive to the swim beach. Year-round camping; reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Lakeport State Park North Campground
note: Main modern campground: 194 sites, 30/50-amp electric, some pull-throughs, paved/grass pads, two toilet/shower buildings, on the Lake Huron side of the park. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Lakeport State Park South Campground
note: Smaller south unit across the village of Lakeport: 56 sites, 50-amp electric, paved parking pads, modern toilet/shower building. Set back ~2-min walk from the water (not on the lake). Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Rifle River Recreation Area - Grousehaven Modern Campground
note: 75 modern sites on two loops, electric (mostly 30-amp), accessible toilet/shower building, designated swim beach; RVs reported to ~40 ft. Upper Loop overlooks Grousehaven Lake, Lower Loop is level with the lake. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Rifle River Recreation Area - Devoe Lake Rustic Campground
note: Rustic DNR campground (state rec area, not a state forest): 58 drive-in sites, no electric, vault toilets, boat launch and swim area on Devoe Lake. Drive-in sites take small/mid RVs. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Algonac State Park - Riverfront Campground
note: Large modern campground (~220 sites in north/south loops, ~110 electric with 30/50-amp; north loop has full hookups), open grassy gravel-spur sites, fits RVs to 50 ft, along the half-mile St. Clair River frontage. A through-road runs between the front sites and the river; freighter-watching is the draw. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Algonac State Park - Wagon Wheel Campground
note: 87-site modern campground (~0.45 mi inland from Riverfront) in a wagon-wheel layout tucked in the woods, paved pads, more shade and privacy and away from road noise. Reservable online. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Metamora-Hadley Recreation Area Campground
note: Modern campground (~214 sites) on 80-acre Lake Minnawanna, two loops: north loop is open/grassy with waterfront sites, south loop is wooded/shaded. 20/30-amp electric at most sites, some 50-amp and non-electric; sanitation station. Typically open April-October. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Yankee Springs Recreation Area - Gun Lake Modern Campground
note: Modern campground (~200 sites) along Gun Lake within Yankee Springs Recreation Area; mix of shaded, semi-shaded and open sites near a buoyed swim beach and camper boat launch. 20/30-amp electric on each site, showers/flush toilets, sanitation station. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Yankee Springs Recreation Area - Deep Lake Rustic Campground
note: Rustic (state-rec-area) campground (~78 sites) on Deep Lake within Yankee Springs Recreation Area; no electric, vault toilets and hand-pump water, plus a separate group area. Drive-in sites; fishing pier and boat launch on Deep Lake. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. Note: reportedly closed for the 2024 season but operating again by 2025. --Claude

name: William C. Sterling State Park Campground
note: Large modern campground (~244-256 sites) at Michigan's only Lake Erie state park; mostly paved, generous sites, the majority with Lake Erie/lagoon views and beach access within steps. All sites 20/30-amp electric (76 sites with 50-amp), sanitation station, modern restrooms. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Muskegon State Park - Lake Michigan Campground
note: Modern campground (~105 sites), mostly wooded, set behind a large dune from Lake Michigan with a stairway/overlook providing beach access. Electric at most sites, modern restrooms, dump station (no water hookups at sites). Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Muskegon State Park - Channel Campground
note: Modern campground (~139 sites) in two loops near the Muskegon navigation channel: loop 1 (sites ~1-92) adjacent to the channel, loop 2 (sites ~101-147) larger, near the dune and Muskegon Lake. Electric at most sites, modern restrooms, dump station (no water hookups at sites). Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: P.J. Hoffmaster State Park Campground
note: 293-site modern campground in two loops on the north side of the park off Lake Harbor Rd; all but ~4 sites have electric (30/50-amp), modern restrooms/showers. Reservable online via MiDNRReservations. Campground sits behind a wooded foredune ridge; closest sites (25-60) are under a quarter-mile walk to the Lake Michigan beach. Open seasonally (roughly Apr-Oct). MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Newaygo State Park Campground
note: 99-site rustic campground in two loops (Oak #1-48, Poplar #49-99) atop ~20-ft embankments overlooking Hardy Dam Pond (a 6-mi impoundment of the Muskegon River). No electric, vault toilets, hand-pump water, dump station at exit; large shaded sites with 20-30 ft forested buffers fit a 23-ft rig. Reservable online via MiDNRReservations. Open seasonally. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Leelanau State Park Campground
note: 51-site rustic campground at the north tip of the Leelanau Peninsula near the Grand Traverse Lighthouse (off N. Lighthouse Point Rd). Drive-in forested sites (some pull-through), no electric/showers/dump station; vault toilets and drinking water; 3 mini-cabins. About half the sites sit directly on Lake Michigan/Cathead Bay, the rest tucked in the woods. Rustic and known for small sites, but drive-in RV access confirmed and 23-ft rigs fit the larger sites. Reservable online via MiDNRReservations. Open seasonally. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Onaway State Park Campground
note: 82 modern/electric (30-amp) drive-in sites on Black Lake; lower loop runs along the shore, upper loop atop a bluff. Open ~mid-April to mid-November. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: W.J. Hayes State Park Campground
note: ~180 modern/electric (20/30-amp) drive-in sites on Round Lake in two loops; sites 1-10 full hookup; 2 mini-cabins + 2 tiny houses. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Charles Mears State Park Campground
note: 175 modern/electric drive-in sites (concrete back-in pads) in two concentric loops at the Lake Michigan beach/dune in Pentwater; advertises rigs up to 40 ft so 23 ft fits. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. (Closed Apr 22-May 20, 2026 for electrical upgrades, since reopened.) --Claude

name: Petoskey State Park - Dunes Campground
note: Dunes Campground (~70 modern/electric sites, smaller/wooded loop) on Little Traverse Bay; paved/electric, 23 ft fits. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. (Park closed Sept 2025 for sewer/road work, targeted reopen ~mid-May 2026 -- confirm 2026 availability on MiDNRReservations.) --Claude

name: Pinckney State Recreation Area - Bruin Lake Modern Campground
note: 186 modern/electric (30 & 50-amp) drive-in sites on Bruin Lake; lower loop mostly paved, upper loop grass/dirt; max RV 40-50 ft. Boat launch, swim beach, dump station. Season ~Apr 15-Nov 1. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: North Higgins Lake State Park Campground
note: 174 modern drive-in sites (mostly 20/30/50-amp electric, water on many) in East and West loops set back north of N. Higgins Lake Dr; 2 mini-cabins, dump station; max ~50 ft. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Twin Lakes State Park Campground
note: 62 modern/electric (20/30-amp, some 50) grass drive-in sites on Lake Roland in a single loop; 1 mini-cabin, dump station, modern restroom/shower; max 50 ft. Season ~mid-May to mid-October. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. (Park temporarily closed for infrastructure work, anticipated reopen ~Aug 1, 2026.) --Claude

name: Pontiac Lake Recreation Area Campground
note: 176 modern/electric (20/30-amp, some 50) drive-in sites in East and West loops off Teggerdine Rd; max ~40-50 ft. Reservable online up to 6 months ahead; MI Recreation Passport required. (Modern campground closed for 2026 sanitation-station work, anticipated reopen ~Sept 1, 2026.) Separate 24-site equestrian camp is not this entry. --Claude

name: Highland Recreation Area - Rustic and Equestrian Campground
note: 25 rustic drive-in sites (no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump water) open to BOTH equestrian and non-equestrian campers; sites 20 & 21 ADA concrete pads; RVs up to 45 ft. Season ~April 1-Sept 30. Reservable online; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Thunder Bay River State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on the Thunder Bay River, 9 mi SW of Alpena via M-32 and Indian Reserve Rd. 10 sites for tent/small trailer at the end of a narrow gravel/dirt road; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations (not on MiDNRReservations); ~$20/night, 15-day max. Small boat launch; Wah-Wah-Tas-See Pathway and ORV trailhead adjacent. Open spring-fall. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Avery Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on Avery Lake, 9 mi SW of Atlanta via Cty Rd 487 and Avery Lake Rd. 16 sites; 10 accommodate a 40-ft vehicle/trailer and 3 are walk-in tent-only. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max. Boat launch. Open spring-fall. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Jackson Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground along the edge of Jackson Lake, 6 mi N of Atlanta via M-33. 18 sites; 15 accommodate a 40-ft vehicle/trailer. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max. Boating/fishing, hiking pathway and ORV trail access. Open spring-fall. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Leverentz Lake State Forest Campground
note: 18 rustic sites (1-11 on Big Leverentz Lake, 12-18 on Little Leverentz Lake), ~2.7 mi E of Baldwin; vault toilets, hand-pump well, no electric. FCFS, not reservable; ~$20/night, 15-day limit, open year-round (roads unplowed in winter). No posted max length and some sites reported to fit 30-ft trailers, so a 23-ft rig fits. Big Leverentz has a boat launch and 50-ft floating dock. Michigan Recreation Passport required. Baldwin Field Office. --Claude

name: Healy Lake State Forest Campground
note: 24 rustic sites on Healy Lake, ~7.5 mi SW of Copemish (Manistee Co.); vault toilets, hand-pump well, no electric, plus a sandy swimming beach and boat launch. FCFS, not reservable; $20/night, ~May 1-Oct 31, 15-day limit. No firm posted length cap; a reviewer reports a 22-ft rig fit and large sites that could take a 30-ft trailer, so a 23-ft rig fits. Phone last verified 2016, confirm before relying. Michigan Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Platte River State Forest Campground
note: 26 rustic sites on the Platte River, ~2.5 mi SE of Honor via US-31 & Goose Rd (Benzie Co.); vault toilets, hand-pump well, no electric (two mini cabins also on site). FCFS, not reservable; $20/night, 15-day limit. No posted max length; reviews describe large sites by the river, so a 23-ft rig fits. Trout fishing and paddling. Michigan Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Veterans Memorial State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on the Platte River, 3.5 mi E of Honor (Benzie Co). 24 sites for tent and small trailer use; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations (not on MiDNRReservations). 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Maple Bay State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on the west side of Burt Lake, 4 mi E of Brutus (Emmet Co). 35 sites for tent, small AND large trailer use; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations. Sandy shoreline + two-lane boat launch. 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Long Lake State Forest Campground (Wexford County)
note: Rustic state forest campground on Long Lake, 8 mi N of Cadillac via Long Lake Rd/Old US-131 (Wexford Co). 12 sites for tent and small trailer use; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations. In-campground boat ramp; direct ORV/cycle trail access. ~$20/night; 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Twin Lakes State Forest Campground
note: Rustic Mackinaw State Forest campground on a forested bluff above Twin Lake, ~6 mi SE of Alverno in Cheboygan County. 11 sites for tent/small trailer, no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well. FCFS, no online reservations (not on MiDNRReservations); 15-day stay limit; managed by Cheboygan State Park. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: King Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest CG on the north shore of King Lake (498-acre lake), 14.8 mi E of Covington. 6 sites for tents/small trailers; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. Long narrow drive in — fine for a 23-ft rig, not for big rigs. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Deer Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest CG on Deer Lake, 17 mi N of Crystal Falls (Iron County). 12 sites for tents/small trailers with boat launch; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$15-20/night, 15-day max stay, open ~May-Oct. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Glidden Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest CG on the east shore of Glidden Lake, ~5 mi SE of Crystal Falls (Iron County); managed by Bewabic State Park. 23 drive-in sites for tents/trailers; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric; carry-in/carry-out (no trash). FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. Day-use area has gravel boat launch and sandy shoreline; Lake Mary Plains Pathway trailhead adjacent. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: McCollum Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest CG on McCollum Lake, 6 mi SE of Curran. 20 sites (11 accommodate up to 40-ft trailers, 3 are walk-in tent-only); open-field layout, minimal tree cover; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: DeTour State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest CG on a Lake Huron peninsula, 5 mi W of DeTour Village; managed by Brimley State Park. 21 sites for tents/small trailers (3 listed up to 40 ft); vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric; sandy Lake Huron beaches. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. Access road is narrow and winding through pine/hemlock — fine for a 23-ft rig but not big-rig friendly (reviewers warn ~30 ft practical max). MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Garey Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI state forest CG on Garey Lake, ~12-13 sites (vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric, $20/night). Drive-in; a 36-ft rig was recorded on record (8/2018) so a 23-ft EKKO fits, though reviewers say most sites suit smaller rigs/tents. FCFS, no reservations; open to equestrian use but a general drive-in CG (distinct from the separate Garey Lake Trail Camp). 14-day limit. MI Recreation Passport required. Per DNR recsearch + Campendium. --Claude

name: Mud Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI state forest CG on Mud Lake, 8 large flat sites in a loop (vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric, ~$20/night). Most sites fit a 30-ft trailer and site #7 is a pull-through, so a 23-ft EKKO fits easily. FCFS, no reservations. Carry-in boat launch; direct access to Green Pine Lake Pathway. Open all year but roads not plowed (effective Apr-Oct). 14-day limit. MI Recreation Passport required. Per DNR recsearch + Campendium reviews. --Claude

name: Genes Pond State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI state forest CG (UP) on Gene's Pond, 14 sites for tent/small/large trailer (vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric, ~$15/night) so a 23-ft EKKO fits. FCFS, no reservations. Boat launch on the pond; shaded by upland hardwoods. Open all year but roads not plowed. 14-day limit. MI Recreation Passport required. Per DNR recsearch + Campendium. --Claude

name: Spring Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI state forest CG on Spring Lake (canal access to Fife Lake), 32 sites for tent/small trailer (vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric, ~$15/night); reported max vehicle ~50 ft so a 23-ft EKKO fits. FCFS, no reservations. Gravel boat ramp; direct access to North Country Trail / Fife Lake Loop. 14-day limit. MI Recreation Passport required. Per DNR recsearch + Campendium. --Claude

name: Old US-131 State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI state forest CG on the Manistee River, 7 mi N of Manton; 25 sites for tent/small trailer (5 of those are walk-in sites near the water for paddlers). Vault toilets, hand-pump potable water, no electric. First-come-first-served, not reservable online; ~$20/night, 15-day limit. MI Recreation Passport required. Connects to North Country Trail / Fife Lake Loop. --Claude

name: Lake Marjory State Forest Campground
note: Small rustic MI state forest CG near Frederic on Lake Marjory (a fishing lake); 10 primitive tent/RV sites, 2 of which accommodate up to a 40-ft trailer. Vault toilets, hand-pump potable water, no electric. First-come-first-served, not reservable online; ~$20/night, 15-day limit. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Upper Manistee River State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI state forest CG ~7.7 mi SW of Frederic on the main branch of the Manistee River; 30 drive-in sites for tent/small & large trailer use, plus 10 separate walk-in tent-only sites (parked 1/4 mi SE). Vault toilets, hand-pump potable water, no electric. First-come-first-served, not reservable online (the reservable Manistee backcountry/canoe sites are a separate USFS program); ~$20/night, 15-day limit. River access via a staircase mid-campground and a footpath by site #16. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Portage Bay State Forest Campground
note: Remote rustic MI state forest CG ~10 mi S of Garden on Big Bay de Noc (Lake Michigan); 23 sites for tent/small trailer. Vault toilets, hand-pump potable water, no electric. First-come-first-served, not reservable online; ~$15-20/night, 15-day limit. Access is ~8-10 mi of dirt/gravel road, last few miles tight with low branches — passable by a normal vehicle and short rigs (reviewers fit ~22-ft RVs) but tight for a 23-ft rig; large trailers not recommended. Ninga Aki Pathway hiking; boat launch S of the CG. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Big Bear Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI state forest CG on the N shore of Big Bear Lake, ~SW of Vienna Corners (near Gaylord/Johannesburg); 31 sites for tent/small trailer, 11 of which accommodate ~30-ft trailers. Vault toilets, hand-pump potable water, no electric. First-come-first-served, not reservable online (sources conflict but official DNR shows FCFS); ~$20/night, 15-day limit. This is the main Big Bear Lake loop (distinct from the separate Big Bear Point SFC). MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Mead Creek State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on the Manistique River, ~6 mi SW of Germfask. 9 sites for tent/small trailer (fits a 23-ft rig); sites 1-4 are riverfront, the rest sit on a bluff above the river. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: South Manistique Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on the west shore of South Manistique Lake, ~5.5 mi SW of Curtis. 30 sites for tent, small and large trailer (fits a 23-ft rig); site 27 is a pull-through. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric; boat launch (shallow/sandy). FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: House Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground in the Gladwin Field Trial Area, ~17 mi N of Gladwin. 41 sites in two loops with water access to House and Hoister lakes; 35 of the 41 sites accommodate up to 40-ft vehicles/trailers (easily fits a 23-ft rig). Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric; ~$20/night. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Trout Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground near Trout Lake in the Gladwin Field Trial Area, ~17 mi N of Gladwin; three small loops. 35 sites for tent/small trailer, 10 of which accommodate up to 40-ft vehicles/trailers (fits a 23-ft rig); site 19 is a pull-through. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. NOTE: Trout Lake is significantly drawn down since an April 2025 emergency dam drawdown -- campground remains open and operating but the lake is low. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. --Claude

name: Big Knob State Forest Campground
note: Rustic Lake Superior State Forest campground on Lake Michigan; 23 sites for tent/small & large trailer (site #22 is pull-through), vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS self-register, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required for entry. 13.5 mi SW of Naubinway at the dead end of Big Knob Rd. --Claude

name: Milakokia Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic Lake Superior State Forest campground on the NE side of Milakokia Lake; 35 sites for tent/small & large trailer in two loops (sites 1-11 right of entrance, 12-35 left and better for large trailers), vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. Boat launch for small craft. FCFS self-register, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. 20 mi W of Naubinway. --Claude

name: Shupac Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic AuSable State Forest campground on Shupac Lake; 30 sites for tent/small trailer, vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. Boat launch; clear lake good for fishing/paddling. FCFS self-register, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. 3 mi N of Lovells via Twin Bridge Rd and Campground Trail. --Claude

name: Jones Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic AuSable State Forest campground on Jones Lake; 42 sites for tent/small trailer across six loops, vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. Boat launch on Jones Lake. FCFS self-register, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay, one unit/6 people per site. MI Recreation Passport required. Near Grayling/Frederic. --Claude

name: Keystone Landing State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on the main branch of the Au Sable River, 4 mi E of Grayling. 18 sites for tent/small trailer; vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric; ~$20/night. First-come-first-served (no reservations); 15-day max stay. Carry-in canoe/kayak access on site. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Lake Margrethe State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on Lake Margrethe, ~6.6 mi W of Grayling. 37 drive-in sites (tent/small+large trailer) plus 9 separate walk-in sites; vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. First-come-first-served (official DNR page lists no reservations; older 'reservable' listings are stale); 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Burton's Landing State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on the main branch of the Au Sable River, 3.5 mi E of Grayling. 12 sites for tent/small trailer; vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric; ~$20/night. First-come-first-served (no reservations); 15-day max stay, one unit/6 people per site. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Manistee River Bridge State Forest Campground
note: Rustic campground on the main branch of the Manistee River, 7.8 mi NW of Grayling. 23 sites for tent, small and large trailer; vault toilets, hand-pump well, no electric. FCFS, no reservations, ~$20/night, 15-day max stay, MI Recreation Passport required. Two river-access points (deck behind site 3; grass canoe/kayak landing). --Claude

name: Anderson Lake West State Forest Campground
note: Secluded rustic campground on Anderson Lake, 5 mi SW of Gwinn via CR 577. 13 drive-in back-in sites for tent, small and large trailer; pit toilets, hand-pump well, no electric. FCFS, no reservations, ~$20/night, 15-day max stay, MI Recreation Passport required. Boat landing and small sand swimming beach; Anderson Lake Pathway trailhead (6 mi of loops) in the campground. Only Anderson Lake SFCG currently listed by DNR (no separate 'East'). --Claude

name: Bass Lake (Marquette) State Forest Campground
note: 22 rustic sites on a single shaded loop on the east side of Bass Lake; for tent, small and large trailer use. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. Carry-in/carry-out. FCFS — no reservations. 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Little Lake State Forest Campground
note: 16 rustic sites on the east side of Little Lake; tent, small and large trailer use, with sites 6, 8 and 12 pull-throughs suited to large trailers. Small central beach. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. Carry-in/carry-out. FCFS — no reservations. 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Houghton Lake State Forest Campground
note: 50 sites on the north shore of Houghton Lake; semi-modern rustic, some sites accommodate up to 40 ft. Vault toilets plus a modern (no-shower) restroom building, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS — no reservations (fee ~$20, self-register at kiosk). Season late April to mid-September. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Reedsburg Dam State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on the Muskegon River/Reedsburg impoundment, 47 sites for tent and small trailer use (38 sites fit up to 40 ft, 15 of those waterfront); vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. FCFS only, not reservable; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. Boat launch in campground. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Lake Dubonnet State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on Lake Dubonnet (managed via Interlochen State Park), 50 tent/small-trailer sites among pines; vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. FCFS only, not reservable; ~$20/night, 15-day max, one unit/6 people per site. Boat launch and Lost Lake Pathway in campground. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Carney Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on Carney Lake, Dickinson County (managed via Bewabic State Park), 16 tent/small-trailer sites; vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. FCFS only, not reservable. Boat launch, hiking and ORV-trail access. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Big Bear Point State Forest Campground
note: 14 rustic sites on north shore of Big Bear Lake; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS (no reservations). Boat launch within campground. Open ~April-October; ~$15-20/night. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Town Corner Lake State Forest Campground
note: 12 rustic sites for tent/small trailer between Town Corner Lake and the Black River in Pigeon River Country SF; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS (no reservations). Boat launch on lake. Open ~April-October; ~$15-20/night. Remote, no cell service. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: CCC Bridge State Forest Campground
note: 31 rustic sites for tent/small trailer along the Manistee River (Wild & Scenic); vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. Drive-in sites (also some boat-in). FCFS (no reservations). Popular for paddling/fly-fishing. ~$15-20/night. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Guernsey Lake State Forest Campground
note: ~35 rustic sites (8 walk-in) on north shore of Guernsey Lake; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS (no reservations). Boat ramp/swimming; adjacent Sand Lakes Quiet Area pathway. Reviewers report rigs up to ~22 ft fitting. ~$15-20/night. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Pickerel Lake (Kalkaska) State Forest Campground
note: 13 rustic sites on Pickerel Lake, 13.5 mi N of Kalkaska via CR-612 & Sunset Trail Rd. Vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night. Concrete boat launch on NW side. Campers report 22-ft rigs fit some sites. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Lake Ann State Forest Campground
note: 30 rustic sites near Lake Ann, 2 mi SW of Lake Ann village via Almira & Reynolds Rds. Vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night. Beech/maple forest. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Goose Lake State Forest Campground
note: 30 rustic sites on Goose Lake, 2.5 mi NW of Lake City. Vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night. Paved boat launch; mixed evergreen/hardwood. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Long Lake (Missaukee) State Forest Campground
note: 11 rustic sites on Long Lake, 3.5 mi NW of Lake City; access road runs through Goose Lake SFCG. Vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night. Sites on a hill along the shoreline. Small sites — confirm a 23-ft rig fits before relying on it. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Little Wolf Lake State Forest Campground
note: 24 rustic sites for tent/small/large-trailer use on Little Wolf Lake; sites accommodate up to 35-ft trailers, no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; $20/night, 15-day max stay. Boat ramp on the lake. MI Recreation Passport required. ~3 mi SE of Lewiston. --Claude

name: Muskrat Lake State Forest Campground
note: 12 rustic sites for tent/small-trailer use on shallow warm-water Muskrat Lake; 2 sites accommodate a 40-ft vehicle/trailer (per official DNR listing), no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; $20/night, 15-day max stay. Boat ramp; access to Mio ATV trail. MI Recreation Passport required. ~12 mi NW of Mio. --Claude

name: Carrieville State Forest Campground
note: 31 large rustic sites (most fit a 30-ft trailer, one up to 40 ft) bordered on the east by the Little Manistee River; mix of wooded and open-field sites, no electric, no dump station, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; $20/night, 15-day max stay. ORV trailhead; trout/steelhead river. MI Recreation Passport required. Lake County, ~2.8 mi E of Luther. --Claude

name: Silver Creek State Forest Campground
note: 26 rustic sites on the Pine River; sites 1-19 are drive-in (tent/small trailer), sites 20-26 are walk-in on a river peninsula (tent). No electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; ~$13-20/night, 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. Open spring-fall, ~5.5 mi N of Luther. --Claude

name: Graves Crossing State Forest Campground
note: 10 rustic sites for tent/small-trailer use in the Jordan River Valley (Michigan's first designated scenic river); no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. Carry-in canoe/kayak access just east of the entrance. FCFS, no reservations; ~$15-20/night, 14-15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. Open year-round; a camper reported a 19-ft trailer fits comfortably. --Claude

name: Baxter Bridge State Forest Campground
note: 25 rustic sites (no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water) on the Manistee River, ~12 mi NW of Manton via M-42 & 31 Rd; Campendium tags it big-rig friendly. FCFS, no reservations; $15/night, 15-day max stay. Open year-round. Popular canoe/kayak/tube put-in with an in-campground kayak landing. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: North Gemini Lake State Forest Campground
note: 17 rustic sites (no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water) on Gemini Lake in two loops; drive-in, tent/small-trailer use, no posted numeric length cap. FCFS, no reservations; $20/night, 15-day max stay. Central boat launch. MI Recreation Passport required. UP, ~10 mi from Pictured Rocks. --Claude

name: Ross Lake State Forest Campground
note: 10 rustic, well-spaced sites (no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water) on Ross Lake, ~3 mi off the main road via a well-maintained dirt road; official wording cites tent, small-trailer and large-trailer use. Sites set back from the lake (not on the shoreline) with steep foot paths down to the water; boat launch <0.25 mi north. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. UP near Melstrand. --Claude

name: South Gemini Lake State Forest Campground
note: 8 rustic sites on the south shore of South Gemini Lake; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; 14-day max stay. Drive-in via sand/dirt forest roads (Twin Lakes Truck Trail), passable in dry conditions. A 28-ft Class C was firsthand-reported fitting site 4, so 23-ft rigs fit. ~10 mi from Pictured Rocks. MI Recreation Passport required. Managing office: DNR Shingleton FMU. --Claude

name: Ocqueoc Falls State Forest Campground
note: 13 rustic sites for tent/small-trailer use along the Ocqueoc River near Ocqueoc Falls (the Lower Peninsula's largest waterfall); vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; $20/night honor-system self-registration, 15-day max stay. ~12 mi W of Rogers City via M-68. MI Recreation Passport required. Managing office: DNR Atlanta FMU. --Claude

name: Mio Pond State Forest Campground
note: 24 rustic, secluded wooded drive-in sites on the west side of Mio Pond (an Au Sable River impoundment behind Mio Dam); a separate 10-site paddle-in group camp is on the east side. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; $20/night, 15-day max stay. ~3 mi NW of Mio (M-33 & Popps Rd). 'Small trailer' sites with no numeric length cap on the official DNR page (a 20-ft figure appears only in aggregator AI prose). MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Little Brevort Lake-South State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground at the south end of Little Brevort Lake (a state-designated natural area), 23 sites for tent/small trailer with some rated up to ~40 ft; no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay; ~$20/night. MI Recreation Passport required. Designated swim beach monitored by the state BeachGuard program. --Claude

name: Little Brevort Lake-North State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on the NW side of Little Brevort Lake (a state-designated natural area), 20 sites for tent/small trailer with some rated up to ~40 ft; no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay. MI Recreation Passport required. Non-motorized boat launch near the picnic area; 2.3 mi E of Brevort via Worth and Ozark roads. --Claude
