name: Hog Island Point State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on Lake Michigan, ~42 sites for tent/small trailer (sites 7 and 32 are pull-through); no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay; ~$15/night. MI Recreation Passport required. 7.7 mi E of Naubinway on US-2; most campsites have direct shoreline access, day-use sandy beach on the east side. --Claude

name: Black River State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on the Black River, 12 sites for tent/small or large trailer (some rated up to ~40 ft); no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay; ~$15-20/night. MI Recreation Passport required. On the Black River 8 mi NE of Naubinway via US-2 and Black River/Peters Creek Rd; Peters Creek Pathway hiking and steelhead/salmon fishing nearby. --Claude

name: Pretty Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic Lake Superior SF campground in the Pretty Lake Quiet Area, ~25 mi NW of Newberry. 18 drive-in sites for tent/small/large trailer use, plus 8 separate walk-in/paddle-in sites (#19-26) along the pathway -- only the 18 main-loop sites are drive-in RV. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Blind Sucker No. 1 State Forest Campground
note: Rustic Lake Superior SF campground on the Blind Sucker Flooding, ~34 mi NW of Newberry via CR-407. 17 sites for tent/small/large trailer use; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric, boat launch. FCFS, no reservations; 15-day max stay; MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Holland Lake State Forest Campground
note: 15 rustic sites for tent and small trailer use on Holland Lake (a dammed flowage). Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. Hilly terrain so leveling a larger rig can be a project. Pine Ridge ORV trail starts in the campground. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Natalie State Forest Campground
note: 12 rustic sites for tent and small trailer use on the Dollarville Flooding (Tahquamenon River watershed), ~4 mi W of Newberry. Vault toilets, potable water spigot, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$15-20/night, 15-day max stay. Longest vehicle reported ~24 ft; dirt access road. Boat launch adjacent; calm flooding good for paddling. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Perch Lake State Forest Campground
note: 32 rustic sites for tent, small and large trailer use on the shore of Perch Lake; managed by Muskallonge Lake State Park. Vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. Central stairway lake access plus boat launch on the south side; in-campground road is a bit rough and some sites are hard to level for big rigs. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Shoepac Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on 52-acre Shoepac Lake in Mackinaw State Forest; 25 sites for tents and trailers, vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night plus required MI Recreation Passport; 15-day max stay. Season ~April-November. Near the Sinkhole Pathway limestone sinkholes. Most sites are wooded and set back from the water. --Claude

name: Tomahawk Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on 40-acre Tomahawk Lake; 25-26 sites for tent and small/large trailer use, vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night plus required MI Recreation Passport; 15-day max stay. Season ~April-November. --Claude

name: Tomahawk Creek Flooding State Forest Campground
note: Large rustic state forest campground on the Tomahawk Creek flooding (impoundment); 47 sites in two units (sites 1-24 east unit, 25-47 west unit), each with a boat launch; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS, no reservations; ~$20/night plus required MI Recreation Passport; 15-day max stay. Season ~April-November; known for fishing among submerged timber. --Claude

name: Van Etten Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on Van Etten Lake near Oscoda; 49 sites for tent, small and large trailer use (large trailers accommodated, so a 23-ft rig fits), vault toilets, hand-pump well water, improved boat launch, no electric. FCFS — not reservable online; ~$15/night, 15-day max stay, open roughly May-Oct. Sites in the upper-30s to mid-40s reach the lake via footpaths. MI Recreation Passport required. Note: MDHHS PFAS fish-consumption/foam advisory for the lake. --Claude

name: Ossineke State Forest Campground
note: 42-site rustic MI DNR state forest campground on Lake Huron's Thunder Bay, ~1 mi east of Ossineke, set in cedar forest. No electric; vault toilets, hand-pump well water; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. FCFS — not reservable online (not on MiDNRReservations). About half the sites have waterfront access to a sandy beach with a boardwalk to the water; takes tents and small/large trailers. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Munuscong River State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on the Munuscong River near Pickford, managed by Brimley State Park; 25 sites for tent/small trailer, vault toilets and hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS — not reservable online. Boat launch with river access leading ~1 mi to Lake Munuscong. ~$20/night, open seasonally ~May-Nov. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Black Creek State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground in Midland County ~3 mi N of Sanford, with 23 shaded sites along Black Creek; vault toilets, potable water from a spigot, no electric. FCFS self-registration — not reservable online; ~$20/night, 15-day max stay. 15 sites accommodate up to a 40-ft trailer, so a 23-ft rig fits easily. Open roughly mid-April to mid-Sept. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: East Branch of Fox River State Forest Campground
note: 19 rustic sites for tent, small trailer and large trailer use (large trailers accommodated, so a 23-ft rig fits); vault toilets, hand-pump well water, no electric. FCFS — not reservable; ~$15/night, 15-day max stay. On the East Branch of the Fox River and King's Pond, ~8 mi N of Seney; former fish-hatchery site. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Kingston Lake State Forest Campground
note: 16 rustic sites for tent and small trailer use on Kingston Lake SW of Grand Marais; vault toilets, hand-pump well, no electric. FCFS — not reservable; ~$20/night, 15-day max. Managed by Indian Lake State Park; nearly ringed by water with a boat launch and picnic area. Sites are tight — some fit a 23-ft rig but larger rigs struggle (a reviewer's 18-ft fit fine). MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Big Eric's Bridge State Forest Campground
note: ~20-21 rustic sites for tent and small trailer use on the Huron River in Baraga County; vault toilets, hand-pump well, no electric. FCFS — not reservable; open ~April-Nov. Popular for trout/steelhead; not equestrian. ~6.7 mi E of Skanee via Skanee and Erick roads. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Emily Lake State Forest Campground
note: 9 rustic sites for tent and small trailer use on the south shore of 54-acre Emily Lake in Houghton County; vault toilets, hand-pump well, no electric, parking spur/fire ring/table per site. FCFS — not reservable; ~$20/night, 15-day max. Boat launch on site; shaded by conifers and hemlock. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Pigeon River State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground in Pigeon River Country State Forest, ~13.5 mi E of Vanderbilt via Sturgeon Valley Rd. 14 sites for tent/small trailer, vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric, $20/most sites. FCFS only (no reservations) - self-register at kiosk; 15-day max stay. On the Pigeon River; Shingle Mill Pathway runs through it. Open spring-fall. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Round Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground in Pigeon River Country State Forest, ~14 mi E of Vanderbilt via Sturgeon Valley Rd & Round Lake Rd. 10 sites for tent/small trailer, vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric, $20/most sites. FCFS only (no reservations); 15-day max stay. Sits on the shoreline of Round Lake; access to High Country Pathway. Open spring-fall. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Big Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic state forest campground on the east shore of Big Lake (127 acres), ~9 mi N of Covington via M-28 W, W Plains Rd & Big Lake Rd. ~12-15 sites for tent/small trailer, vault toilets, hand-pump water, no electric, ~$15-20. FCFS only (no reservations). Small concrete boat ramp for canoes/kayaks/small boats; access to Baraga Plains ORV Trail; bass/perch/crappie fishing. Open ~May-Nov. MI Recreation Passport required. --Claude

name: Ambrose Lake State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on Ambrose Lake, 11 mi N of West Branch. 25 sites (incl. accessible #6, #8), no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water; ~$20/night. FCFS - not reservable online. 15-day max stay. Recreation Passport required. Boat launch + carry-in paddle access; some sites directly on the lake. --Claude

name: Haakwood State Forest Campground
note: Rustic MI DNR state forest campground on the Sturgeon River, 2 mi N of Wolverine. 18 sites for tent/small trailer, no electric, vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS - not reservable online. 15-day max stay. Recreation Passport required. Sites are smallish (a reviewer fit a 22-ft rig into site 1) but a 23-ft rig fits; no posted max length under 20 ft. --Claude

name: Weber Lake State Forest Campground
note: 18 rustic sites for tent/small trailer use, no electric; vault toilets, hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations (~$20/night); 15-day max stay; MI Recreation Passport required. ~7 mi NW of Wolverine via Wolverine Rd and Peru Rd. Firsthand review: 22-ft toy hauler fit at site 18, sites large and spread out (some unlevel). On Weber Lake; lake reached via a stairway on the south side. --Claude

name: Pickerel Lake (Otsego) State Forest Campground
note: 39 rustic sites for tent/small trailer use, no electric; vault toilets, hand-pump well water, trash collection. FCFS, no reservations (~$20/night); MI Recreation Passport required. ~10 mi NE of Vanderbilt via Sturgeon Valley Rd and Pickerel Lake Rd, within Pigeon River Country State Forest. Largest/most popular PRC family campground; small shallow gravel boat launch adjacent (electric motors only); Pickerel Lake Pathway access. --Claude

name: Pine Grove State Forest Campground
note: 6 rustic sites for tent/small trailer use, no electric; vault toilets, free-flowing artesian/hand-pump well water. FCFS, no reservations (~$20/night); 15-day max stay; MI Recreation Passport required. Secluded along the Pigeon River in Pigeon River Country State Forest, ~12 mi E of Wolverine via Wolverine Rd, Webb Rd and Campsite Rd; good High Country Pathway stopover. Twisty/rutted dirt access road but firsthand reviews confirm RVs make it in (direct river fishing access). --Claude

name: Nichols Lake South Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the south shore of 160-acre Nichols Lake; 29 family sites on paved spurs, flush toilets, showers, drinking water. Day-use area (swim beach) is on the north shore. Reservable online. No hookups. --Claude

name: Bowman Bridge Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the Pere Marquette National Scenic River; 16 family sites in a fairly open field plus 4 group sites, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Reservable online. Popular canoe/fishing access. --Claude

name: Timber Creek Campground
note: Manistee NF rustic campground near a small impoundment (not a popular fishing spot per the Forest Service); first-come, first-served, $10/night, potable water available, North Country Trail trailhead. Not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: Udell Rollways Campground
note: Manistee NF rustic campground on a bluff overlooking the Manistee River (a stairway descends to the river for fishing; the river is below, not at the sites). First-come, first-served, no hookups; 18-hole disc golf course on site. Drive-in via Horseshoe Bend Rd / forest roads (standard-clearance vehicle). No working official Forest Service page as of 2026; well-confirmed operating via reviews. --Claude

name: Pine Lake Campground
note: Manistee NF rustic campground on Pine Lake; 12 sites, vault toilets, drinking water, boat access. First-come, first-served, no hookups. Forestcamping lists pads to ~74 ft. Near Bear Track / North Country Trail area. --Claude

name: Minnie Pond Campground
note: Manistee NF rustic campground on Minnie Pond, a man-made impoundment popular with waterfowl/hunters; ~10-11 sites, vault toilets, NO potable water (bring your own), boat launch, max RV ~30 ft. First-come, first-served, ~$10/night. --Claude

name: Old Grade Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the Little Manistee River near Peacock; 20 family sites, first-come, first-served, vault toilets. Short interpretive hiking trail through hemlock/birch; near wetlands and fields. No hookups. --Claude

name: Peterson Bridge Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the Pine National Scenic River; ~30 family sites (sites 01-31, one host), many shaded and secluded, vault toilets. Reservable online (managed by American Land & Leisure). Popular canoe/kayak access on the swift Pine River. No hookups. --Claude

name: Pines Point Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the White River; ~27-30 sites, flush toilets, drinking water, canoe/kayak launch and day-use area; pads to ~80 ft. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Sand Lake Recreation Area Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on Sand Lake; ~45-47 sites on paved pads (RVs to ~40 ft), coin showers, flush toilets, drinking water. Two loops - sites 1-30 main loop, 30+ secondary loop. Reservable online. No hookups. --Claude

name: Seaton Creek Campground
note: Manistee NF rustic campground at the upper end of the Hodenpyl Dam backwater of the Big Manistee River, adjoining Seaton Creek; 17 family sites + 2 group sites, pine forest, near a picnic area. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Bear Track Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the Little Manistee River in Lake County; ~16-20 sites (capacity ~20 vehicles), potable water, vault toilets, fire pits, picnic tables. Drive-in via FR 5202. First-come, first-served. North Country Trail nearby. Open early May-Labor Day. --Claude

name: Benton Lake Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on 33-acre Benton Lake; ~25 sites, vault toilets, boat launch (no-wake/electric-motor lake). First-come, first-served, no hookups. --Claude

name: Brush Lake Campground
note: Small secluded Manistee NF campground on Brush Lake (surrounded by NF land); tent/RV sites, carry-in boat launch in the campground plus a drive-in launch on the lake. First-come, first-served, low use. --Claude

name: Hemlock Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the western shore of 1,600-acre Lake Mitchell near Cadillac; 19 sites for tents/RVs/trailers (parking for ~20 vehicles w/ trailers), vault toilets, boat launch. NO potable water on site. Quiet marshy end of the lake (milfoil - not great for swimming). First-come, first-served. Forestcamping lists pads to ~75 ft. --Claude

name: Hungerford Lake Campground
note: Manistee NF family campground in the Hungerford Recreation Area (6,500-acre nonmotorized area) near Hungerford Lake; 48 sites, FCFS, ~$15/night, pads to ~40 ft. Separate equestrian/group horse camp exists nearby - this entry is the FAMILY campground, not the horse camp. --Claude

name: Lake Michigan Recreation Area Campground
note: Manistee NF campground on the shore of Lake Michigan ~15 mi south of Manistee, in the Nordhouse Dunes area along Porter Creek; ~99 sites (4 loops, incl. group + tent-only), vault toilets, boardwalk/stairs to the beach. Reservable online. No hookups. Forestcamping lists pads to ~56 ft. --Claude

name: Monument Campground
note: Huron NF, on the River Road Scenic Byway above the AuSable River near the Lumberman's Monument. 19 secluded sites in a red pine plantation; pressurized drinking water, vault toilets, grills, camp host. Reservable online during peak season (otherwise FCFS); $15/night. --Claude

name: Mack Lake ORV Campground
note: Huron NF, largest campground on the Mio Ranger District; 42 standard nonelectric sites in two loops (Warbler 1-21, Beaver 22-42) on 175-acre Mack Lake with swimming beach and carry-in boat access. ORV-trail campground (Mack Lake Trail System) but a normal drive-in RV campground, not restricted to ORV users. Pit toilets, potable water. Mix of reservable (online) and first-come, first-served sites. --Claude

name: Gabions Campground
note: Huron NF, on the National Scenic stretch of the AuSable River; 8 drive-in sites with picnic tables, fire rings, lantern posts, vault toilet. No water provided. OHV trail access at the campground road. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Pine River Campground
note: Huron NF near Glennie, small rustic drive-in campground (~5-vehicle capacity) on the Pine River; vault toilets, no potable water (bring your own), CCC-era picnic shelter. Drive-in RV access. First-come, first-served; $10/night; open mid-April to Nov 30. Not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: Rollways Campground
note: Huron NF near Hale, perched on a high bluff overlooking Loud Dam Pond on the AuSable River. 19 sites; pull-through sites accommodate large motorhomes/trailers. Adjacent day-use area and Westgate Scenic Overlook. Reservable online during peak season; $15/night. --Claude

name: Round Lake Campground
note: Huron NF near National City, rustic wooded campground on 91-acre Round Lake (campground is the only public access). 33 sites in two loops; about a quarter have water access. Vault toilets, garbage service. Sites 1-13 reservable online, sites 14-33 first-come, first-served; $14/night. --Claude

name: Horseshoe Lake Campground
note: Huron NF near Curran, small lightly-used rustic campground (~9-11 sites) on 16-acre Horseshoe Lake with boat launch and a short lake-loop trail. Vault toilets, hand-pump water. Best for vans/tents/small RVs under 25 ft (a 23-ft rig fits; access is tight — survey on foot before bringing a larger rig). First-come, first-served; $10/night. Not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: Jewell Lake Campground
note: Huron NF near Barton City, on the east side of 193-acre Jewell Lake with swimming beach and boat launch. Tent and RV sites (~32-vehicle capacity); spacious private sites. First-come, first-served; $15/night; open early May to late Oct. Not on recreation.gov. --Claude

name: Kneff Lake Campground
note: Huron NF near Grayling, on 20-acre Kneff Lake (designated trout lake, stocked rainbow trout; carry-in non-motorized boats only). 27 sites; sites 1-18 crushed-stone pads, 19-27 asphalt, site 9 tent-only. Hand-pump wells, vault toilets. Mix of reservable (online) and first-come, first-served sites. --Claude

name: Meadows ORV Campground
note: Huron NF near Luzerne, small 12-16 site campground at the Meadows ORV Trail System trailhead. ORV-trail campground but a normal drive-in CG, not restricted to ORV users; sites accommodate trailers/RVs up to 25 ft. Hand-pump well, vault toilets. First-come, first-served (no reservations for 2026); $10/night. --Claude

name: Wagner Lake Campground
note: Huron NF near Rose City, small 12-site campground on shallow Wagner Lake (beach play, swimming, carry-in non-motorized boats; good bass/panfish). Paved pads, hand-pump wells, vault toilets. 1.5-mile dirt access road and a narrow, hilly interior loop with tight curves — a 23-ft rig fits but is near the practical limit; bigger rigs struggle. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Island Lake Campground
note: Huron NF near Rose City, on 58-acre Island Lake (25-ft-deep, cold, clear; swimming beach marked with buoys, non-motorized boating). 17 sites, no power, paved pads, dump station available; hand-pump wells, vault toilets. 1.25-mile loop trail; spur to Loon Lake Day Use Area. Mix of reservable (online) and first-come, first-served. --Claude

name: Bay Furnace Campground
note: Rustic Hiawatha NF campground on the south shore of Lake Superior near Munising, across from Grand Island. ~50 sites, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Mix of reservable and first-come sites; reservable online. Back-in gravel sites, many accommodating 40-60 ft RVs. --Claude

name: Little Bay de Noc Campground
note: Hiawatha NF campground on Little Bay de Noc (Lake Michigan) south of Rapid River off CR-513. Single-family and two group sites, tent and RV camping, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Reservable online. Walleye fishing; Hunter's Point boat ramp under a mile away. --Claude

name: Monocle Lake Campground
note: Hiawatha NF campground on inland Monocle Lake near Brimley. Vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Reservable online; sites accommodate a range of RV lengths (many 30-50 ft, some pull-throughs). Boat launch and swimming on the lake. --Claude

name: Carp River Campground
note: Rustic Hiawatha NF campground with 20 wooded sites along the Carp Wild and Scenic River, about 8 miles from St. Ignace. Tables, fire rings, water pumps, vault toilets, no hookups. Mix of reservable and first-come sites; reservable online. --Claude

name: Bay View Campground
note: Rustic Hiawatha NF campground on Lake Superior (Whitefish Bay) off the Whitefish Bay Scenic Byway near Brimley. 25 sites beneath pine/oak/maple/birch, a few steps from a sandy beach; tables, fire rings, drinking water, toilets, no hookups. Reservable online (up to 6 months ahead); camp host in summer. --Claude

name: Camp 7 Lake Campground
note: Hiawatha NF campground on 60-acre Camp Seven Lake in the Manistique Ranger District. 39 sites (12 with electric), tables, fire rings, vault toilets, drinking water; paved lakeside walking trail and swimming beach. Reservable online. FS notes some sites are right on the lake. --Claude

name: Chicago Lake Campsites
note: Small primitive Hiawatha NF drive-in cluster at the north end of 188-acre Chicago Lake with an improved boat launch. Now 3 sites (site 1 decommissioned); NO drinking water, primitive toilets only. Sites 2 and 4 are open and accommodate trailers/2 families; site 3 has difficult trailer turning. Reservable online. A 23-ft rig fits sites 2/4; access road is decent gravel. --Claude

name: Widewaters Campground
note: Hiawatha NF campground sandwiched between Irwin Lake and the Indian River, about 16 miles from Munising. 34 forested, well-spaced sites; tables, grills, drinking-water pumps, vault toilets, no hookups. Reservable online. Access point for the Indian River Canoe Trail with boat access at the campground. --Claude

name: Colwell Lake Campground
note: Hiawatha NF campground on the eastern shore of 145-acre Colwell Lake. Many single-family sites plus a group site and two lakeshore tent-only sites; campfire rings, vault toilets, drinking water, boat ramp near the entrance. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Flowing Well Campground
note: Small Hiawatha NF campground on the west bank of the Sturgeon River off FH-13 north of Rapid River. 10 spacious sites, some along the riverbank; tables, fire rings, vault toilets, artesian well water (high iron/sulfur). Reservable online. Sturgeon River Canoe Trail runs through. --Claude

name: Indian River Campground
note: Small Hiawatha NF campground on a bluff overlooking the Indian River, ~20 miles north of Manistique on M-94. 5 large, wooded, private sites; drinking water and vault toilets. First-come, first-served (not on recreation.gov). Convenient overnight stop on the Indian River Canoe Trail. --Claude

name: Petes Lake Campground
note: Hiawatha NF campground on Pete's Lake about 10 miles south of Munising off FH-13. 41 spacious forested sites; picnic tables, grills, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Day-use area with swimming beach and fishing pier; trailhead for Bruno's Run. Reservable online (premium and walk-in tiers exist). --Claude

name: Soldier Lake Campground
note: Hiawatha NF campground surrounding 15-acre Soldier Lake off M-28 near Raco/Brimley. 44 sites among red pine/aspen/maple; tables, fire rings, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Two sandy beaches and day-use picnic areas; North Country Trail access. Reservable online. --Claude

name: Three Lakes Campground
note: Rustic Hiawatha NF campground between Whitmarsh, Brown and Walker Lakes near Eckerman. 10 single-family sites for tent and RV camping; tables, fire rings, vault toilets, drinking water, no hookups. Non-motorized boating, carry-down launch. Sites are first-come, first-served (also listed on recreation.gov). --Claude

name: Corner Lake Campground
note: Rustic Hiawatha NF campground on 100-acre Corner Lake near Wetmore. About 9 large wooded sites with room for RVs; primitive - no drinking water, bring your own. First-come, first-served (not on recreation.gov). OHV camping and lake access. Open mid-May to early Sept. --Claude

name: Island Lake Campground
note: Small quiet Hiawatha NF campground on 32.5-acre Island Lake south of Munising. 11 wooded sites; fire pits, tables, vault toilets, drinking water. Reservable online. One of the few NF campgrounds allowing OHV riding within the campground with direct OHV trail access. Narrow one-way road - rustic, but a general campground (OHV-offering, not OHV-only). --Claude

name: Black River Harbor Campground
note: Ottawa NF developed campground at the Black River mouth near Lake Superior, end of Black River Rd (CR-513) north of Bessemer. 39 paved camping spurs, pressurized water, vault toilets; 23 sites reservable online, 16 FCFS. Seven sites have a Lake Superior overlook. Note: water system out of service for the 2026 season per rec.gov alert. --Claude

name: Marion Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on 317-acre Marion Lake, Watersmeet/Iron River RD. ~37 single drive-in sites (three loops) plus one reservable group site; single sites are FCFS, only the group site books on recreation.gov. Adjacent sandy beach, day-use area, and two boat landings. --Claude

name: Sylvania (Clark Lake) Campground
note: Drive-in Clark Lake Campground in the Sylvania Recreation Area, Ottawa NF (distinct from the Sylvania Wilderness backcountry). 48 sites in four loops; paved access, flush toilets, dump station; some standard-electric sites ($26) and standard non-electric ($22). Mixed reservable/FCFS on recreation.gov. Adjacent day-use swimming beach and canoe landings on Clark Lake. --Claude

name: Sparrow Rapids Campground
note: Small rustic Ottawa NF campground on the East Branch Ontonagon River near Kenton, Kenton RD. 6 sites, vault toilets, no potable water; drive-in via FR 1100. First-come, first-served (not on recreation.gov). River access path between sites 3 and 4. --Claude

name: Bobcat Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on Bobcat Lake, ~3 mi SE of Marenisco, Bessemer RD. 11 sites in one loop, vault toilets, hand-pump water. First-come, first-served (rec.gov listing is scan-and-pay self-registration, not reservable). Sandy beach/day-use area 1/2 mile east. --Claude

name: Burned Dam Campground
note: Rustic developed-dispersed Ottawa NF campground on the Middle Branch Ontonagon River near Watersmeet, drive-in via FR 4500. First-come, first-served, no fee listed; river access for fishing/canoeing. Site count not published; small rustic area - confirm a 23-ft rig fits on arrival. --Claude

name: Courtney Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on 33-acre Courtney Lake, ~22 mi SE of Ontonagon, Ontonagon RD. Single loop, 21 sites (19 drive-in for trailers/motorhomes, 2 walk-in tent; 6 sites have horse corrals). Vault toilets, pressurized water. First-come, first-served. Adjacent boat landing. --Claude

name: Golden Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on Golden Lake (deep cold trout lake), Watersmeet/Iron River RD. 22 sites in a single loop, vault toilets; each site has table, fire ring, lantern post, parking spur. First-come, first-served, $16/night. Adjacent boat ramp and picnic area. --Claude

name: Lake Ottawa Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground in the Lake Ottawa Recreation Area on Lake Ottawa, ~7 mi W of Iron River, Watersmeet/Iron River RD. 32 non-electric sites; paved access, pressurized water, flush toilets, dump station. Reservable on recreation.gov, $22/night. --Claude

name: Moosehead Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on Moosehead Lake, Bessemer RD near Marenisco. Drive-in sites, vault toilets. First-come, first-served (rec.gov listing is scan-and-pay self-registration, not reservable). --Claude

name: Norway Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on Norway Lake among red pines, ~8 mi SE of Sidnaw, Kenton RD. 27 drive-in sites, vault toilets; each site has tent pad, table, fire ring, parking spur. First-come, first-served, $22/night. Adjacent boat landing and picnic area; sites naturally screened by vegetation. --Claude

name: Perch Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on 994-acre Perch Lake, Kenton RD near Watton. 20 sites in a single loop, accommodates tents/trailers/motorhomes. Reservable on recreation.gov in season, FCFS self-registration in October. Adjacent boat ramp. --Claude

name: Pomeroy Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on Pomeroy Lake, Bessemer RD near Marenisco/Watersmeet. 11 drive-in sites in a single loop for tents/trailers/motorhomes (one RV or trailer per site). First-come, first-served (rec.gov listing is scan-and-pay self-registration). --Claude

name: Sturgeon River Campground
note: Small rustic Ottawa NF campground along the Sturgeon River, ~5 mi N of Sidnaw via FR 2200, Kenton RD. 9 sites, vault toilets. First-come, first-served, $16/night. Drive-in; confirm a 23-ft rig fits on arrival (length not published). --Claude

name: Bob Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on 133-acre Bob Lake, ~18 mi SE of Ontonagon, Ontonagon RD. 17 drive-in sites in a single loop for tents/trailers/motorhomes, vault toilets. First-come, first-served, $22/night. Adjacent free boat launch; many sites situated near the lake. --Claude

name: Henry Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on Henry Lake, Bessemer RD near Marenisco. Drive-in sites, vault toilets. First-come, first-served (rec.gov listing is scan-and-pay self-registration, not reservable). --Claude

name: Imp Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on 111-acre (no-wake) Imp Lake, Watersmeet/Iron River RD. 22 sites in two loops, vault toilets, boat ramp. First-come, first-served, $22/night. FS page notes lakeside sites with a view of a small loon-nesting island, off-lake sites set among hemlocks. --Claude

name: Lake Ste. Kathryn Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on 151-acre Lake Ste. Kathryn, Kenton RD near Trout Creek. 24 drive-in sites, vault toilets, hand-pump water; each site has table, fire ring, lantern post. First-come, first-served, $22/night. Adjacent boat ramp; small beach/picnic area near site 13. --Claude

name: Langford Lake Campground
note: Ottawa NF campground on Langford Lake, ~11 mi SE of Marenisco, Bessemer RD. Single loop, 11 sites with accessible wood tables, fire rings, and parking spurs; two vault toilets, hand-pump water; sites screened by natural vegetation. First-come, first-served. (A 2019 third-party listing flagged it closed, but the Forest Service still lists it as an active campground.) --Claude

name: Canoe Harbor State Forest Campground
note: MI DNR state forest campground on the South Branch of the AuSable River (Mason Tract); 41 sites for tent/small-trailer use plus 4 walk-in/canoe sites, vault toilets, hand-pump well, no hookups. First-come, first-served; canoeing and trout fishing. --Claude

name: Garnet Lake State Forest Campground
note: MI DNR state forest campground on Garnet Lake in the eastern UP; 10 rustic sites for tent/small-trailer use, vault toilets, hand-pump well, no hookups. First-come, first-served; larger rigs may find sites tight. --Claude

name: Merwin Creek State Forest Campground
note: MI DNR state forest campground on the Manistique River near Gulliver; 10 rustic sites for tent/small-trailer use, vault toilets, hand-pump well, no hookups; gravel access road. First-come, first-served; best for smaller rigs. --Claude

name: Spaulding Lake Campground
note: Private family RV park in SW Michigan (Niles), open Apr 1-Oct 31. 120+ full-hookup sites, 30/50-amp, pull-throughs for big rigs, swimming/fishing ponds, laundry. Takes nightly transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Amigo Park Family Campground
note: Private family campground on Perrin Lake near Sturgis. Primitive (no-hookup) and modern electric sites, free hot showers, dump station, swimming beach, boat rentals; alcohol-free park. Takes nightly transient (one-night deposit). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Steinke Resort
note: Family-owned campground (since 1932) on Long Lake near Burr Oak, open Apr 15-Oct 15. ~143 sites, mix of seasonal and transient plus modern/rustic cabins; fishing piers, boat launch, dock slips, swimming beach, store. Reservable on Campspot. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Sunset Cove Resort
note: Family-owned (50+ yrs) campground on Marble Lake near Quincy, 30+ acres. Partial-hookup RV and tent sites, direct beach access, boat launch/dock rentals, general store. Offers both seasonal and overnight transient camping. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Totem Pole Park
note: Private campground near Petersburg with full-hookup RV and tent sites, 30/50-amp, back-in and pull-through, adjacent a small lake. Takes nightly transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wilderness Campground
note: Private gated campground within Dundee city limits along the River Raisin, with a sand-beach swimming lake. Daily, weekly, monthly and seasonal RV camping; quiet, no highway noise, near US-23/M-50. Takes nightly transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cranberry Lake Campground
note: Privately-owned quiet family campground on Cranberry Lake near Marcellus, open May 1-Oct 1. ~60-101 sites incl. 42 full-hookup and 18 elec/water, 30/50-amp; swimming beach, boat launch, store. Some sites directly on the water. Takes nightly transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: En Gedi Resort & Canoe Livery
note: Family campground on the St. Joseph River near Leonidas with on-site canoe/kayak livery, open Memorial Day-Labor Day. ~30 sites: 4 full-hookup, rest water/electric, plus primitive and cottages; allows rigs up to 50 ft; alcohol/marijuana-free Christian-oriented park. Takes nightly transient. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Potawatomi Campground & Recreation Area
note: Family-owned (50+ yrs) campground on Fox Lake (chain of 3 non-motorized lakes) near Union City, open Apr 15-Oct 15. 180 sites, rustic plus some full-hookup, rigs up to 60 ft; large sandy beach, fishing dock, swimming. Seasonal and transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Waffle Farm Campgrounds
note: Large family RV park on the North Chain of 7 Lakes (Craig/Morrison lakes) near Coldwater. Full-hookup 30/50-amp sites incl. ~30 waterfront sites, big-rig pull-throughs, full-service marina/boat ramp, store. Mostly seasonal (240 seasonal) but offers transient sites. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Somerset Beach Campground & Retreat Center
note: Nonprofit campground/retreat center (since 1982) on Somerset Lake near Somerset Center, 226 acres. 150+ electric sites, full-hookup options, RVs up to 70 ft, pull-throughs and back-ins, 15/20/30/50-amp; private swimming beach, boat rentals, trails. Takes nightly transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Camp Sequoia
note: Family-owned (since 1953) campground near Adrian, open May 1-Oct 1. 114 sites, large 30x60 grassy level sites with electric and 60 elec/water (no sewer on site), 30/50-amp; swimming pool, fishing lakes, trails. Caters to seasonal but accepts nightly transient (~$40; Oct 2025 reviews confirm operating - one stale directory lists it closed). RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Eden Springs Park at the House of David
note: Historic year-round RV campground on M-139 in Benton Harbor (former House of David community, on National Register). 42 full-service pull-through gravel sites, 30/50-amp, water/sewer suitable for all RV sizes; Amish-built cabins; adjacent historic park with vintage train. Takes nightly transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). No standalone official website found - listed via Facebook/directories. --Claude

name: Timber Trails RV Park
note: RV park on Lake of the Woods (sand-bottom lake) near Decatur, open May 1-Oct 15. 162 full-hookup sites, 30/50-amp, 16 pull-throughs for big rigs. Primarily seasonal but daily/transient sites available. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hemlock Lake Campground
note: Family campground with 1,700 ft frontage on Hemlock Lake near Marcellus, open May-Oct. ~77-85 sites all with water/electric, sewer/50-amp/pull-through, big-rig friendly up to 50 ft; boating, swimming, fishing. Daily, weekly and monthly rates (transient). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Twin Pines Campground & Canoe Livery
note: Family-owned campground on the Kalamazoo River, ~50 acres, open May-mid-Oct. Electric+water hookup sites plus primitive; restrooms/showers, camp store, canoe/kayak livery. Nightly transient ~$35-40. Official .org domain was unreachable from the sandbox but the campground is well-confirmed operating (active Facebook, dated reviews). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wildwood Acres Campground
note: Family-run since 1970, ~26 wooded acres, ~125 sites (electric+water, a few pull-throughs, ~25 tent), open ~Apr 15-Oct 1. On-site 25-acre lake plus Kalamazoo River access; canoe/kayak rentals. Nightly transient, reservable (RoverPass). No standalone official website found; booking/listing via RoverPass and aggregators. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Ja Do Campground
note: ~60 sites on flat grassy/wooded Irish Hills terrain near US-12, open ~May 1-Oct 15. Electric+water plus primitive/partial sites; tent camping. Small spring-fed fishing pond set back in the woods. Nightly transient. Large grassy sites; 23-ft RV fits. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tri-Lake Trails Campground
note: Private family campground, 300 acres with 3 small fishing lakes (boat rentals, swimming), ~272 sites; 30-amp electric+water, no sewer (dump station). Season ~May 1-Oct 1. Mix of nightly transient + seasonal; nightly bookings genuine and reservable online (RoverPass). Official trilaketrails.com was unreachable from the sandbox; well-confirmed operating (active FB, dated reviews, online booking). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Dune Lake Campground
note: Private nature-focused campground in SW Michigan, ~51 modern sites (20/30-amp electric+water) plus ~26 primitive; restrooms, dump service. Season ~May-Sep/Oct. Small ~1-acre swim-only on-site lake. Nightly transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Greenwood Acres Family Campground
note: Large private RV park (~1,100 sites / 300 acres) on Goose Lake, season Apr 1-Oct 31. Heavily seasonal (~950 sites seasonal) but ~150 sites bookable nightly/weekly transient online (3-night min holiday weekends). Water/sewer/30-50 amp, big-rig pull-throughs; designated lakefront sites cap at 28-ft RV. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Gilmore Car Museum Campground
note: General overnight camping on the museum grounds (Apr 1-Oct 31), not event-only. ~100 sites: 2x 50A, 4x 30A, 8x 20A, plus unlimited non-electric; rustic camping on a level mowed grass field, no water/sewer, no grey/black discharge. Restrooms (no showers). $20-$50/night; museum admission required per night. Open grass field, 23-ft RV fits easily. Reservable online. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Rockey's Campground
note: Private campground on Silvers Lake (Chain of Five Lakes), ~May-Sep. ~100 grassy/shaded water+electric (20/30-amp) sites; dump station, sandy beach, boat ramp. Daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal mix with genuine nightly bookings (reservable online). Allstays lists 45-ft max; 23-ft fits. RV Life coord was off ~0.5 km E; corrected. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hell Creek Ranch & Campground
note: Private family campground (NOT horse-only; runs on-site stables for trail rides but is a general campground), 100 sites — 60 electric/30-amp+water, 40 primitive; 60-ft max length, 23-ft fits. Apr 1-Oct 31, nightly transient ~$20-35, reservable. Borders Pinckney/Waterloo Rec Area state land. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Whispering Waters Campground
note: Private family-run campground on the Thornapple River, Barry County, ~26 wooded acres, late Apr-early Oct. Full-hookup pull-through and water/electric sites. Nightly rates confirmed ($60 FHU, $40 E-only) and reservable online despite a seasonal/long-term lean. 23-ft fits easily. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tyler Creek Golf Course & Campground
note: Private golf course + 203-site campground along Tyler Creek (trout stream) W of Grand Rapids. Rustic ($33), water/electric ($50), full-hookup w/ sewer ($63); 20/30/50-amp, 10 pull-throughs. Allstays 40-ft max; 23-ft fits. Nightly transient confirmed (2-night min weekends); seasonal lots also offered. ~Apr 15-end Oct. Reservations by phone/email (no online booking). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Walnut Hills Family Campground by GatherGrounds
note: 45-acre family campground on the Shiawassee River, ~203 grassy sites — RV sites with water/electric (some full hookup), primitive tent sites, and cabins. About half the sites seasonal but nightly transient RV reservations available online (RV from ~$49). On-site canoe livery, swimming lake with beach + water park. RV length cap not published; spacious grassy sites. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Chapel in the Pines Family Campground
note: ~100 sites — 26 tent, 26 electric-only, 74 electric+water; 'upfront' sites have water/electric, 'back' sites no water. Mix of seasonal campers and nightly weekender bookings (~$44); reservations by phone. On-site small lake with sandy beach for swimming/fishing. Spacious grassy family park; 23-ft fits. Official site is a Google Sites page. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lakeside Resort Campground
note: Private 66-site family campground on a private lake just south of Ionia; large grassy full-hookup sites (50-amp electric, water, sewer), picnic table + fire ring each. Full-service RV sites plus rental travel trailers; bookable online via Campspot. Reviews note the majority of campers are seasonals, but nightly transient sites are available. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Highway to Haven Family Campground
note: Former Campers Paradise (800 Robbins Rd), rebranded Highway to Haven Family Campground under new ownership (reopened 2022; further facelift 2026). 100+ large shaded sites, independent water + 20/30-amp electric; hot showers, laundry, dump, store, dog park. Nightly transient reservations accepted online; ~2 mi from Lake Michigan / Grand Haven Beach (urban setting, near US-31). RVs to 50 ft. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Hilltop Campground
note: Family-owned ~60-site campground on Lake Nepessing (all-sports lake), >500 ft of shoreline with sandy swim beach and boat launch. Every site has 50-amp electric, water, sewer, picnic table, fire ring; mature-tree rolling terrain. Store, laundry, showers, propane. Open ~May 1-Oct 31. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Maple River Campground
note: ~63-site private campground on the banks of the Maple River, surrounded by ~3,500 acres of state land; tree-canopied riverfront sites plus primitive sites. Electric+water sites with 20/30/50-amp service, dump station, bath house, fishing/kayaking/canoeing. Nightly transient plus weekly/monthly/seasonal rates ($10 deposit, book direct). Open ~May 1-Nov 1. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Cedar Springs RV Resort
note: Private 25-acre RV park (formerly Lakeside Camp Park), ~157 sites with full and partial hookups (20/30/50A); mix of seasonal areas plus daily/weekend transient sites, back-in pads. Stocked private lake with sandy swimming beach, kayak/paddleboat rentals, clubhouse. Books via Campspot; 2026 season May 1-Oct 31. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Chinook Campground
note: Private RV/tent campground on the Muskegon River; grass sites, 20/30-amp E/W and some E/W/S, dump station, pool, boat launch, tube/kayak rentals. Mix of nightly transient and seasonal lots (seasonal lots currently full/waitlist; non-seasonal nightly Apr 25-Oct 1, season Apr 25-Oct 25). Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: St. Lorenz Churchgrove & Campground
note: Lutheran-church-run campground open to the public, ~65 grass RV/tent sites, ~50 with water, 30-amp, dump station, showers. First-come-first-served, no reservations; ~$40/night. Open May 1 through last weekend of October. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: St. Louis Campground
note: Church of God (Anderson, IN)-affiliated campground but open to the public for nightly transient RV camping; 120+ sites all with water/electric, 30-amp $30/night and 50-amp $35/night (also weekly/monthly), up to ~40 ft rigs, cabins/dorms also rentable. Open ~May 1 through October. Reserve by phone. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Lake of Dreams Campground
note: Non-membership family RV campground, ~250 modern sites all with water/electric, 2 bathhouses, 2 dump stations. 4-acre dug swim lake with sandy beach, mini-waterpark, putt-putt. Nightly transient (~$40) plus seasonal; open mid-April to early October. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Wesleyan Woods Camp
note: State-licensed PUBLIC RV campground (religiously affiliated, alcohol-free) on 160 acres along the Cass River; ~226 sites, 120 full-hookup, plus rustic tent sites and cabins. Public can book nightly online via Campspot (not members-only). River canoe/kayak trips. Open May 1-Oct 15. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: River Ridge Family Campground
note: Family-run campground on the Pine River, ~150 sites (about 50 seasonal, rest nightly transient) with full and partial hookups; heated pool, playground, river access for kayak/canoe/fishing, camp store. Open May 1-Oct 15. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Stony Haven Campground & Cabins
note: Small (8-acre) family campground, owner-run since 1996; 48 RV sites (partial & full hookup, 13 seasonal) plus 16 rustic tent sites and cabins. About 1 mile from Stony Lake and 2 miles from Lake Michigan public access; no on-site swimming/lake frontage. Open May 15-Oct 31. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Soaring Eagle Hideaway RV Park
note: Tribally owned (Saginaw Chippewa) RV park, 67 full-hookup sites with 50' concrete pads and asphalt approaches on 42-acre Grewe's Lake; no tents. Nightly transient bookable via Campspot; complimentary shuttle to Soaring Eagle Casino/Waterpark. A genuine RV park, not a casino overnight lot. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Saginaw Chippewa Campground
note: Tribally owned (Saginaw Chippewa) campground at 7529 E Tomah Rd (locally 'The Hill'), ~46 shaded sites, 20/30/50-amp, pull-throughs to ~65 ft, pool/showers/laundry/dump station; self-check-in, largely FCFS; 24-hr shuttle to Soaring Eagle Casino. RVs/campers only (no tents). Distinct from Soaring Eagle Hideaway. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Cran-Hill Ranch Family Campground
note: Christian camp/retreat that also runs a public family campground (~150 sites incl. ~30 tent), grass/shady sites, mostly 30-amp electric+water with ~6 full hookups and a dump station; open to the public for nightly online reservations, seasonal Apr-Oct. Two on-site lakes. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Martiny Lakes Hide-Away
note: Family-run lakeside campground (~56 sites) on the Martiny Chain of Lakes; drive-in RV sites with electric/water (Hipcamp listing confirms full hookups, rigs to ~40 ft), plus tent sites and rentals; nightly bookable, seasonal ~May-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Sebewaing River Campground
note: Campground on the Sebewaing River (to Saginaw Bay) at 541 Union St; ~54-59 large full-hookup sites (water/sewer/20-30-50 amp) plus ~10 tent sites, boat ramp, rental docks; nightly ($70, 2-night min weekends) and seasonal stays, season ~May-Dec. Own commercial site; no Village ownership confirmed, treated private (NOT the separate Huron County 'Sebewaing County Park'). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Lake Michigan Family Campground
note: Former United Methodist camp (now a Grace Outside property) at 5807 Ridge Rd, open to the public; ~100 wooded RV sites with water/electric (20/30/50 amp, limited full-hookup), on 150 acres of forest and dunes with a half-mile private Lake Michigan beach via stairs/boardwalk. Reservations by phone/email. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Whispering Oaks Campground & Cabins
note: Private wooded campground on M-37 south of Baldwin (since 1969). RV sites with water/electric (20/30/50-amp, no sewer), pull-thru and back-in, max RV ~60 ft; also cabins, lodges, and primitive tent sites. On-site water is a swimming pool only. Mixed transient + seasonal, nightly transient prominently offered (~$45-55, 2026); book by phone/email. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Kit's Hillside Camp & Cabins
note: Private family-run campground at 6170 100th Ave outside Evart (distinct from Evart's city Riverside Park). Full-hookup, all pull-thru RV sites on spacious grassy ground (fits 23 ft); also primitive/tent sites and 5 small cabins. ~2-acre on-site swim/trout pond. Book by phone. Official domain flaky on fetch - soft strike, but Hipcamp listing + recent reviews confirm operating. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: River Country Campground
note: Private family campground and canoe livery on the Muskegon River at Evart (~153 sites). Full-hookup and electric/water RV sites, pull-thru and back-in, 20/30/50-amp, sewer, dump station; tent sites, camp store, mini-golf. Nightly transient via Campspot (~$30-40) plus a separate seasonal section; fits 23 ft. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Timber Surf Camping Resort
note: Family-owned campground on Round Lake near Fountain, ~70 sites with water/electric and 21 full hookups, 20/30/50 amp, back-in and pull-through; takes rigs to 50 ft. Mix of nightly transient and seasonal sites. Online booking via Campspot; sandy swim beach, kayak/boat rentals. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Harrison RV Family Campground
note: Formerly Camp Withii; licensed ~88-site campground under new ownership since 2020, on Hampton Rd in Harrison. Rustic, electric, water/electric, and 30/50-amp full-hookup sites; takes rigs to 50 ft. Welcomes nightly transient guests alongside a seasonal program; also open year-round for shorter hunter/snowmobiler stays. Not on water. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Hidden Hill Family Campground
note: Wooded family campground operating since 1979 off US-127 at 300 N Clare Ave, Harrison; open ~Apr 15-Oct 15. 44 RV sites (most full hookup water/sewer, metered 50/30/20 amp, 6 big-rig pull-throughs) plus ~11 primitive sites; camp store, laundry, showers. Takes nightly transient guests. Not on water. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Dodge City Campground
note: Private wooded campground in an oak setting near Harrison; ~50 sites, 30 full-hookup and 20 modern (water/electric). Transient family park, by reservation. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Bird Creek RV Resort
note: New (2023) private RV park off M-25 near downtown Port Austin; room for ~65 campers, fire pits. A limited number of seasonal sites reserved, remainder daily/transient. No standalone official site found (Facebook is primary channel). RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Farmview Resort
note: Private campground off US-31 in Free Soil surrounded by farmland; 40 sites (12 full-hookup, 15 elec/water, 13 elec-only), pull-throughs, accepts RVs to 40 ft. Daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal rates offered (transient available). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Irons RV Park and Campground
note: Family-owned 10-acre campground in the Huron-Manistee NF near Irons; 68 sites (46 full-hookup, 22 water/electric) plus tent area, pool, ORV-trail access. Transient, reservable online. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Cadillac Woods Campground
note: Private wooded campground on M-115 SE of Cadillac; 37 sites (electric-only and elec/water, 15 pull-thru) plus rustic cabins, pool, mini-golf. Max RV length ~30 ft (fits 23-ft rig). Open May-Oct, transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Loomis Landing Campground and Marina
note: Private campground and marina on the backwaters of Tippy Dam (Manistee River impoundment) near Wellston, surrounded by Huron-Manistee NF; full-hookup RV sites, boat slips, launch and fuel dock. Transient, reservable. RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Kestelwoods Campground
note: Family-owned campground between Cadillac and Manistee in the Manistee NF; 96 sites all with water/30-amp electric, heated pool, near the Pine River. Open Apr-Oct, transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Greenwood Campground
note: Family-owned ~50-site campground near Alger; most sites water/electric (30-amp), clubhouse with game room. Near Greenwood Lake (no-wake, sandy beach just west of camp) and Rifle River Rec Area. Transient. No standalone official domain found; michigan.org listing used. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Houghton Lake Travel Park Campground
note: Private family campground near Houghton Lake; ~70 camper sites plus primitive tent sites and cabins, heated pool, store, game room. Transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Shady Oaks Campground & RV Park
note: Private campground between Au Gres and Tawas City along the Au Gres East Branch River; electric/water RV sites (RVs have sewer/water) plus tent areas, playground. Transient. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tawas RV Park
note: Private RV-only park (no tents) on 40 wooded acres at 1453 Townline Rd, Tawas City; 59 grass/treed sites, full and basic hookups, cement patios, cable TV. Minutes from Lake Huron shoreline (not on-water). Open May-Oct, transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Northern Exposure Campground
note: Large private campground on 300 wooded acres with 2.5 mi of frontage on Hodenpyle Pond (Manistee River impoundment) near Mesick; ~260 sites, waterfront and inland, paved roads, full hookups, accepts RVs to 40 ft. Open Apr-Oct, transient, reservable via Campspot. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Kampvilla RV Park & Family Campground
note: Private 40-acre family campground on Pleasanton Hwy near Bear Lake; 96 large grassy sites from rustic to full-hookup pull-thru (50-amp), pool, catch-and-release fishing pond. ~5 mi from Lake Michigan shoreline (not on-water). Transient. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Homestead Resort on the Betsie River
note: Small private river resort on the Betsie River at Homestead Dam, Benzonia. ~4 drive-in RV sites with full hookups (water/electric/sewer) plus 7 cabins; books online. River/boat-launch access. RV Life 4*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Paddle Brave Camp & Canoe
note: Private campground and canoe/kayak livery on the South Branch AuSable River, Roscommon. 43 sites (33 electric 20/30-amp, 10 rustic/tent), accepts RVs/travel trailers up to 40 ft; walk-ins or online reservations. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Betsie River Campsite
note: Small family-owned campground on the Betsie River, 1923 River Road near Frankfort/Elberta. Mix of primitive tent sites and electric RV sites (20-amp and 30-amp pull-throughs); also rustic cabins. Self-described as 'not a big concrete pad RV park' but accommodates campers/travel trailers; bathhouse, cafe, fish station. Books via ResNexus. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Kalkaska RV Park and Campground
note: Private RV park at 580 M-72 SE on the edge of Kalkaska; ~93-105 sites including full-hookup 30/50-amp pull-throughs (big rigs to 60 ft), pool, laundry, 40 acres of trails. Open Apr-Oct. Not on water. Online reservations. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Trails Campground
note: Private ORV/ATV-staging campground at 4066 Old 27 N north of Frederic; 58 sites with electric (30-amp) and water plus wooded rustic sites, pull-throughs, bathhouse, laundry. Direct ATV/snowmobile trail access. Official site cert was expired at audit time (strike) but well-confirmed operating (active Facebook/Yelp/michigan.org). Not on water. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: River Park Campground
note: Private campground on the East Branch AuSable River, 2607 Peters Rd, Grayling. 97 sites (24 electric-only, 43 electric+water, 20/30-amp), grassy/shaded; swimming beach, store, rec room. ~Half seasonal lots, half daily/transient. Open mid-May to mid-Oct. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Mio Pine Acres Campground
note: Family-run private campground at 1215 W 8th St, ~1 mi west of Mio on M-72. 75 sites (35 electric-only, 40 electric+water, 20/30/50-amp), 12 pull-throughs, big rigs to 45 ft; dump, laundry, store, mini-golf. ~1 mi from AuSable River (not on it). Open May-Nov; honors Passport America discount (not a membership-only park). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Antrim Meadows Campground
note: Small private campground (~23-25 sites) at 764 E Elder Rd south of Mancelona; full-hookup and W/E RV sites (30-amp), pull-thrus fit big rigs, plus rustic tent sites and 2 cabins. Transient/reservable online; basecamp for the Antrim Chain of Lakes (no on-site frontage). RV Life 4*/2$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Paul Bunyan Family Campground
note: Private family campground at 2498 E Hubert Rd, Spruce; ~36 grassy sites with 30-amp full and W/E hookups (back-in and pull-thru) plus tent sites. Pool, clubhouse, store; reservable online via Campspot. Off US-23 ~15 min from Alpena, near Lake Huron's BeeBee Beach and across from Thunder Bay River State Forest (no on-site water). RV Life 4.5*/2$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Campers Cove RV Park & Canoe Livery
note: Private 26-acre campground at 5005 Long Rapids Rd with frontage on the Thunder Bay River and Lake Wynah; 98 sites incl. full-hookup RV (rigs to ~50 ft) and rustic, plus canoe/kayak livery and boat launch. Open ~May 1-mid Oct; reservable online via RezExpert. RV Life 4.5*/2$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Elkwood Campground
note: Private family campground on 73 wooded acres, 65 sites (rustic/tent, W+E 30/50 amp, full-hookup pull-throughs up to 50 amp); mix of nightly/weekly and seasonal (seasonal $1800-2000/yr). Pool, rec room, playground, nature trails. Reserve on the website. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Chalk Hills Campground
note: Privately-owned 25-site campground in a single loop near the Chalk Hills Dam on the Menominee River (UP, MI side). 30/50 amp electric + water, pull-throughs, big-rig friendly; large shaded pads. Open mid-April to late fall, ~$20/night, reserve by phone/email (no online booking). Short trail to the river via adjacent WE Energies land. RV Life 5*/$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Island Resort & Casino RV Park
note: Standalone bookable transient RV park (separate amenity from the casino), 42 modern sites with electric+water (no sewer; dump station), 15 pull-throughs, fire rings, pool, laundry, shuttle to casino. Open May 1-Nov 30, ~$35/night, register at hotel front desk, reserve by phone (no online booking); tents not allowed. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Park Place of the North
note: Small private campground in Delta County off M-35 NE of Escanaba: 25 sites (20 RV with electric+water, 5 tent), 13 pull-throughs, wooded, restrooms/showers/laundry, dump station, playground. Open May 1-Oct 15. Transient. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Whispering Valley Campground & RV Park
note: Private RV park on US-2, Rapid River. Pull-through full-hookup sites (water/sewer/30 & 50-amp), level, big-rig friendly; laundry, propane, showers, free WiFi, one rental cabin. Daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal stays; transient-oriented. Reservable online via RoverPass. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Tiki RV Park & Campground
note: Private family-owned RV park (50+ years), 200 S Airport Rd, St. Ignace. 100+ sites: full-hookup, water/electric, electric-only, and primitive; 20/30/50-amp, pull-thru and back-in; fits RVs to ~43 ft. Dump station, laundry, showers. Walking distance to Mackinac Island ferry docks. Transient/vacation-oriented. Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Elli's Lazy Days Resort and Campground
note: Small private campground (~10 sites) on the west shore of Indian Lake near Kitch-iti-kipi and Hiawatha NF. Large sites with fire ring + electric (30/50-amp); water via ground pump; vault toilets, shower room. Rental cabin; kayak/canoe/paddleboat rentals. Drive-in on Co Rd 455. Taking 2026 reservations by phone. Lightly reviewed; no standalone official website (operator runs a Facebook page) -- minor strike, but confirmed currently operating. --Claude

name: Reddmann's Sleepy Hollow Campground
note: Rustic family-run campground on Lake Huron (St. Martin Bay) N of St Ignace via ~2 mi dirt road (passable; a 37-ft motorhome reported easy access). ~16-50 sites: mostly non-electric dry (~$15, FCFS, cash) plus ~6 electric 30-amp sites reservable by phone. Vault toilets, well water, fire rings, sandy beach. No standalone official website -- minor strike, but well-confirmed operating (Campendium 5.0*). FCFS for dry sites; phone for electric. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Loons Point RV Park and Campground
note: Private campground on the Les Cheneaux channel of Lake Huron, 1.5 mi E of Cedarville. ~54 grassy level RV sites (full-hookup + water/electric, some 50-amp), plus tent sites, a cabin and guest house. Small swim beach, dock, kayak rentals; premium waterfront sites. Open ~May 15-Oct 1. Reserve by phone (no online booking). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Trailhead Campground & RV Park (Drummond Island)
note: Private commercial campground at the Trailhead bar/restaurant, Drummond Island. ~17-19 numbered sites with concrete pads: tent/rustic ($40), 30-amp water/electric (waterfront + wooded mix), 50-amp water/electric next to an inland lake ($55). Some pull-through/big-rig. Across from the 117-mi DNR ORV trail. Reservable online via Campspot. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Wa-Wen Resort
note: Family-owned resort on Maxton Bay, Drummond Island. ~25 full-hookup RV sites (50/30/20-amp) plus 8 cottages and a tent area. Transient nightly (~$60) and seasonal ($2,500-3,000 May-Oct). Boat ramp/docks, fish-cleaning house, laundry, heated pool. Reserve by phone (no online booking). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Paint River Hills Campground
note: Private campground on 24-32 acres on bluffs above the Paint River in downtown Crystal Falls. 23 RV sites (10 pull-through, 4 full-hookup; 30/50-amp, dump station), 9 tent sites, 5 cabins; max RV ~65 ft. Showers, WiFi, ATV/state-trail access. Open May 15-Nov 30. Reserve by phone; reported cash-only. Official site is HTTP-only. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Michihistrigan Campground
note: Private 37-acre campground attached to a bar & grill, Gould City. 34 pull-through sites: lots 1-12 full hookup (9-10 are 50-amp), lots 13-35 water+electric; also tent/rustic. ~$35 full hookup / ~$20 boondock; max ~32 ft. Restrooms, showers, dump, laundry, store; on-site bar & grill, mini golf, disc golf, archery, trout ponds. Nightly transient plus some seasonal. Season ~May 1-Dec 1. Reserve by phone/email. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Big Cedar Campground and Canoe Livery
note: Family-owned campground on the Manistique River next to Seney NWR, Germfask. 50 sites all water/electric, 9 full-hookup, 8 pull-through; fits big rigs. Mostly transient/nightly (one monthly site for 2026). Bath house, laundry, fiber WiFi; on-site canoe/kayak/tube livery. Open May 1-Sep 30. Reservable online (ResNexus). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Log Cabin Resort & Campground
note: Resort on Big Manistique Lake, Curtis (D&K Log Cabin Resort & Campground, LLC). 54 campsites -- full-hookup deluxe pull-thru (advertised to 77 ft), water/electric, electric-only, and rustic; nightly $19-$39. Swimming beach, marina/slips, boat & pontoon rentals; 14 cabins separate. Reserve by phone or online (Firefly). Use phone 906-287-0799 (aggregator 906-586-9732 is stale). RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Big Lake Campground
note: Small rustic campground (15 sites) on the east shore of Big Manistique Lake, ~4 mi N of Curtis on H-33; family-run since 1975. 5 Lakefront + 6 Northside lots (Northside easier for big rigs), 30-amp electric + central water; sewer/dump available. Shallow sandy beach, boat launch. Allows rigs to 50 ft. Nightly (~$30) and seasonal. By-phone reservations, no online booking; the official .com domain (biglakecurtis.com) is dead -- using the operator's WordPress page. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Otter Lake Campground and Lodges
note: Private 92-acre campground on Otter Lake near Hiawatha NF and Pictured Rocks, Munising. 50+ nightly sites: rustic tent, 20/30-amp electric-only, and water/electric; dump, showers, laundry, store. Some pull-throughs, max RV ~45 ft. Cabins also offered. ORV trail access; kayak/canoe/SUP rentals; swimming. Season May 21-Sep 30. Reservable online (ResNexus). RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Northland Outfitters Campground
note: Private RV/tent campground & canoe/kayak livery on the Manistique River (M-77), Germfask; est. 1972. ~18 sites: 1-4 water/electric, 5-10 electric with potable water (site 5 the only pull-through), 11-14 no-hookup/tent; 4 cabins. No sewer; dump on site. Max RV 75 ft. Showers, store, boat launch. Transient (nightly ~$44). Season May 1-Oct 15. Reservable online. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Larry's RV Park & Cabins
note: Small lakeside RV park (~4 full-hookup grass RV sites: water/electric/sewer) plus rental cabins on Helmer Bay of Big Manistique Lake, McMillan. Big drive-in sites -- firsthand review: 'huge sites close to the waters edge.' Transient nightly ~$40, reservable online via RoverPass. Pets/tents OK. Official owner site (michiganlakeshorerealestate.net) was unreachable during research (timeouts / expired TLS) -- verify it still loads; minor strike. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Clear Lake Campground
note: Privately-owned ~80-site RV/tent campground 14 mi S of Sault Ste. Marie on S Mackinac Trail; 25 pull-thrus, 20 electric-only + 60 electric/water sites, 20/30-amp, dump, laundry, sandy beach on Clear Lake. Accommodates rigs to ~50 ft, so a 23-ft RV fits. Nightly/transient bookable (also offers seasonals) mid-May to Oct 1. RV Life 5star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Bond Falls Consolidated Campground
note: Rustic drive-in campground (~20+ sites, West/Consolidated + East loops) on the Bond Falls Flowage, administered by Upper Peninsula Power Company (UPPCo utility - private, not USFS despite being inside Ottawa NF). Free camping, FCFS (14-night max); a host visits ~11am to issue the required permit. No hookups; vault toilets, hand-pump well. Packed-sand roads; big-rig friendly (longest reported 34 ft), so a 23-ft RV fits. FCFS - not on recreation.gov. RV Life 4.5star/$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Au Train Lake Campground
note: USFS Hiawatha National Forest drive-in campground at the south end of Au Train Lake. 37 large wooded back-in gravel sites; reservable on recreation.gov with some FCFS. No hookups (vault toilets, drinking water). Per-site max RV length 45-60 ft, so a 23-ft RV fits. Songbird interpretive trail and lake access for boating/fishing. RV Life 4.5star/$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Catchin' Crickets Campground & Vacation Rentals
note: Rustic family campground ~9 mi E of Munising near Pictured Rocks (off Co Rd H-15). 21 rustic + 4 electric-only sites; hand-pump water, vault/pit toilets, picnic table + fire ring per site. No water/sewer hookups (dry camping). Official site-length cap 30 ft but reviewers report fitting 40-ft fifth wheels, so a 23-ft rig fits; gravel/grass pads. FCFS (no online reservations). Transient/short-stay; cabin rental also offered. RV Life 5star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Kritters Northcountry Campground and Cabins
note: Full-service campground on M-123 ~8.5 mi N of M-28, base for Tahquamenon Falls / Pictured Rocks. ~50 spacious grassy sites, 40 pull-throughs; mix of full-hookup (8), elec-water (24), electric-only (8) and rustic; 30/50-amp, dump, showers, laundry, cable, WiFi. Big-rig friendly - 23-ft RV fits easily. Reservable online (Campspot). Season ~mid-May to mid-Oct; cabins year-round. Transient short-stay focus, not a seasonal/full-timer park. RV Life 4.5star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Bay Mills Casino - Sunrise RV Park
note: Tribal casino (Bay Mills Indian Community) RV park - a real dedicated park (~120 designated gravel sites), full hookups (30/50A, water, sewer), free cable/WiFi; on-site restrooms, showers, laundry. Overlooks the Back Bay (Waiska Bay) of Lake Superior; walking distance to casino. Bookable nightly, public (some sources say FCFS/first-choice on arrival). RV Life 4star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Ojibwa Casino RV Park (Marquette)
note: Tribal casino (Keweenaw Bay Indian Community) RV park at Harvey/Marquette - a real dedicated park, 57 full-hookup sites incl. 14 pull-throughs, fire rings + picnic tables, full bathhouse/restrooms, dump station. Bookable nightly online via Campspot, public; ~$38/night. Coords pinned to actual RV sites, not casino center. RV Life 4.5star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Kewadin Casino RV Park (Sault Ste. Marie)
note: Tribal casino (Sault Ste. Marie Tribe of Chippewa) RV park - a real dedicated park, 64 sites incl. 20 pull-throughs, 30A electric + water hookups, dump/water-fill station, shower houses, free laundry; hotel pool/sauna access. Open May-Oct, 'open to all,' ~$25/night, bookable via 1-800-KEWADIN; max ~70 ft. RV Life 4star/$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Soo Locks Campground
note: Private RV campground on the St. Marys River in downtown Sault Ste. Marie, a short walk from the Soo Locks (watch freighters lock through). ~100 sites, water/electric (no full hookups), 9 pull-throughs; dump station, restrooms, camp store, rec room. Open seasonally ~May 1-Oct 20, reservable (book early). Transient only; sites are tight but big rigs / 23-ft fit on the pull-throughs and corner sites. RV Life 4star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: JoeIda Campground
note: Small secluded campground on Russ Lake Rd ~1 mi off H-58, between Munising and Grand Marais near the Pictured Rocks area, minutes from Kingston Lake. 8 large secluded sites, each w/ fire ring + picnic table (handicap accessible); vault toilet + hand-pump well, no hookups (dry camping). Welcomes tents/truck campers/trailers; official guidance under 40 ft (recommend <36 ft) - a 23-ft RV fits, large motorhomes not recommended. FCFS plus advance reservations; from $30/night. Phone unverified (directories list conflicting 906-387-1221 / 877-778-2683 - omitted). RV Life 5star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Ojibwa Casino RV Park (Baraga)
note: Tribal casino (Keweenaw Bay Indian Community) RV park - a real dedicated park, 12 paved full-hookup sites (20/30/50A, water, sewer), WiFi, picnic tables, newer bathrooms; hotel pool/sauna/hot tub access. Bookable online via Campspot / hotel front desk, public. Small but genuine designated-site park, not overflow. RV Life 4star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: River Road RV Park
note: Private RV park/campground at 600 River Rd, Ontonagon, ~1 mi from downtown along the Ontonagon River near the marina (Lake Superior nearby). ~40 sites, all pull-through, 30/50 amp, full hookups plus shaded tent sites; laundry, showers, dump, WiFi, playground. Open year-round; mix of seasonal and transient sites, reservations accepted (also Hipcamp/RoverPass). Fits large rigs (longest reported 37 ft), so 23-ft fits. Verify official site loads before relying on it (FB page shows a stale 'permanently closed' flag; aggregators show open). RV Life 4star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Shamrock Park
note: Owned/operated by the Village of Berrien Springs (local government, mislabeled commercial in RV Life) - year-round camping/fishing park on the bank of the St. Joseph River. 120+ sites, most full-hookup 30/50-amp plus electric/water-only and no-hookup; DNR boat ramp, fish-cleaning station, dump. Reservable on Campspot. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). NOTE: ownership is local, not private - flag for reviewer. --Claude

name: Wolverine Campground
note: Genesee County (MI) park = LOCAL ownership (candidate was tagged private). ~195-site county campground on the north shore of Holloway Reservoir (1,973-acre all-sports lake); full-hookup (20/30/50A) and electric-only sites with concrete or gravel pads, modern restrooms/showers, dump station, boat launch and swimming beach. Transient nightly camping, reservable online; 2026 season May 1-Oct 18. RV Life 4.5*/$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Water Tower RV Park
note: Operated by the City of Lapeer Parks & Recreation = LOCAL ownership (candidate was tagged private). ~24 full-hookup (water/sewer/electric) pull-through sites, no tent camping; restrooms/showers, dump station, walking path around a small pond with fishing dock. Transient nightly ($35/night); accepts rigs up to 50 ft. Seasonal May 1-Oct 31; advance registration encouraged. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: First Lake Park
note: Municipal campground operated by Belvidere Township at 9888 Park Rd, Six Lakes = LOCAL ownership (candidate was tagged private); offers town lake access on First Lake. Nightly RV $35, tent $25, monthly $750. Book by calling Park Manager. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: Scottville Riverside Park
note: City of Scottville municipal park on the Pere Marquette River. 54 modern RV sites (water/electric/cable) plus 5 primitive tent sites; pull-thru sites, dump station, two bathhouses (one ADA + laundry). Open May-Oct; reservable online (Campspot) or by phone. Primarily nightly transient. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: North Park Campground
note: City of Harbor Beach municipal park (city-owned since 1903), open May-Oct. 184 sites: full-hookup modern, rustic wooded, plus tent/group; takes RVs to 50 ft, dump station, showers, WiFi. Reserve by phone. Sites reported tightly spaced. Across N. Huron Ave (M-25) from Lake Huron. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude

name: West Branch RV Park (Ogemaw County Park)
note: GOVERNMENT: owned by Ogemaw County (originally West Branch Township, 99-yr lease to the county); 53 large wooded sites with electric and water hookups, 1.5 mi N of I-75 exit 212. Open seasonally, transient. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Hopkins Park Campground
note: GOVERNMENT: municipal campground operated by the Village of Bear Lake, directly on Bear Lake; 30-31 sites with electric hookups, flush toilets, boat launch, fishing pier, swim beach. Lakefront and back-lot sites. Reservable via Campspot. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Lake Billings RV Park & Campground
note: GOVERNMENT: seasonal public park owned by the City of Manton, on Lake Billings within walking distance of downtown Manton; 37 full-hookup and 48 electric/water sites, swimming, fishing. Open Apr 15-Oct 15, transient. No dedicated official domain found; michigan.org listing used. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Old Orchard Park Campground
note: GOVERNMENT: large municipal campground operated by the Township of Oscoda (Consumers Energy FERC dam project land), nearly 4 mi along the Au Sable River overlooking Foote Pond; 500+ sites in modern/waterfront/primitive categories, electric/water hookups, accepts large RVs. Reservable online via township portal. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Craven Park
note: Municipal campground owned/operated by the Village of Bellaire on Craven Pond; 35 sites (full-hookup, W/E, and rustic), mix of reservable and FCFS (sites 22-25, 31-35 first-come). Open Apr 1-Oct 31. Reservable online via Firefly. RV Life 4*/2$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: East Jordan Tourist Park
note: Municipal campground owned/operated by the City of East Jordan on the south arm of Lake Charlevoix at 218 N Lake St; 90 sites (75 full-hookup 30/50-amp, 12 tent) plus 3 cabins and 18 boat slips. Public swimming beach; reservable online via CampLife. RV Life 4*/2$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Camp Petosega
note: Emmet County park on Pickerel Lake (300 forested acres): 90 sites with electric+water, five full-hookup pull-throughs, four rustic sites (cabins also rented; RVs to ~40 ft). Swimming beach, kayak launch, trails. Transient, reservable online via Premier Campground. RV Life 5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Upper Peninsula State Fair Campground
note: Delta County fairgrounds campground in Escanaba (UP State Fair transferred from the State of Michigan to Delta County in 2009). ~470 sites: 120 full-hookup, 350 water/electric, plus primitive. Open to the general public by advance reservation outside fair week ($40/night, $800/month); fair week (mid-Aug) is $300/week reservation-only with no daily camping. Reservable by mailed/emailed application. RV Life 4.5*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Gladstone Bay Campground
note: City of Gladstone municipal campground, 65 sites on Little Bay de Noc. Full-hookup (50-amp), electric-only, and primitive sites; dump station, WiFi, 1,500-ft sandy beach. Mix of transient and seasonal campers; RVs to 60 ft. Reservable online via RoverPass. RV Life 4*/$$ (auto 6/2026). --Claude

name: Kinross RV Park East
note: Kinross Charter Township (local government) RV park ~2.5 mi E of I-75 on M-80/Tone Rd; 64 back-in sites, full hookups (electric/water/sewer), 20/30-amp, bathhouse, dump, clubhouse, paved interior roads. Sites fit large rigs (~50 ft), so a 23-ft RV fits. Transient daily/weekly + monthly; reservable online via Campspot. Seasonal operation (host mid-May to mid-Sept). RV Life 4star/$$ (auto 6/2026) --Claude
